## PAGE 00571: Filtering by Legend items

- page_id: `136710601`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710601/Filtering+by+Legend+items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:47.012+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1947346740

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1947346743

2010910600

**On this page**

**43**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1947346742

Diagrams showing multiple aspects of the system are often difficult to understand and analyze since they are quite crowded. Filtering diagrams by Legend items enables users to reduce diagrams to the chosen views only, i.e., hide unnecessary layers or show only a few necessary ones in order to make them more comprehensible. The feature is especially useful when there is a need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Mode' space='MD2024x'] to stakeholders only one (e.g., electrical) or a few aspects displayed in the diagram.

700500

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Filtering a diagram by the selected Legend item *iElectricity*. 
Filtering preference is set to *Show*(on the left) and *Hide***(on the right).

##### Filtering diagram by Legend items

Once you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024x']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='MD2024x'] a Legend and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='MD2024x'] for it, you can filter your diagram by Legend items to highlight the chosen layers.

You can filter the diagram directly from:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024x'] (mainly used when the Legend shape is not displayed).

##### Selecting filtering preferenceSelecting filtering preference

Before using the filter, you need to select the filtering preference. There are two options you can choose from: *Show* or *Hide*.

Selecting the *Show* preference allows you to display the element symbols filtered by a Legend item while hiding all the other element symbols. Selecting the Hide preference allows you to hide the element symbols filtered by a Legend item while all the other element symbols of the diagram are displayed. Keep in mind that only one preference can be selected - the *Show* and *Hide* preferences cannot be combined.

###### Selecting the filtering preference for a specific diagram

To select the filtering preference for a specific diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Filter by Legend Items .
3. In the open dialog, select either *Show* or *Hide*to set the preference to filter your diagram by. The default filtering preference is *Show*. You can change it via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024x'].If there are multiple Legends on the diagram, the same preference applies to all of them.If the applied filter is saved, the filtering preference remains as is once the diagram is closed and reopened. Otherwise, the preference resets to default.
4. Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Filtering comments using different filtering preferences:

- Hide : all comments are hidden;
- Show : all comments are shown including anchored elements.

###### default filtering preferenceSelecting the default filtering preference

The default filtering preference is *Show*, however, you can change it via the Project Options. The change applies to all existing and future diagrams of the project, except fordiagrams with an already applied filter.

To select the default filtering preference

1. In the main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General options group, select Legends .
3. Set the **Filtering Preference** option to *Show* or *Hide*. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Filtering from legend symbolFiltering when Legend shape is displayed

If the Legend shape is displayed on the diagram pane, you can filter your diagram by Legend items directly from it. To be more specific, you can either filter your diagram from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024x'] or the selected [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024x']. The latter method comes in handy when there is a need to filter the diagram by multiple Legend items at once.

###### **Filtering from smart manipulator**Filtering by Legend items from the smart manipulator toolbar

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend item

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the Legend shape on the diagram pane, select a Legend item to filter your diagram by.
3. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The diagram is filtered by the selected Legend item, meaning that the diagram is limited to one specific aspect. If the filtering preference is *Show*, the diagram displays only the element symbols filtered by the Legend item, while the rest of the element symbols are hidden. If the filtering preference is *Hide*, the diagram hides the element symbols filtered by the Legend item while the rest of the element symbols are displayed. TipTo filter the diagram by multiple Legend items, repeat the procedure.

To filter a diagram by all Legend items at once

1. Select a Legend shape on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='filtering_by_all_legend_items.png']
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_button_smart_manipulator.png'] . The diagram is filtered by all items specified in the Legend, meaning that all diagram aspects are either hidden or displayed.

###### **Filtering from legend item shortcut menu**Filtering by Legend items from the shortcut menu

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend items from the shortcut menu

1. In the Legend shape on a diagram, select the Legend item(s) to filter your diagram by.
2. Right-click and then in the shortcut menu, select Filtered . [ATTACHMENT filename='filtering_by_multiple_legend_items.png']

##### Filtering from diagram toolbarFiltering when Legend shape is not displayed

Alternatively, you can filter your diagram by the selected Legend items directly from the diagram toolbar. This is the primary filtering method when the Legend shape is not displayed on the diagram. In this case, you can select whether to filter the diagram by a single or multiple Legend items as well.

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend item(s)

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Filter by Legend Items .
3. In the open dialog, specify the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024x'].
4. Select the Legend items to filter your diagram by.
5. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Managing and removing the applied filter

Once diagram layers are filtered, the following notification appears above the diagram:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Click **Clear**to clear the applied filter.
- Click **Save** to keep the filter applied after the diagram is reloaded.

Alternatively, to clear the applied filter, you can:

- click[IMAGE alt='' src='']in the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.
- uncheck the box next to **Filtered** in the Legend item shortcut menu.
- clear the corresponding boxes in the **Filter by Legend Items**([IMAGE alt='' src=''] > **Filter by Legend Items**) dialog.

The diagram filtered by Legend items is read-only, meaning that it cannot be edited unless the filter is removed.

1010241802

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the *Layers* sample model that comes with the SysML plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='layers.mdzip'] .
- Open the model from the <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML directory .

1947346737

## PAGE 00572: Filtering table data

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Using wild card

936188854

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Using wild card

936188865

936188853

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Using wild card

936188864

By filtering data in the table you can quickly find the elements you need. This is especially handy when you are working with a large table containing many rows and columns. Elements can be filtered by a phrase specified in the Filter box of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024x']. The following figure shows the Generic table before and after filtering.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Filtered rows and columns in the Generic table.

##### Filtering a table

To filter a table

1. In the Table toolbar's Filter box, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_settings.png'] icon and specify the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2024x'] .
2. In the input field, write the keyword to search the table and either press **Enter**or click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to see the results. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Disabled filter during table loadingFiltering is disabled if the table is still loading, as indicated by the warning icon [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Once the table is loaded and the warning disappears, either press **Enter** or click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to filter the table. You can also track the table loading status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='MD2024x'], [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon stands for **Loading** and [IMAGE alt='' src=''] for **Loading completed**.
3. Once the table is filtered, the filter icon is marked with a green check mark [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_green_chek_mark.png'] .

##### Specifying filter settingsSpecifying filter settings

The search for the phrase can be performed either in the whole table or in selected columns. You can specify filter settings by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the Filter box. The list with the following main areas opens:

- Specify the columns to search (1). You can search the entire table by selecting the **All** option or selecting one or several particular columns.
- Specify case sensitivity (2).
- Specify wild card or regular expression (3). Using wild cardIn the **Filter**box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:“*” substitutes any range of characters. If your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names will be found. For example, “Address” and “ThreadDeath”.“?” substitutes only one character. If your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “Address”. “ThreadDeath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.Be sure the **Match Anywhere**command is unchecked when using wildcard characters!
- Specify how the results should match your keywords (4).
- Specify if the children/parent elements should be displayed (5). 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The main areas in the filter settings.

You can save the filter criteria for a particular table. The filter criteria will be saved even after reloading the project or restarting the modeling tool. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving the filter criteria' space='MD2024x'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can also track the table loading status in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Table info area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="status_loading_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> icon stands for <strong>Loading</strong> and <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="status_loading_complete_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> for <strong>Loading completed</strong>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Once the table is filtered, the filter icon is marked with a green check mark <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_green_chek_mark.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ded46565-d860-442d-b141-11d6d38c90f6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Specifying filter settings</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Specifying filter settings</h3><p>The search for the phrase can be performed either in the whole table or in selected columns. You can specify filter settings by clicking <ac:image ac:title="Filter settings" ac:alt="Filter settings"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_settings.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Filter box. The list with the following main areas opens:</p><ul><li><p>Specify the columns to search (1).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40996788-1d4a-46ee-bb72-057e434a5588"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can search the entire table by selecting the <strong>All</strong> option or selecting one or several particular columns.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Specify case sensitivity (2).</p></li><li><p>Specify wild card or regular expression (3).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea48d02-4c40-45e4-aaeb-a54461c04c84"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Using wild card</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Filter </strong>box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:</p><ul><li>“*” substitutes any range of characters. If your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names will be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress” and “Threa<u>dD</u>eath”.</li><li>“?” substitutes only one character. If your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress”. “Threa<u>dD</u>eath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.</li></ul><p>Be sure the <strong>Match Anywhere </strong>command is unchecked when using wildcard characters!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Specify how the results should match your keywords (4).</p></li><li><p>Specify if the children/parent elements should be displayed (5).<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quickFind_in_generic_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 20.0px;">The main areas in the filter settings.</h6></li></ul><p>You can save the filter criteria for a particular table. ````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711320 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00573: Final state

- page_id: `136711320`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711320/Final+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:32.186+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The final state symbol (a circle surrounding a smaller solid circle) is used to represent the object destruction. The final state is optional in the diagram because there is a system that runs without interruption after the start of the activities. Also, there can be several final states in the same state diagram, denoting that the life of the object can finish depending on several conditions.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

The final state is optional in the diagram because there is a system that runs without interruption after the start of the activities. Also, there can be several final states in the same state diagram, denoting that the life of the object can finish depending on several conditions.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c2481e8e-3300-47ca-a89e-0b94602a4af1"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709556 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00574: Find and Replace dialog

- page_id: `136709556`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709556/Find+and+Replace+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:56.074+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The Find and Replace functionality allows replacing one specified model value with another. You can change the values for the following properties:

- Names
- Documentation
- Tag values
- Text included to Notes
- Text included to Text Boxes
- Expressions

To open the **Find and Replace** dialog, do one of the following

- On the main menu, click Edit > Find and Replace .
- Press Ctrl + R.
- On the main toolbar, click the arrow by the Find button [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button_2.png'] , then select Find and Replace .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following table provides descriptions of the **Find and Replace** dialog components.

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Find What | Text box with drop- down list | Type or select from the list to search the value of the element to be replaced. |
| Replace With | Text box with drop- down list | Type or select from the list the value of element that will replace the found value. |
| Options area |  |  |
| Type | Text box with | Click the button and select one or more element types from the list provided in the open Select Element Type dialog. You have the option to include standard subtypes and custom types of the selected element type in the search results. Alternatively, you can leave the default value <any>.Select the Include Subtypes check box to include the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages.Select the Include Custom Types check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package. |
| Scope | Text box with | Click the button and select a package wherein the content search will be performed. |
| Load diagrams and autoloadable used projects in read-write mode | Check box | Select to load all elements to be included in the search if the model has diagrams or used projects that are not loaded. Used project will not be loaded and elements from it will not be included into the search, if the load mode of that used project is Manual load. |
| Include elements from standard/system profiles | Check box | Select to include elements from standard/system profiles. |
| Include elements from additional content | Check box | Select to include additional elements from Smart packages if Smart packages fall into the search scope. |

You may see an error when trying to replace a value with an invalid one.

- For example, if the value type is boolean , you may replace values from true to false or from false to true , but if you will try to replace the value true or false to other, for example, to Motor, an error message will be displayed.
- You will not be allowed to change the Integer value to String when it is a part of the value. For example, if you have the 120 value, trying to replace 20 with AB will cause an error.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709520 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00575: Find dialog

- page_id: `136709520`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709520/Find+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:12:05.939+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760352

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760355

599760349

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760354

#### EXCERPT: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

INLINE

The **Find** dialog provides a quick way to search for specific model elements. Use it to search for elements by name, applied stereotype name, tag value and documentation content. The dialog can be opened from several different places in your modeling tool. To complete a simple search, use the main area of the dialog, or click the **Options** button, to specify criteria that will reduce your search results to only applicable ones.

To search for elements using the Find dialog

1. Open the Find dialog in one of the following ways:

1. 
  - In the main menu, click Edit > Find .
  - In the main toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='find.png'] .
  - Press Ctrl+Shift+F.
2. Specify the search criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MD2024x'] .
3. If you would like your search to be more specific, click the Options button to expand the **Options** area[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MD2024x'] , and select the desired options.
4. When you finish specifying your search criteria, click the Find button.

After completing the steps above, the search results are displayed in the **Search Results** tab of the model browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Find** dialog with the extended **Options** area.

#### TIP: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

Searching tips

- To search for textual information in an active diagram, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the diagram toolbar or press Ctrl+F .
- To quickly access your search results, add them to Favorites or save them as the contents of a Smart Package.

#### EXPAND: Click to learn how to add search results to Favorites

Click to learn how to add search results to Favorites

1. In the Search Results tab of the Model browser, select the element(s) you want to add to Favorites. Press and hold the Shift button to select multiple items.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites.png'] on the toolbar of the Search Results tab. Then select Add Selected to Favorites from the menu.

The selected search results are now added to Favorites. They can be accessed by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and selecting the desired element under the **Go to Favorites** group.

#### EXPAND: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_search_results_button.png'] on the toolbar of the Search Results tab containing the search results you want to add to a Smart Package.
2. From the open menu, select one of the following:
  - Save Query as Smart Package if you want the content of a newly created Smart Package to be dynamic. The content of the Smart Package will then be updated according to your search. The specified search criteria will be saved as the Query property value.
  - Save Results as Smart Package if you want the content of a newly created Smart Package to be static. In this case, your model is not queried, and new elements meeting the specified search criteria are not automatically included in the Smart Package.
3. In the open dialog, select the Package that will own the created Smart Package.

A new Smart Package containing all the search results from the **Search Results** tab is created under the selected owner.

main

##### Main area of the Find dialog

Use the main area of the **Find**dialog to specify the most common search criteria, such as an element name or the scope of the search. The table below provides detailed descriptions of all the elements in the main area.

Find dialog options

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| What | Text box with drop- down list | The What box allows you to search for model elements by the text used in their names or textual properties. To search for desired model elements, enter the text you want to find. To search in element names, select the Search in names check box. To search in textual properties including Documentation, select the Search in all texts check box. Note that both of these options are available only if the Options area is collapsed. To make your search more specific, click as illustrated below.You may use wildcard characters (,?) in your search phrase. "" means zero, one, or more characters. If your search phrase is "dd", all the elements containing "dd" in their names can be found, e.g. "Address" and "ThreadDeath". "?" means exactly one character. If your search phrase is "?dd", only the elements with "dd" in their names, beginning with the second character (e.g., "Address"), can be found. "ThreadDeath" cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase "dd". Be sure to check the Match exactly command when using wildcard characters! |
| Type | Text box with | Click and select one or more element types from the list provided in the open Select Element Type dialog. You have the option to include standard subtypes and custom types of the selected element type in the search results.Select the Include Subtypes check box to include the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages.Select the Include Custom Types check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package. |
| Scope | Text box with | The scope refers to the package of your model in which the content search will be performed. Click and select the desired package. Specify the search scope before opening the Find dialog by performing one of the following:Right-clicking a package in the Model Browser, and selecting Find from its shortcut menu.Selecting a package in the Model Browser and pressing Ctrl + F. |
| Properties | Text box with | The box is available only when the Options area is expanded. Click , select the properties applicable to your search and specify their values. The property list in the open dialog contains only the properties of the element types selected in the Type box. Select at least one element type to make the button available. Keep in mind that the property list contains primitive value properties, e.g. string, boolean, integer, and a non-primitive value property named Applied Stereotype. |

options

##### Options area of the Find dialog

The **Options** area allows you to select from a number of additional search options that will make your search more specific and narrow down the search results.

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Clear Previous Results | Check box | Select to remove all previous search results from the Search Results tab in the Model Browser. |
| Load diagrams and autoloadable used projects | Check box | Select to load all elements to be included in the search if the model has diagrams or used projects that are not loaded. If the load mode of the used project is Manual load, the used project will not be loaded and elements from it will not be included in the search. |
| Include elements from standard/system profiles | Check box | Select to include elements from standard/system profiles. |
| Include elements from additional content | Check box | Select to include additional elements from Smart packages if Smart packages fall into the search scope. By default, this check box is not selected and elements from Smart packages are excluded from search results. This enables you to avoid search results being duplicated by results outside of Smart packages. |
| Search data unused in diagrams | Check box | Select to search for only those elements that do not have symbols in any diagram. Note that the check box is unavailable if the Find dialog is opened by using the Find in Diagram command in the diagram shortcut menu. |
| Show results in new window | Check box | Select to display the results of every subsequent search in a new Search Results tab of the Model Browser. |

##### Advanced search options

The **Find** dialog offers a number of advanced search options allowing you to narrow down search results. Below are some specific cases illustrating how search criteria can be defined.

To find an element by an element ID

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. In the What box, enter the prefix " xmi.id =" followed by the ID of the element you want to find.
3. Click the Find button. [ATTACHMENT filename='find_dialog_fragment1.png']

To find abstract Classes

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Type box, and select Class .
3. Expand the Options area if it is not yet expanded.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Properties box, select the Is Abstract property, and set its value to true .
5. Click the Find button.

To find the elements with a specific stereotype and a certain phrase in their documentation

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Type box, select the element type you want to find (e.g, Actor ) and select the Include Subtypes check box.
3. Expand the Options area if it is not yet expanded .
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Properties box, select the Applied Stereotype property, and select a custom stereotype (e.g., «BusinessActor») as its value.
5. In the same dialog, select the Documentation property and enter a search phrase as its value.
6. Click the Find button.

Use it to search for elements by name, applied stereotype name, tag value and documentation content. The dialog can be opened from several different places in your modeling tool. To complete a simple search, use the main area of the dialog, or click the <strong>Options</strong> button, to specify criteria that will reduce your search results to only applicable ones.</p><p><br /></p><p>To search for elements using the Find dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog in one of the following ways:</li></ol><ol><li style="list-style-type: you finish specifying your search criteria, click the <strong>Find</strong> button. </li></ol><p><br />After completing the steps above, the search results are displayed in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab of the model browser.<br />  </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" Ctrl+F</span><span>.</span></li><li>To quickly access your search results, add them to Favorites or save them as the contents of a Smart Package.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d60fc82-2ba4-44fe-a54c-ffd234066118"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click to learn how to add search results to Favorites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>In the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab of the Model browser, select the element(s) you want to add to Favorites. Press and hold the Shift button to select multiple items.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorites.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab. Then select <strong>Add Selected to Favorites</strong> from the menu.</li></ol><p>The selected search results are now added to Favorites. They can be accessed by clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorites.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and selecting the desired element under the <strong>Go to Favorites</strong> group.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="103d4986-4b2c-4e6f-9357-832eb0dff1ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_search_results_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab containing the search results you want to add to a Smart Package.</li><li>From the open menu, select one of the following:<br /><ul><li><strong>Save Query as Smart Package</strong> if you want the content of a newly created Smart Package to be dynamic. The content of the Smart Package will then be updated according to your search. The specified search criteria will be saved as the <strong>Query</strong> property value.</li><li><strong>Save Results as Smart Package </strong>if you want the content of a newly created Smart Package to be static. In this case, your model is not queried, and new elements meeting the specified search criteria are not automatically included in the Smart Package.</li></ul></li><li>In the open dialog, select the Package that will own the created Smart Package.</li></ol><p>A new Smart Package containing all the search results from the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab is created under the selected owner.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a60565a7-a1ff-4dc8-9cad-39d230b9090b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">main</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Main area of the Find dialog</h3><p>Use the main area of the <strong>Find </strong>dialog to specify the most common search criteria, such as an element name or the scope of the search. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709554 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00576: Find TODO

- page_id: `136709554`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709554/Find+TODO
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:55.836+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Command **Find TODO** performs a quick search for all *To Do* values.

You can specify *To Do* values in the element Specification window, the **To** **Do** property cell. You can use the **To Do** property cell for adding the notes about the work you need to do. Then you can quickly find the elements that have the **To Do** properties defined.

Results of this kind of search are displayed in the **Search Results** tab on the Model Browser.

To start the **Find TODO** search, do one of the following

- On the main menu, click Edit > Find TODO .
- On the main toolbar, click the arrow nearby the [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button_2.png'] button and then select Find TODO .

The **Search Results** tab opens with results represented in it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f14189ed-2da7-44ac-8704-8a1bb678b3f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Command <strong>Find TODO</strong> performs a quick search for all <em>To Do</em> values.</p><p>You can specify <em>To Do</em> values in the element Specification window, the <strong>To</strong> <strong>Do</strong> property cell. You can use the <strong>To Do</strong> property cell for adding the notes about the work you need to do. Then you can quickly find the elements that have the <strong>To Do</strong> properties defined.</p><p>Results of this kind of search are displayed in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab on the Model Browser.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To start the <strong>Find TODO</strong> search, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Find TODO</strong>.</li><li>On the main toolbar, click the arrow nearby the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_button_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Find TODO" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and then select <strong>Find TODO</strong>.</li></ul><p>The <strong>Search Results</strong> tab opens with results represented in it.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715159 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00577: Finding elements by name or by meta-type

- page_id: `136715159`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715159/Finding+elements+by+name+or+by+meta-type
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:26:02.454+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder* for finding elements by the name or type (meta-type) in a project. This utility class provides several internal finders for executing different types of search operations.

For example:

```java
models = Finder.byNameAllRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");]]>
```

```text

```

**Finding or creating Classifiers**

A special utility class is available for search or creating Classifiers by metatype, simple or qualified name - *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassifierFinder*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Retrieving meta information about element' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder</em> for finding elements by the name or type (meta-type) in a project. This utility class provides several internal finders for executing different types of search operations.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fc8c8b9-aa83-401a-a514-2f31e191100e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   Project project = ...;
   //find a Model element under the project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is not a recursive search
   Model model = Finder.byName().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");
   //find a first Model element under project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is a recursive search.
   Model model = Finder.byNameRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");
   //find all Model elements under project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is a recursive search.
   Collection<Model> models = Finder.byNameAllRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre><br /></pre><p><strong>Finding or creating Classifiers</strong></p><p>A special utility class is available for search or creating Classifiers by metatype, simple or qualified name - <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassifierFinder</em></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f3df531-9a85-4f43-a56f-cbcb8d51f363"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Retrieving meta information about element" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710774 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00578: Finding predefined diagram aspects

- page_id: `136710774`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710774/Finding+predefined+diagram+aspects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:00.607+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581335849

581335852

581335851

There are predefined aspects that are only included in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x'] or other customizations. The predefined diagram aspects are the following: <<optical>>, <<mechanical>>, <<electrical>>, <<software>>, <<conceptual>>, and <<logical>>. The procedure below explains how to find them in your project. You can use predefined aspects in your project, or you can create custom aspects. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom aspects' space='MD2024x']>]]>

To find predefined aspects in the project when SysML Plugin is installed

1. In the Containment tree, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the **Show Auxiliary Resources** option.
2. Expand packages *MD Customization for SysML* > *additional_stereotypes > aspects*.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The location of the predefined diagram aspects in the project when SysML plugin is installed. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

581335848

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample models that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x']. To open this sample properly you need to install the SysML plugin in the MagicDraw and:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711978 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00579: Finishing and canceling merge

- page_id: `136711978`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711978/Finishing+and+canceling+merge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:03.218+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge
- labels: ['finish-merging']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Availability

1077613678

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Availability

1077613680

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Availability

1077613679

To finish the merge procedure

- On the Merge window, click the Finish Merging button and a ll accepted changes will be copied to the target.

After the merge results are copied to the project, do not forget to either save or commit the project to the server.

To cancel the merge procedure

- On the Merge window, click the Cancel button. The question dialog box opens asking you if you want to cancel the merge procedure.
  - Click Yes if you are sure that you want to cancel merge procedure.
  - Click No and you will be returned back to the Merge window.

Also you can cancel merge procedure by clicking **Close** button on the **Merge** window.

**#diagramannotationson Turning on merged diagram annotations**

Before finishing the merge procedure, you can show merged diagram annotations. For this, click the **Annotate Merged Diagram** button on the toolbar located at the top of the **Merge** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Annotate Merged Diagram button in the Merge Results window toolbar.

#### NOTE: Availability

Availability

This button is available if any positions of symbols in diagrams have been changed in both contributors.

The purpose of the annotation is to mark symbols that have different positions on the same diagrams in ancestor and source projects on a merged diagram. If the Annotate Merged Diagram button is enabled, those symbols are annotated in yellow, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Annotated symbols in a merged diagram.

Click the selected symbol to open a smart manipulator and select a resolution for symbol merging.

1077613677

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711032 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00580: Flow Final Node

- page_id: `136711032`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711032/Flow+Final+Node
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:15.637+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Flow Final Node terminates a flow and destroys all tokens that arrive at it. It has no effect on other flows in the activity.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of flow final node element notation

#### PANEL: Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024x']

## PAGE 00581: Flows on decision

- page_id: `136712802`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712802/Flows+on+decision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:54.454+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

DecisionOut

**Description**

This validation rule checks if a Decision Node has more than one Outgoing Edge.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

DecisionNode

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure a Decision Node has more than one Outgoing Edge.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Decision Node has only one Outgoing Edge. The error is resolved by creating a second Outgoing Edge.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711328 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00582: Fork and Join

- page_id: `136711328`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711328/Fork+and+Join
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:33.267+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: ['fork-node', 'join-node']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

727781504

727781506

727781505

The Fork Vertices are used to split an incoming transition into two or more transitions terminating on the orthogonal target vertices (i.e., vertices in different regions of the composite state). The segments going out of a Fork Vertex must not have guards or triggers.

The Join Vertices are used to merge several transitions emanating from the source vertices in different orthogonal regions. The transitions entering a Join Vertex cannot have guards or triggers.

1. A Join Vertex must have at least two incoming Transitions and exactly one outgoing Transition.
2. A Fork Vertex must have at least two outgoing Transitions and exactly one incoming Transition.

727781503

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c902cf81-5f32-4707-a698-210d7af051d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781504</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7e215b3-7d48-47ad-b630-d0ac85e828fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="014d4885-020e-4033-aa17-cda423d28c88"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781505</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Fork Vertices are used to split an incoming transition into two or more transitions terminating on the orthogonal target vertices (i.e., vertices in different regions of the composite state). The segments going out of a Fork Vertex must not have guards or triggers.</p><p>The Join Vertices are used to merge several transitions emanating from the source vertices in different orthogonal regions. The transitions entering a Join Vertex cannot have guards or triggers.</p><ol><li>A Join Vertex must have at least two incoming Transitions and exactly one outgoing Transition.</li><li>A Fork Vertex must have at least two outgoing Transitions and exactly one incoming Transition.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4dbdf741-78b3-4c5f-9d81-69a5c98c3fd7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781503</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction Overview diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711214 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00583: Fork and Join Nodes

- page_id: `136711214`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711214/Fork+and+Join+Nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:27.740+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Fork Node splits a flow into multiple concurrent flows, while a Join Node synchronizes them.

1. A Fork Node can have exactly one incoming Activity Edge (or Transition), though it may have multiple outgoing Activity Edges (or Transitions).
2. A Join Node can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge (or Transition) but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges (or Transitions).

The Fork and Join are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the**Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge****notations** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x'] to draw different notations for the**Fork**and**Join**. This option is set to *true*by default.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example shows the different Fork and Join notations

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Fork Node splits a flow into multiple concurrent flows, while a Join Node synchronizes them.</p><ol><li>A Fork Node can have exactly one incoming Activity Edge (or Transition), though it may have multiple outgoing Activity Edges (or Transitions).</li><li>A Join Node can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge (or Transition) but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges (or Transitions).</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Fork and Join are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the </span><strong>Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong> notations</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to draw different notations for the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Fork </strong></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and </span><strong>Join</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. This option is set to <em>true </em>by default.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fork-join.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Fork and Join Nodes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example shows the different Fork and Join notations</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711641 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00584: Formatting symbols

- page_id: `136711641`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711641/Formatting+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:48.290+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2038795777

2038795781

2038795780

| Definition | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Symbol | The term "symbol" means a visual representation of some model elements in the diagram. Symbols are subdivided into shapes and paths (lines in the model, for displaying various relationships). |
| Symbol properties | Every symbol may have its own style: color, font, size, and so forth. Symbol properties may be defined for the concrete symbol, for all symbol of one element, or according to the diagram type. |

You may define the symbol properties in the following ways:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog .
- In the Symbol tab on the Properties panel at the bottom of the Model Browser .
- In the Project Options dialog. Using this dialog you can change all available symbol properties, create your own style for the project, apply different symbol properties for different diagrams, define stereotype properties that may be bound to the symbol, and more.
- On the diagram toolbar. Using this toolbar you can change the color, font, path style of a symbol, and paste the symbol properties.

2038795776

**Related pages**

true****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713986 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00585: Formatting template code

- page_id: `136713986`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713986/Formatting+template+code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:57.709+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard template language
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports the **Tab** key to help you format template code at ease. The **Tab** key is useful to indent velocity code. You can press the **Tab** key after the symbol "#" or "$" followed by the velocity code. The **Tab** key can help you format the template code, but it does not affect the output of a generated report. See figure below for example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The velocity code in the figure above uses the **Tab** key to indent code. However, the generated report does not include those indents. The**Tab** key support is available in *RTF*, *DOCX*, and *ODT* format files.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard supports the <strong>Tab</strong> key to help you format template code at ease. The <strong>Tab</strong> key is useful to indent velocity code. You can press the <strong>Tab</strong> key after the symbol &quot;#&quot; or &quot;$&quot; followed by the velocity code. The <strong>Tab</strong> key can help you format the template code, but it does not affect the output of a generated report. See figure below for example.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Indented Template Code " ac:alt="Indented Template Code "><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 59.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Formatting template code" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The velocity code in the figure above uses the <strong>Tab</strong> key to indent code. However, the generated report does not include those indents. The<strong> Tab</strong> key support is available in <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX</em>, and <em>ODT</em> format files.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712238 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00587: Forward traceability - realization

- page_id: `136712238`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712238/Forward+traceability+-+realization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:20.738+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Traceability > Predefined traceability rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The forward traceability ensures that all specified artifacts are covered by elements from the lower abstraction level.

| Property Name | Description | Applied for | Reference through... | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Manifested By Artifacts | The property shows artifacts that physically render the current component. | Component | Relationships: Manifestation | Artifact | Example |
| Realizing Component | The property shows the components representing the class realization in the Implementation model. | Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Component | Example |
| Realizing Classifier | The property shows classifiers that realize components through component realization. | Component | Relationships: Component Realization, Realization | Classifier | Example |
| Realizing Class | The property shows the classes representing the use case realization in the Design model. | Use Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Class | Example |
| Realizing Use Case | The property shows the realizing use cases of the current use case in the lower level of abstraction thus demonstrating how the use case is implemented. For example, the Requirements Use Case realizes the Business Use Case. | Use Case | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Realizing Classifier | The property shows the classifiers conforming to the contract specified by the interface and related with this interface through the interface realization relationship. | Interface | Relationships: Interface Realization | Classifier | Example |
| Realizing Element, All Realizing Elements.Available in Enterprise and Architect editions only. | The Realizing Element property gathers the source elements of the abstraction relationship.The All Realizing Elements property gathers recursively (performs a realizing element search for the result) source elements of the abstraction relationship. | Element | Relationships: Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization. | Element | Example |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711035 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00588: Fragment

- page_id: `136711035`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711035/Fragment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:16.127+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A fragment is an abstract notion of the most general interaction unit. It is a piece of an interaction. Each interaction fragment is conceptually similar to an interaction by itself.

Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an alternative flow' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Each interaction fragment is conceptually similar to an interaction by itself.</span></p><p>Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion. </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59bc2015-43d9-41f0-a825-33aa65d60798"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related References</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4fbb2ebd-c062-4358-a8cd-b2c2997293bd" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="335ed78c-5d96-4333-9fc2-5753c5c4e64f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating an alternative flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Alternatives]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05738bc0-b61f-4c4a-9f8e-cb36a2356cec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628658395</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2635d820-faad-44db-be15-ed07f3e60e5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628658398</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87f6ecfa-2140-4677-9764-80085f3f4882"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628658397</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Free Form diagram allows you to draw various geometrical shapes. The diagram also includes shapes for drawing business flowcharts.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Free_form_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Free Form diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of the Free Form diagram Business Startup Procedure</h6><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c86a29f-ef62-413c-9d06-339a2ba462df"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628658394</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages:</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Extension diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711040 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00590: Function Behavior

- page_id: `136711040`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711040/Function+Behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:17.296+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The function behavior allows modeling external functions that take only inputs and produce outputs. It has no effect on the specified system.

To create a function behavior

1. In the **Model Browser**, right click any root package in your model, in the figure below the root package is *Car*.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH185'], select **Create Element > Function Behavior [IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a package importing a function.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Functions' space='CST2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711528 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00591: Functions of Specification window

- page_id: `136711528`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711528/Functions+of+Specification+window
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:16:42.190+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Specification window
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1291722198

1291722200

1291722199

Using Specification window you can perform various actions that are necessary when working with model elements. Every model element has its own specification.

The following table describes only common functions of the Specification window.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add/modify modelelement propertiesand inner elements | Choose the desired property group and specify its properties in the general specification pane. |
| Open referencedelementspecifications | From the shortcut menu, select Open Specification or click . When another element specification is open, you can work with both specifications in the same window. |
| Navigate betweenopen specifications | To navigate between open specifications, use the Back and Forward buttons. |
| Manage relationships | View all relationships in which the element participates. Modify the name of the relationship.View/change the direction of the relationship.Modify the target element.Create new outgoing or incoming relationships. |
| Add/edit elementdocumentation | Add element description, describe use case scenarios, or add other relevant information that can be used for generating reports or technical documentation. Documentation also can be written in HTML using the . |
| Manage elementhyperlinks | Open, edit, add, or remove from the selected model element to a file, address, other element / symbol, or requirements. |
| Manage element tags and tagged values | Create tag definitions, default tagged values. |
| Manage elementconstraints | to an element. |
| Track elementsymbol usage in diagrams | Find out in which diagrams the symbol is used. Open these diagrams directly from the specification window. |
| Select modelelement in theContainment tree | From the shortcut menu, choose Select in Containment Tree or click . |
| View element traceability | Monitor element changes and the impact of those changes using t. |

Select a specification property and from the shortcut menu, select the actionyou want to perform.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711043 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00592: Gate

- page_id: `136711043`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711043/Gate
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:17.666+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw allows for displaying the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x']when leaving or entering a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024x'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024x']. The Gate is a connection point for representing a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] from the outside to the current [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024x'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024x'].

Gates can be used in three cases:

- For passing and returning arguments to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024x'] , which calls some Interaction.
- For displaying “exceptions“ as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] that stops an interaction execution and leaves it.
- For “calling“ sequence blocks represented as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024x'] .

Gate has no notation. Gates are created as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] ends when drawing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] to/from a diagram frame, an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024x'], or a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024x'].

To create a formal gate, do one of the following

- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] from the diagram frame.
- Draw a reply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] to the diagram frame.
- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024x'] (inside [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024x'] ).

- You can view the created gates of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] in the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']. In the **Send [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MD2024x']**list box you can see a formal gate. The **Receive**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MD2024x']**Event**lists the actual gate.
- The Gate uses text from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] as an identification name. For example, a message name or a message operation.

To draw create an actual gate of the formal gate, do one of the following

- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024x'] , which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The Select Formal Gate dialog opens.
- Draw a reply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MD2024x'] , which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The Select Formal Gate dialog opens.
- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024x'] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024x'] (outside [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024x'] ). The Select Formal Gate dialog appears.

You can also view the formal and actual gates in the gates Interaction[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'], Interaction Use dialog, Combined Fragment dialog, and the Actual Gates and Formal Gates panes.

To select a formal gate for the actual gate

1. Do one of the following:
  - Draw a message.
  - From the message shortcut menu, choose Select Formal Gate .
2. In the open dialog, select one of the listed formal gates and click OK . An actual gate is created.

The**Select Formal Gates**command is available only if there are formal gates.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']Activity diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Gates are created as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> ends when drawing <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[messages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to/from a diagram frame, an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p> </p><p>To create a formal gate, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the diagram frame.</li><li>Draw a reply <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the diagram frame.</li><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (inside <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b1ee808-c94e-4183-8228-eebc06d67590"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p>You can view the created gates of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. In the <strong>Send <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Event ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>list box you can see a formal gate. The <strong>Receive </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Event" /><ac:link-body><strong>Event</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>lists the actual gate.</p></li><li><span>The Gate uses text from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as an identification name. For example, a message name or a message operation.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To draw create an actual gate of the formal gate, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Draw a reply <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog opens. </li><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (outside <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog appears.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e783d43e-08e7-4010-aa09-1222e7aa2a27"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can also view the formal and actual gates in the gates Interaction </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link><span>, Interaction Use dialog, Combined Fragment dialog, and the Actual Gates and Formal Gates panes.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To select a formal gate for the actual gate</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Draw a message.</li><li>From the message shortcut menu, choose <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the open dialog, select one of the listed formal gates and click <strong>OK</strong>. An actual gate is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d5f9bbd-3931-4ac1-ac23-1b54c618de41"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The </span><strong>Select Formal Gates</strong><span> command is available only if there are formal gates.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2bcf321e-b978-4308-b7fa-e6212d58c3e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4e58255f-45e4-4706-9c1f-f027869c05be"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x"
