<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711047 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00593: Generalization

- page_id: `136711047`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711047/Generalization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:18.336+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A generalization is a taxonomic relationship between a more general classifier and a more specific classifier. Each instance of the specific classifier is also an indirect instance of the general classifier. Thus, the specific classifier inherits the features of the more general classifier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Generalizations can be grouped to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization tree' space='MD2024x']. 
Generalization may be assigned to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024x'].

You can format the generalization symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

A generalization can be specified by changing its property values in the generalization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']

#### INFO: Sample Model

Sample Model

You can see a generalization relationship sample in*<MagicDraw installation directory>\samples\case studies\Magic Library.mdzip**.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Thus, the specific classifier inherits the features of the more general classifier.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Generalizations can be grouped to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[generalization tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br />Generalization may be assigned to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[generalization set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You can format the generalization symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>A generalization can be specified by changing its property values in the generalization <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715549 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00595: Generalization Language Properties

- page_id: `136715549`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715549/Generalization+Language+Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:34.400+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering > Conversion from an Earlier Project Version > Language Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This is the example of generalization forchildClassclass that extends fromParentClassclass.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generalization Example in Class Diagram

There areInheritance typeandVirtual modifierin the Generalization C++ Language Properties that need to be translated and applied to the«C++Generalization» stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generalization Inheritance visibility

| Generalization - Inheritance visibility type |  |
| --- | --- |
| Old value | Translation |
| public | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set inheritance visibility tag value to public. |
| protected | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set inheritance visibility tag value to protected. |
| private | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set inheritance visibility tag value to private. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generalization Virtual inheritance modifier

| Generalization - Virtual inheritance modifier |  |
| --- | --- |
| Old value | Translation |
| nonvirtual | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set virtual inheritance tag value to false. |
| virtual | Apply the «C++Generalization» stereotype and set virtual inheritance tag value to true. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711054 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00596: Generalization set

- page_id: `136711054`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711054/Generalization+set
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:19.097+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Generalization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A generalization set is a packageable element whose instances define collections of subsets of generalization relationships. Each generalization set defines a particular set of generalization relationships that describe the way which a general classifier may be divided using specific subtypes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Example of *Movie* and *Audiobook***generalization sets

You can format the generalization symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog. For more information on how to open the generalization set's Symbol Properties dialog, scroll down to read on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024x'].

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x'].

You can specify generalization set properties in the Generalization Set's Specification window. For more information on how to open the Generalization Set's Specification window, scroll down to read on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024x']. In the same window, you can find the description of each Generalization Set property in the description area of the Specification Window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024x'].

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For a generalization set, use the name of a selected generalization set (not the relationship path) to open the **Symbol Properties** dialog or Specification window.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']

##### Creating a new generalization set

You can create a generalization set using a generalization Specification window or a command from the generalization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH2024x'].

To create a new generalization set using the Specification window

1. Select the generalization and open its Specification window.
2. In the **Generalization Set**property value cell, click the Edit button [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The Select Generalization Set dialog opens. For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024x'].
3. Select a generalization set owner and click the Create button. If this command is unavailable, turn on the Creation Mode .
4. In the opened list, click Generalization Set . The Generalization Set Specification window opens.
5. Type the generalization set name and set other properties you need. Click Close when you are done.
6. The created generalization set is displayed in the model tree or list and in the Selected elements area of the Select Generalization Set dialog.
7. Select the created generalization set and click **OK**. The generalization set is created and assigned for the current generalization. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Selected Elements

Selected Elements

In the **Generalization Set** property value cell, several generalization sets can be listed, but only the first generalization set name is displayed on the diagram pane near the generalization.

createTo create a new generalization set from the generalization shortcut menu

1. On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Generalization Set . The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.
3. If it's already there, select the generalization. Otherwise, click the Create Generalization Set button.
4. The Select Owner dialog opens.
5. In the dialog, select an existing or create a new generalization set owner and click OK when you are done.
6. The Generalization Specification window opens. Type the generalization set name and set other properties you need.
7. Click Close when you are done.

#### INFO: Several Generalizations

Several Generalizations

To create a generalization set for several generalizations at a time

1. Select one generalization, then hold down the Shift key and select the other generalizations you desire.
2. While hovering your mouse over one of the generalizations, right click to show the shortcut menu and select Generalization Set.
3. Repeat steps 2-7 from the aforementioned set of instructions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Assigning a generalization set for a generalization

#### WARNING: Assigning a Generalization Set

Assigning a Generalization Set

Before assigning a generalization set, make sure you have at least one generalization set in your model.

To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the Specification window

1. Select a generalization and open its Specification window.
2. In the Generalization Set property value cell, click the Edit button [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.
3. Select the desired generalization set and click **OK**. The selected generalization set is displayed in the **Generalization Set** property value cell. For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024x'].
4. Close the Specification window.

To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the shortcut menu

1. On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu
2. Click **Generalization Set**. In the opened Select Generalization Set dialog, select the generalization set you need. If you do not see your desired Generalized Set then you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024x'] from the dialog.

#### TIP: Another Alternative to Assigning Generalization Sets

Another Alternative to Assigning Generalization Sets

Draw or move a generalization line to a generalization set tree or to a generalization that belongs to a generalization set (see the following figure). The newly created generalization is assigned to the same generalization set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign a generalization to a particular generalization set

1. Open the Generalization Set Specification window.
2. In the Generalization property value cell, click the Edit button [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Select Generalization dialog opens.
3. From the list of generalizations, select all you need and click Apply .

You can select generalizations that belong to other sets. Selecting such a generalization removes it from the previous set and adds it to the current one.

##### Opening the Generalization Set Specification window and Symbol Properties dialog

specificationTo open its Specification window

Do one of the following:

- On the diagram pane, double click on the generalization set name revealing the Generalization Set Specification window.
- Invoking the Generalization Set Specification window from the diagram pane shortcut menu
  1. On the diagram pane, right click the generalization set name revealing its shortcut menu.
  2. Select the Specification command opening the Generalization Set Specification window.
- Opening the Generalization Set Specification window from the Generalization Specification window.
  1. Open the Generalization Specification window by double-clicking on the Generalization arrow.
  2. In the Generalization Specification window, select the Generalization Set property cell.
  3. Select the generalization set you desire.
  4. Right-click to open the generalization set shortcut menu. Select Open Specification .
  5. The Generalization Set Specification window opens.

symbolTo open its Symbol Properties dialog

- On the diagram pane, select the generalization set name and from its shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens.

Grouping generalizations to generalization set trees

#### NOTE: Make Generalization Set Tree command

Make Generalization Set Tree command

The Make Generalization Set Tree command is available if the general element has at least two generalizations and at least two generalizations has assigned the same generalization set.

To group the generalizations to the generalization set trees

1. Select a general element on the diagram pane and invoke its shortcut menu.
2. Click Refactor > Make Generalization Set Tree .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A generalization set is a packageable element whose instances define collections of subsets of generalization relationships. Each generalization set defines a particular set of generalization relationships that describe the way which a general classifier may be divided using specific subtypes.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-07-07 at 9.56.13 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;font-weight: bold;">Example of <em>Movie</em> and <em>Audiobook</em></span><em style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;font-weight: bold;"> </em><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;font-weight: bold;">generalization sets</span></p><p>You can format the generalization symbol properties in the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog. For more information on how to open the generalization set's Symbol Properties dialog, scroll down to read on <ac:link ac:anchor="symbol"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[how to open the generalization set's Symbol Properties dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ac:link>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19aa51b2-b4f6-4ae5-b87a-0d31db6145b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about symbol representation properties, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can specify generalization set properties in the Generalization Set's Specification window. For more information on how to open the Generalization Set's Specification window, scroll down to read on <ac:link ac:anchor="specification"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[how to open that Specification window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ac:link>. In the same window, you can find the description of each Generalization Set property in the description area of the Specification Window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02202a08-01b8-4664-b9d1-3104c6e4ecc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about the Specification window usage, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94d48b5e-7ede-452b-8fe6-0be7fddaf601"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a generalization set, use the name of a selected generalization set (not the relationship path) to open the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog or Specification window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2426401a-e208-46b8-a7cd-5e9c03d6736d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf4e5426-82fc-4638-a18f-1fbb0e98141b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f918bbaa-5f4d-4bc1-a2ad-71b678497fec"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;">Creating a new generalization set</span></h3><p>You can create a generalization set using a generalization Specification window or a command from the generalization <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2024x" ri:content-title="DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shortcut menu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new generalization set using the Specification window</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the generalization and open its Specification window.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Generalization Set </strong>property value cell, click the Edit button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a02f5a21-0818-49d6-bab7-ca6cd49c2f6c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Select a generalization set owner and click the <strong>Create </strong>button. If this command is unavailable, turn on the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>. </li><li>In the opened list, click <strong>Generalization Set</strong>. The Generalization Set Specification window opens.</li><li>Type the generalization set name and set other properties you need. Click <strong>Close </strong>when you are done.</li><li>The created generalization set is displayed in the model tree or list and in the <strong>Selected elements</strong> area of the <strong>Select Generalization Set </strong>dialog.</li><li><p>Select the created generalization set and click <strong>OK</strong>. The generalization set is created and assigned for the current generalization.<br /><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-07-07 at 9.48.07 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d900c13c-5221-4622-ac9d-394b46086faf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Selected Elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Generalization Set</strong> property value cell, several generalization sets can be listed, but only the first generalization set name is displayed <span>on the diagram pane near the generalization.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64946d34-8a8f-4d8f-8757-aed7878ee160"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a new generalization set from the generalization shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Generalization Set</strong>. The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.</li><li>If it's already there, select the generalization. Otherwise, click the <strong>Create Generalization Set </strong>button. </li><li>The Select Owner dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select an existing or create a new generalization set owner and click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done.</li><li>The Generalization<strong> </strong>Specification window opens. Type the generalization set name and set other properties you need.</li><li>Click <strong>Close </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b45ecaad-2f64-465d-a324-7a5dc601a748"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Several Generalizations</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a generalization set for several generalizations at a time</p><hr /><ol><li>Select one generalization, then hold down the Shift key and select the other generalizations you desire.</li><li>While hovering your mouse over one of the generalizations, right click to show the shortcut menu and select <strong>Generalization Set.</strong></li><li>Repeat steps 2-7 from the aforementioned set of instructions.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-07-07 at 10.19.39 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Assigning a generalization set for a generalization</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4df9c7e-fb47-4848-9430-3752b3b51f44"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Assigning a Generalization Set</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before assigning a generalization set, make sure you have at least one generalization set in your model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the Specification window</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a generalization and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the <strong>Generalization Set</strong> property value cell, click the Edit button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="SysMLP" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.</li><li><p>Select the desired generalization set and click <strong>OK</strong>. The selected generalization set is displayed in the <strong>Generalization Set</strong> property value cell.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe09f902-11fe-473e-ab38-56fd112d0059"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Close the Specification window.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu</li><li><p>Click <strong>Generalization Set</strong>. In the opened Select Generalization Set dialog, select the generalization set you need. If you do not see your desired Generalized Set then you can <ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[can create a new generalization set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ac:link> from the dialog. </p></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="701610da-1a69-4fe1-999e-1d5c432393c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Another Alternative to Assigning Generalization Sets</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Draw or move a generalization line to a generalization set tree or to a generalization that belongs to a generalization set (see the following figure). The newly created generalization is assigned to the same generalization set.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:width="317"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-07-07 at 10.40.14 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To assign a generalization to a particular generalization set</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Generalization Set Specification window.</li><li>In the <strong>Generalization </strong>property value cell, click the Edit button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="SysMLP" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The Select Generalization dialog opens.</li><li>From the list of generalizations, select all you need and click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d8cabc5-d929-48b7-8cd3-f8511a8b7f87"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select generalizations that belong to other sets. Selecting such a generalization removes it from the previous set and adds it to the current one.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Opening the Generalization Set Specification window and Symbol Properties dialog</h3><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d16676a-5511-4bbd-8a29-c2de0955e417"><ac:parameter ac:name="">specification</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To open its Specification window</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>On the diagram pane, double click on the generalization set name revealing the Generalization Set Specification window.</li><li>Invoking the Generalization Set Specification window from the diagram pane shortcut menu<ol><li>On the diagram pane, right click the generalization set name revealing its shortcut menu.</li><li>Select the <strong>Specification</strong> command opening the Generalization Set Specification window.</li></ol></li><li>Opening the Generalization Set Specification window from the Generalization Specification window.<ol><li>Open the Generalization Specification window by double-clicking on the Generalization arrow.</li><li>In the Generalization Specification window, select the <strong>Generalization Set</strong> property cell.</li><li>Select the generalization set you desire.</li><li>Right-click to open the generalization set shortcut menu. Select <strong>Open Specification</strong>.</li><li>The Generalization Set Specification window opens.</li></ol></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;"><br /></span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc64bff0-e656-4c39-b0e3-a5ddfb6a5c2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">symbol</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To open its Symbol Properties dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the generalization set name and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. The Symbol Properties dialog opens.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;">Grouping generalizations to generalization set trees</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29025f5d-d795-4f78-a11c-f2f32351f025"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Make Generalization Set Tree command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Make Generalization Set Tree command is available if the general element has at least two generalizations and at least two generalizations has assigned the same generalization set.</span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><p>To group the generalizations to the generalization set trees</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a general element on the diagram pane and invoke its shortcut menu.</li><li>Click <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong> Make Generalization Set Tree</strong>. </li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711049 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00597: Generalization tree

- page_id: `136711049`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711049/Generalization+tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:18.624+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Generalization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usually the general element has more than one specific elements and sometimes specific elements may have their specific elements - the diagram with these generalizations may become messy. To display the generalization relationships and specific elements orderly, the generalization tree can be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To group generalization paths into a tree, do one of the following

- When drawing generalizations, connect each following generalization to the first one. [ATTACHMENT filename='connect_generalization_to_first.png']
- If all generalizations are drawn separately, do on of the following:
  - Select the generalization and drag it to the other generalization. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_generalization.png']
  - Select the generalization element and from its shortcut menu select Refactor > Make Sub Tree . [ATTACHMENT filename='make_sub_tree.png']
  - Select all elements you want to add to the tree and, on the diagram toolbar, click the Quick Diagram Layout (Ctrl + Q) button and select one of the tree form layout.

To ungroup a generalization tree, do one of the following

- Right click any relationship in the generalization tree. On the opened shortcut menu, click Ungroup tree .
- Select the end of a concrete generalization relationship and drag it to other element.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Usually the general element has more than one specific elements and sometimes specific elements may have their specific elements - the diagram with these generalizations may become messy. To display the generalization relationships and specific elements orderly, the generalization tree can be created.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To group generalization paths into a tree, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>When drawing generalizations, connect each following generalization to the first one.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="connect_generalization_to_first.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>If all generalizations are drawn separately, do on of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the generalization and drag it to the other generalization.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_generalization.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the generalization element and from its shortcut menu select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Make Sub Tree</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="make_sub_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select all elements you want to add to the tree and, on the diagram toolbar, click the Quick Diagram Layout (Ctrl + Q) button and select one of the tree form layout.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ul><p>To ungroup a generalization tree, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Right click any relationship in the generalization tree. On the opened shortcut menu, click <strong>Ungroup tree</strong>.</li><li>Select the end of a concrete generalization relationship and drag it to other element.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db7e8412-9f3a-4180-b306-a9dae86f712e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Formatting Symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generalization Set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713872 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00598: Generate Output pane

- page_id: `136713872`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713872/Generate+Output+pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:53.167+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Generate Output** pane in Report Wizard allows you to configure report files by selecting the report files’ output location and image format, and displaying the report in the viewer.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Generate Output pane.

The table below describes the function of each option in the **Generate Output** pane of Report Wizard.

| Component Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Report file | Shows the report file’s location and name. The default report location will be \data\template_folder\reports\. The default report name will be the same as the report name defined by the user. |
|  | Opens the Select Location dialog in order to locate the report file. |
| Report image format | Selects an image format for your report: JPG, PNG, SVG, EMF, or WMF.Note:Use *.JPG and *.PNG for any template format.Use *.SVG for text, HTML, DOCX, and DocBooK templates.Use *.EMF and *.WMF for text and Microsoft Office templates (RTF, DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX). |
| Auto image size | Changes the size and orientation of an image before inserting it into a document. There are four options available:No Resize-Image will not be resized or rotated.Fit image to paper (large only)-Large image will be fit to within the paper size.Fit and rotate (clockwise) image to paper (large only)-Large image will be fit towithin the paper size and rotated clockwise.Fit and rotate (counter-clockwise) image to paper (large only)- Large image will be fit to within the paper size and rotated counter-clockwise. |
| Display empty value as | Empty text-Leave an empty value of the report output blank.Custom text as-Enter a custom value for an empty value. The default value is NA. |
|  | Displays a complete report in the viewer. Otherwise, the report will be created and kept in a selected location. |
|  | Generates a report. |
|  | Cancels the report generation process and closes the Report Wizard dialog. |
|  | Provides the Help content. |

Displaying an empty value with a text feature was deprecated. Or, you can select one of the following two options:

- Use a template code to replace empty value with text. For example: Using normal 
codeUsing macro 
code#if (!$var || $var == "")Empty#end#macro (replaceNullValueWithText $var)#if (!var || $var == "")EMPTY#end#end

- Use report wizard environment option to configure empty.

Add the property “template.output.on.blank.field” and value in the Report Wizard Environment option to enable this feature. The value of this property will be inserted in the generated output when the variable is empty (see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard environment options' space='MD190SP1']*).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Generate Output</strong> pane in Report Wizard allows you to configure report files by selecting the report files’ output location and image format, and displaying the report in the viewer.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate Output Pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Generate Output pane.</h6><p> The table below describes the function of each option in the <strong>Generate Output</strong> pane of Report Wizard.</p><table style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th>Component Name </th><th>Description</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Report file</td><td>Shows the report file’s location and name. The default report location will be <em>\data\template_folder\reports\</em>. The <br />default report name will be the same as the report name defined by the user. </td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Selectfilelocation_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Opens the <strong>Select Location</strong> dialog in order to locate the report file.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Report image format</td><td><p>Selects an image format for your report: <em>JPG, PNG, SVG, EMF</em>, or <em>WMF</em>.</p><p>Note:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Use <em>*.JPG</em> and <em>*.PNG</em> for any template format.</li><li>Use <em>*.SVG</em> for text,<em> HTML</em>,<em> DOCX</em>, and<em> DocBooK</em> templates.</li><li>Use <em>*.EMF</em> and <em>*.WMF</em> for text and Microsoft Office templates (<em>RTF, DOCX, XLSX,</em> and <em>PPTX</em>).</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Auto image size</td><td><p>Changes the size and orientation of an image before inserting it into a document. There are four options available:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li><strong>No Resize-</strong>Image will not be resized or rotated.</li><li><strong>Fit image to paper (large only)-</strong>Large image will be fit to within the paper size.</li><li><strong>Fit and rotate (clockwise) image to paper (large only)-</strong>Large image will be fit to<br />within the paper size and rotated clockwise.</li><li><strong>Fit and rotate (counter-clockwise) image to paper (large only)-</strong> <br />Large image will be fit to within the paper size and rotated counter-clockwise.</li></ol></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Display empty value as</td><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Empty text-</strong>Leave an empty value of the report output blank.</li><li><strong>Custom text as-</strong>Enter a custom value for an empty value. The default value is <strong>NA</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayinviewer_option.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Displays a complete report in the viewer. Otherwise, the report will be created and kept in a selected location.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Generates a report.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Cancels the report generation process and closes the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Help_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1">Provides the Help content.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ae31dea-c121-4d00-b575-7d8b1cd6fe74"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Displaying an empty value with a text feature was deprecated. Or, you can select one of the following two options:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use a template code to replace empty value with text. For example:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Using normal <br class="_mce_tagged_br" />code</p></th><th><p>Using macro <br class="_mce_tagged_br" />code</p></th></tr><tr><td><pre class="_mce_tagged_br">#if (!$var || $var == &quot;&quot;)</pre><pre class="_mce_tagged_br"> Empty</pre><pre class="_mce_tagged_br">#end</pre></td><td><pre>#macro (replaceNullValueWithText $var)</pre><pre> #if (!var || $var == &quot;&quot;)</pre><pre> EMPTY</pre><pre> #end</pre><pre>#end</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li>Use report wizard environment option to configure empty.</li></ul><p>Add the property “template.output.on.blank.field” and value in the Report Wizard Environment option to enable this feature. The value of this property will be inserted in the generated output when the variable is empty (see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190SP1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard environment options" /></ac:link></em>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711977 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00599: Generating a differences report

- page_id: `136711977`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711977/Generating+a+differences+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:03.143+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['report', 'reporting', 'generating-report', 'report-templates']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1056652507

1056652509

1056652508

When you're done reviewing differences and wish to see them *on paper*, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.

To generate a differences report using a predefined template

1. Click the Report button in the toolbar that is located at the top of the Merge window. [ATTACHMENT filename='merge_toolbar_report.png']
2. Choose the preferred template – Full Report for a more detailed overview, including the entire Merged Result tree with all changes, or Diagrams Report . [ATTACHMENT filename='reports_shortcut_menu.png']
3. The Save Report dialog opens. Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click Save .

If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.

However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.

##### Creating custom report templates

All the .docx files located in *<configuration files directory>\data\merge reports* are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.

You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the **Report** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Creation Tutorial' space='MD2024x']**Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial**.

1056652506

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.</p><p>However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.</p><h3>Creating custom report templates</h3><p>All the .docx files located in <em>&lt;configuration files directory&gt;\data\merge reports</em> are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.</p><p>You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the <strong>Report</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Template Creation Tutorial" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1953b4d9-a38b-49fc-b2f7-5323ba96c83d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1056652506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Analyzing and managing merge results" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuration files" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00600: Generating a project comparison differences report

- page_id: `136712772`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712772/Generating+a+project+comparison+differences+report
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-12-16T09:56:00.782+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['report', 'reporting', 'generating-report', 'report-templates']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you're done reviewing differences and wish to see them *on paper*, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.

To generate a differences report using a predefined template

1. Click the Report button in the Difference Viewer toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='difference_viewer_toolbar.png']
2. Choose the preferred template – Full Report for a more detailed overview, including the entire Differences tree with all changes, or Diagrams Report . [ATTACHMENT filename='reports_shortcut_menu.png']
3. The Save Report dialog opens. Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click Save .

If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.

However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.

The difference reports cannot be customized/extended with the model information using the usual Report Wizard helper tools.

##### Creating custom report templates

All the .docx files found in the Merge Reports directory are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the **Report** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Creation Tutorial' space='']**Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714324 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00601: Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server

- page_id: `136714324`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714324/Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:22.420+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating reports from the command line
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1300364630

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1300364632

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1300364631

INLINE

Apart from the mandatory commands described in [using the Generate command to generate reports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Using+the+Generate+command+to+generate+reports), you can use the following command arguments in the command line to generate a report from the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) server.

- -server , -login , and -password are key arguments to log on to TWCloud server. You will be prompted for a password when trying to enter -server and -login without a password.
- -pversion is a key argument to specify a TWCloud project version. If either you do not specify any version, or the version you specify does not match any version in the TWCloud repository, Report Wizard will generate the latest project version of the specified branch.
- -branch option is used to retrieve a particular project branch. If you do not specify any branch, the trunk would be specified.
- -updatemodule A Boolean parameter. True for updating the used module of the project to the latest version. False by default, when the user does not specify this option in the command.
- **-tag** is an option to specify a Teamwork project. You can specify the tagName to search for a project version. The returned project version may be more than one. You may use this command argument with "**-incremental true**". If you use this command with **-branch,**it will return a matching project version in the specified branch.
- **-projectpassword** is required to specify a password to open a password-protected Teamwork Cloud project. The password is required for the project to generate a report.
- **-usagepackage** specifies the name of one or more packages in Project Usages of a TWCloud project to be included in the report. Package entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).
- **-usageelement** specifies the name of one or more elements in the Project Usages of TWCloudproject to be included in the report. Element entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;). NoteFor TWCloud projects, **-package** / **-element** is used to select one or more packages/elements only in the Primary Model of TWCloud projects. To select packages/elements in Project Usages, specify **-usagepackage** / **-usageelement**. You can also set **-includeAuxiliary** as true if you want to make auxiliary resources available for selection. This **-usagepackage** / **-usageelement** argument can be used with **-package** / **-element** to select packages/elements in both Primary Model and Project Usages.

If, for example, you want to generate a Web Publisher 2.0 report from a TWCloud project by getting the latest project version of the trunk branch, type the following:

```text

```

The following code generates a Class Specification Report report from the TWCloud project by getting the project version number 10 from MyBranch branch of the MyProject project.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific project version by specifying tagName.

```text

```

The following code generates a project without specifying its branch.

```text

```

The following code generates a project from a branch release.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific version of a project without specifying its branch.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific version of a project from a branch release.

```text

```

The following code generates a set of reports from a specific tag.

```text

```

The following code generates a report through an SSL secured connection.

```text

```

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

The specified project version would be used to generate the report as a first priority, in case the specified project version does not belong in the specified branch.

#### WARNING: Note

Note

If the project version specified is not the latest, the **-updatemodule**option would not affect the **generate** command.

1300364629

INLINE

True for updating the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">used module of the project to the latest version. False by default, <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">when the user does not specify this option in the command.</span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>-tag</strong> is an option to specify a Teamwork project. You can specify the tagName to search for a project version. The returned project version may be more than one. You may use this command argument with &quot;<strong>-incremental true</strong>&quot;. If you use this command with <strong>-branch, </strong>it will return a matching project version in the specified branch.</span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>-projectpassword</strong> is required to specify a password to open a password-protected Teamwork Cloud project. The password is required for the project to generate a report.</span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>-usagepackage</strong> specifies the name of one or more packages in Project Usages of a TWCloud project to be included in the report. Package entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).</span></span></span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>-usageelement</strong> specifies the name of one or more elements in the Project Usages of <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">TWCloud </span>project to be included in the report. Element entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).</span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be995a6d-4ed9-42c1-884b-eb5c3f74649a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For TWCloud projects, <strong>-package</strong> / <strong>-element</strong> is used to select one or more packages/elements only in the Primary Model of TWCloud projects. To select packages/elements in Project Usages, specify <strong>-usagepackage</strong> / <strong>-usageelement</strong>. You can also set <strong>-includeAuxiliary</strong> as true if you want to make auxiliary resources available for selection. This <strong>-usagepackage</strong> / <strong>-usageelement</strong> argument can be used with <strong>-package</strong> / <strong>-element</strong> to select packages/elements in both Primary Model and Project Usages.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>If, for example, you want to generate a Web Publisher 2.0 report from a <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">TWCloud</span> project by getting the latest project version of the trunk branch, type the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53949471-b76f-485d-bfd2-cb06bc32f615"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -servertype "twcloud" -project "MyProject" -package "SamplePackage" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The following code generates a Class Specification Report report from the TWCloud project by getting the project version number 10 from MyBranch branch of the MyProject project.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cc1b800-0b39-449e-bfe5-ba4d982c46cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -servertype "twcloud" -project "MyProject" -branch "MyBranch" -pversion "10" -package "SamplePackage" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific project version by specifying tagName.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2dfab75-3939-4e1a-a12b-b8c927f9024a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -branch "TestingLine" -tag "Finish" -incremental true -output "C:/output/out.docx" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "SamplePackage" -server "localhost" -servertype "twcloud" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a project without specifying its branch.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee12cf15-5bf9-4ede-b971-8d33027950e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a project from a branch release.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed35b12a-2ffc-4a6f-8a06-4c5ee2481ba6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject##release" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific version of a project without specifying its branch.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89f0dbc4-ad1d-4a8a-b341-47c56585c0bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -pversion "2" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific version of a project from a branch release.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecb64675-b957-4281-a946-f304f4ed54d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject##release" -pversion "2" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a set of reports from a specific tag.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3722217c-7af2-4045-a2b2-6285ef6362d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -tag "Finish" -incremental true -output "C:/output/output.docx" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a report through an SSL secured connection.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="210bd89b-d369-4eee-9b3c-ca83ca4388f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -package "Model" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -server "localhost:1111" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -ssl "true"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f0749cc-3915-449e-a973-8cf4f323074c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The specified project version would be used to generate the report as a first priority, in case the specified project version does not belong in the specified branch.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff858fc3-8f37-4343-9afc-b16bacf07a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If the project version specified is not the latest, the <strong>-updatemodule </strong>option would not affect the <strong>generate</strong> command.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5aaa2cc5-47bf-43a8-9869-6e1e7b233c49"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1300364629</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Properties filename" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Uploading generated reports to servers" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Syntax rules" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using the Generate command to generate reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715366 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00602: Generating Code

- page_id: `136715366`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715366/Generating+Code
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:27:06.666+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824354040

824354042

824354039

**Demo**

- Code Generation

824354041

You can generate code for the selected and prepared set as well as directly for model elements.

##### Code Generation for a Set

Start code generation once the set or sets are prepared. For more details about creating and editing sets, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering Sets' space='MD2024x'].

- Choose **Generate** from the **Code Engineering Sets** item shortcut menu. It allows code generating for all created sets.
- Choose **Generate** from the selected set shortcut menu. It allows code generating only for the selected set.

The **Code Generation Options** dialog appears.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Code Generation Options dialog

The **Code Generation Options** dialog allows you to specify the way your code will be generated.

Once you specify generating options for the set, code can be generated.

| Box Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Output Directory | Type the directory where the generated files will be saved. |
|  | The Set Output Directory dialog appears. Select the directory for the generated files. |
| Set as Working Directory | The output directory is set as a working directory and files are saved to the working directory. |
| Reverse before generation | Changes your model according to changes in the existing code.Exercise caution when selecting the Reverse before generation check box. If the model differs from the code, all differences in the model will be discarded. In these cases, you will lose some of your work. |
| If element deleted from model | To influence the structure of generated code, click one of the following option buttons:Delete code. The representation of deleted entities will be deleted from the code file.Comment code. Deleted entities will be commented in the code file. |
| Use spaces in place of tabs | When selected, spaces (instead of tabs) will be written to the code file. |
| Number of spaces | Specify the number of spaces to be written. |
| OK | The Notification Window appears, displaying how code files are being generated. The Notification Window informs you about problems and errors (mainly file access and syntax errors) found in the code generation process and generation summary. You are also prompted to confirm that you wish to overwrite the file if the output directory already contains one with the same name. |
| Cancel | Closes the dialog without saving changes. |
| Help | Displays MagicDraw Help |

Code Generation for a Model Element

All the classes contained in the component will be written into one file. However, code for the class can be generated in a different way. Select the class to generate in the browser Data package and click Generate in the class shortcut menu. For packages and components, you can also select **Generate**, but you will not be able to specify the generation options. All the options related to that task will be set according to the default values.

If you choose framework generation for a single class or for packages, the **Code Generation Options** dialog does not appear. The code is generated according to the default values.

If no errors occurred, you can view the results with your favorite file viewer or programming environment. Look for the files in the directory you specified as your **Working directory** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering Sets' space='MD2024x']**Round Trip Set** dialog or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='MD2024x']**Project Options** dialog. Additional sub-directories could be created.

418416384

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714070 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00603: Generating Reports from Report Wizard

- page_id: `136714070`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714070/Generating+Reports+from+Report+Wizard
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-02-01T14:16:52.330+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1261051786

1261051787

1261051787

In addition to default document templates, Report Wizard allows you to create customized specification document templates. Templates may contain your own custom fields not related to model elements, as well as fields that correspond to model elements. Once customized, a Report Wizard template can be used with data from any project.

You can also format your template to create the style you want, including tables of contents, headers and footers, and page numbers. You can apply most character and paragraph formatting available from your rtf editor to the rtf template or specify with html tags in the HTML template, including keywords. Once the template has been completed, you can run the Report Wizard to create a report in a format that corresponds to the template.

Report Wizard templates can be in *txt, rtf, html, odt, odf, odp, docx, xlsx, pptx*, and *xml* for DocBook or FO files. If you prefer, you can work in the Report Wizard Template Editor until you have finished the template file and use the Report Wizard template windows to save it in the template folder. The default templates are located in the *<modeling tool home>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard/data* folder.

All built-in rtf report templates are combined into the RTF (deprecated) report template category.

##### Synchronizing an attached template with a server project

You can [attach a local template](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2021xR2/Template+Management+Pane) to a server project. Then any user who opens this server project can generate a report with the attached template.

Each time the attached template is updated and changes are committed to the server project, the changes will affect everyone using the attached template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To update the locally attached template from a server project

1. Go to **Options > Environment** menu.

2. Select the **Report Wizard** pane.

3. In the Configure report properties table, set the **synchronizeAttachedTemplate** value to **true.**

This way, the locally attached template will be synchronized with the latest attached template of the server project each time a report is generated from the attached template.

To update the server project with a locally attached template

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space=''].

2. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog.

3. Select the attached template and click **Open**.

4. Edit the attached template and click **Save**.

5. Click **Synchronize**.

6. Commit changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1261051785

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714319 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00604: Generating reports from the command line

- page_id: `136714319`

1333020086

1333020087

1333020087

To generate reports and schedule report printing, type the command in the terminal. This feature allows you to generate reports without opening the MagicDraw application and using Report Wizard.

1333020085

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713946 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00605: Generating reports from the Containment tree

- page_id: `136713946`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713946/Generating+reports+from+the+Containment+tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:55.707+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After creating a Report Data in your model, you can generate a report directly from the Containment tree. You can right-click either Model or Report Data to generate it.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='MD2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='MD2024x'] .

You must specify the template and data in the report data to generate a report from the **Report Data** in the Containment tree.

###### GenerateFromModel

To generate a report from Model in the Containment tree

1. Right-click a package in the Containment tree, select Generate Report , and then select a template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating a report from Model in the Containment tree.

2. Select the save location and type the filename. A dialog will open asking if you want to open the report in the default viewer after the report generating process is complete.

3. Click either [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space='MD2024x'] page to learn more.

###### GenerateFromReportData

To generate a report from Report Data in the Containment tree

1. Create a project in MagicDraw.
2. Click File > Use Project > Use Local Project . The Use Project dialog will open.
3. Select the predefined path: <install.root>\profiles** and the predefined profile: Report Profile
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Finish_button.png'] . The Report Profile will be added to your project in read-only mode.
5. Right-click Model and select Create Element .
6. Select **Report Data**. **Report Data** will be created under Model. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating report data in the Model in the Containment tree.
7. Right-click the Report Data in the Containment tree, and select Specification to open its Specification dialog.
8. Select a template file, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a template file for the Report Data using the Specification dialog.
9. Right-click the Class element in the Containment tree, and select **Quick Generate Report**. The **Select Location** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Generating a report from Report Data in the Containment tree.
10. Either create a new file, or select an existing one (the content of the generated report will override that of the selected file).

- To open the Report Wizard dialog and modify the data or output properties, click the Report Wizard on the shortcut menu.
- By default, the Quick Generate Report command will select the default settings from the last changes saved to the Report Data .

#### Glossary

- Model The package that is placed at the highest level of the Containment tree in MagicDraw.

- Report data A collection of variables in a report in Report Wizard.

- Report template A file that serves as a starting point for a new report.

#### More information about generating reports in Report Wizard

To learn more about generating reports using Report Wizard, you can visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='MD2024x'].
