<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714082 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00606: Generating Use Case Description Reports

- page_id: `136714082`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714082/Generating+Use+Case+Description+Reports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:07.740+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1333739779

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1333739781

1333739777

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1333739781

#### Use Case Report Template

Report Wizard provides a Use Case description document template that is designed to capture all Use Case-related artifacts, e.g. Use Cases, actors, Use Case descriptions, in a report. It is used to describe systems functionality and actors in simple format. This template includes all of the Use Case diagram-related keywords. When you select the highest level of the packages (in MagicDraw, Model is the highest level of the packages) as the scope of the report, only Use Case diagrams and their related elements will be added to the document.

To generate a Use Case description document

1. Open the *Magic Library.mdzip* sample model from the directory. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] This step is necessary for illustration purposes, as well as other steps within this scenario.
2. Click Tools > Report Wizard to open the Report Wizard dialog.
3. Select a template, for example, **Default Template** > **Use Case (Simple),** and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. You can choose to: You can create a new report variable, and modify or delete an existing one through the **Report Variable** dialog.
  1. Select the built-in Report Data. You can modify or delete some variables of the Built-in Report Data in the Report Variable dialog by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='Variable_button.png'] or
  2. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new one. Once you have created a new Report Data, for example, MyReportData, you will see it in the Select Report Data pane. You can select it and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create its report variables. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the Built-in report data. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a custom report data . [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Report Variable dialog.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Next_button.png'] once you have completed modifying or creating the report variables. The Select Element Scope option will open in the Report Wizard dialog.
6. Select the scope of the report from the package tree on the left-hand side. In this case, it will be the MagicLibrary Requirements[MagicLibraryRequirements.mdzip] model package, as all Use Case requirements are stored in this package.
7. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The model package will be moved to the **Selected objects** tree on the right-hand side. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The output options pane will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the location to generate the report, the image format and size, and other output options before generating the report.
9. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Browse_bigbutton.png'] to specify the location in which you want to save the report.
10. Select the file location, enter the filename, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The report file format varies depending on the template file format. For example, if the template file format is **.rtf*, the report file format will be **.rtf* as well.
11. Select the report image format, **.png*, **.jpg*, **.emf*, or**.wmf,*and select an image size option. TipIn some cases, a query may return an empty value that causes blank fields in the report. The **Display empty value as** option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.
12. Select either the **Empty text** or **Custom text** option to display empty value information. If you select the latter, type the information you want to display for every empty value.
13. Select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the report document with a default editor. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
14. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Generate_button.png'] .

The Use Case template is one of the default templates from which you can use and select in the Report Wizard dialog. You can see all of the default templates on this page [Default templates](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Default+templates).

#### Glossary

- Use case A Use Case is a list of actions or event steps of interactions between a system and the users to achieve certain goals.

- Model Model is the highest-level package in the Containment tree in MagicDraw.

- Report data Report Data is a collection of report variables that can be included in a report.

#### Sample model

[ATTACHMENT filename='Magic Library.mdzip']

#### More Information on Generating Reports

You can generate different types of reports with Report Wizard. Learn more about generating the other types of reports on the following pages.

- Web Publisher 2.0 Report
- Web Portal Reports

The <strong>Display empty value as</strong> option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Select either the <strong>Empty text</strong> or <strong>Custom text</strong> option to display empty value information. If you select the latter, type the information you want to display for every empty value.</p></li><li><p>Select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayinviewer_option.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the report document with a default editor.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLibrary_test1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>The Use Case template is one of the default templates from which you can use and select in the Report Wizard dialog. You can see all of the default templates on this page <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Default+templates">Default templates</a>.</p><h2><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Glossary</span></h2><ul><li><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Use case</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br />A Use Case is a list of actions or event steps of interactions between a system and the users to achieve certain goals.<br /></span></li></ul><ul><li><strong>Model</strong><br />Model is the highest-level package in the Containment tree in MagicDraw.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>Report data</strong><br />Report Data is a collection of report variables that can be included in a report.</li></ul><h2>Sample model</h2><p> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Magic Library.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190SP1" ri:content-title="Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></p><hr /><h2>More Information on Generating Reports</h2><p>You can generate different types of reports with Report Wizard. Learn more about generating the other types of reports on the following pages.</p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Web+Publisher+2.0+Report">Web Publisher 2.0 Report</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Web+portal+reports">Web Portal Reports</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711779 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00607: Generic numbering mechanism

- page_id: `136711779`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711779/Generic+numbering+mechanism
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:48:41.521+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: ['numbering', 'numbering-scheme', 'numbering-customization', 'numbering-property', 'element-numbering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1367060059

1367060061

1367060060

INLINE

Our modeling tools provide a generic numbering mechanism that can be applied to any elements created in the modeling tool. The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:

- Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.
- Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.
- Show an element's place in a hierarchy.

With this feature you can easily:

- Customize currently applied numbering formats.
- Create and apply your own numbering formats.
- Change the numbering format while creating numbered elements.

The Generic numbering mechanism feature description is organized in two sections:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with generic numbering mechanism' space='MD2024x'] describes how to number elements, apply numbering formats, and modify numbers.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MD2024x'] explains GUI elements, used while numbering elements.

Before starting to describe the generic numbering mechanism, get acquainted with used concepts:

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Numbering customization | A customization class which specifies the element types that will be numbered, the numbering format that will be used to number elements, and the property to which the created number will be assigned. The numbering format should be defined prior to numbering elements. Usually the system administrator . |
| Numbering property | Indicates an element property where the element number will be stored and defines a numbering scheme that will be used for the element numbering. |
| Numbering scheme | A set of rules describing which numbering formats will be applied on the concrete elements. The Numbering scheme is defined while creating the numbering customization. |
| Prefix | An affix placed before the element number. |
| Separator | A symbol used to separate the different level numbers. The Separator is defined in the numbering scheme. |

1367060058

**Related pages**

The numbering format should be defined prior to numbering elements. Usually the system administrator <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Numbering Customizations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creates the numbering customization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Numbering property</h4></td><td><p>Indicates an element property where the element number will be stored and defines a numbering scheme that will be used for the element numbering.</p></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Numbering scheme</h4></td><td><p>A set of rules describing which numbering formats will be applied on the concrete elements. The Numbering scheme is defined while creating the numbering customization.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Prefix</h4></td><td colspan="1">An affix placed before the element number.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Separator</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>A symbol used to separate the different level numbers. The Separator is defined in the numbering scheme.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e5b0e7e-aa0b-4f57-b570-ec5b29924132"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1367060058</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3bfd823f-2db9-4138-8e95-fed09813b4d8" /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1198506714

1198506717

1198506711

**On this page**

42

1198506716

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Generic Table in MagicDraw gives you the ability to manage selected elements and their properties in a single place.

##### **Purpose**

A Generic Table displays a list of selected elements and their properties in a single place. Using Generic Tables enables you to:

- Edit property values of listed model elements.
- Create selected type elements.
- Create new derived properties or custom columns.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Generic Table project sample allows you to read about the Generic Table feature and analyze examples within that project sample. 
To open the sample, do either:

- On the Welcome screen, select Samples > Diagrams > Generic table .
- Go to < MagicDraw installation directory>\samples\diagrams and open the generic table.mdzip file.

All the examples given in this section are based on the data from this sample.

##### **Specific tasks in Generic Table**

All procedures dedicated only for Generic Table are as follows:

## PAGE 00609: Generic table tool

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352031231

1352031232

1352031232

The Generic Table tool allows templates to:

- Access data of the Generic Table and other Table diagrams, e.g., Instance Table, Metric Table, and Glossary Table. See more samples in the tutorial.
- Use diagrams to get data from the Generic Table.
- Get the row elements.
- Get the column names.
- Get the cell values.
- Get column levels for the Generic Table tree view.
- Check for annotation values in table cells.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Generic Table Dialog.

Like other Custom Tools, the Generic Table tool '*generictabletool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom Tools and the installation, see section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom tool' space='MD2024x'].*

To import the Generic Table tool to a template, for example, type the following code in the template.

```text

```

## PAGE 00610: Generic table tool API

- page_id: `136714481`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714481/Generic+table+tool+API
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-11-07T11:02:24.057+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generic table tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### GenericTableTool diagram.

Class GenericTableTool:

- +getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table
- +getTable( diagramName : String) : Table

Methods:

- +getRows() : List<Element>

- +getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String
- +getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String
- +getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String
- +getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String
- +getVisibleColumnIds() : List<String>
- +getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String
- +getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object
- +getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object
- +getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String
- +getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String

#### Getting generic table data

A Generic Table is a special diagram. You can retrieve it by using the variable $Diagram. Thus, every method must accept the diagram instance or diagram name.

Use the following method to get the Generic Table instance. We use Generic Table instances to retrieve table information such as row elements and column names.

##### Getting generic table instances from diagram elements

To get a Generic Table instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.

**getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – the diagram element.

Return the instance of a Generic Table.

Example code:

```text

```

##### Getting generic table instances from diagram names

To get a Generic Table instance from a specific diagram name, use the following code.

**getTable( diagramName : String) : Table**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – the diagram name.

Return the instance of a Generic Table.

Example code:

```text

```

#### Closing tables

Use the following method to close a table diagram.

##### Closing all diagram tables

**CloseTables() : Void**

```text

```

##### Closing a specific table diagram

**closeTable(diagram: Diagram) : Void**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a table diagram you want to close.

##### Closing a table diagram with a specific diagram name

**closeTable(diagramName: String) : Void**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagramName – the name of a table diagram you want to close.

#### Getting row elements

The Generic Table consists of a series of row elements and column names. Use the following methods to retrieve a list of row elements.

##### Getting all row elements

**getRows() : List<Element>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names.

```text

```

##### Getting all row elements including collapsed

**getRows() : List<Element>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names including collapsed.

```text

```

##### Getting all filtered row elements

**getFilteredRows() : List<Element>**

| $table.getFilteredRows() |
| --- |

The returned value is a list of Elements that have been filtered by the Filter field.

| #foreach($row in $table.getFilteredRows()) $row.name #end |
| --- |

The figure below shows the rows returned after applying the filter.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Rows returned after filtering.

##### Getting row elements in specific row numbers

**getRow( rowNumber : int ) : Element**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- rowNumber – the row number starts with 0.

The returned value is an Element.

#### Getting column names

Column names are referred by column IDs. A column is a MagicDraw QProperty ID that is not readable or accessible by the user. We need to create other methods that provide better usability than using QProperty ID. Use the following methods to retrieve column names.

The methods in this section retrieves column names from the list returned by Show Columns menu item from generic table diagram. The methods in the preceding section, however, retrieves column names from the diagram itself and not from the list returned by Show Columns menu item.

##### Getting column names from column ID

**getColumn( columnId : String) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnId – the ID of a column. Using column IDs has a benefit of consistency between different languages, for example, French and English. The column ID can be retrieved by the method getColumnIds().

The returned value is the name of a column.

##### Getting column names from column numbers

**getColumn(columnNumber : int) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the name of a column.

##### Getting all column names

**getColumnNames() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of column names.

Example code:

Prints all column names.

```text

```

##### Getting all column IDs

**getColumnIds() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of column IDs.

Example code:

Print all columns IDs

```text

```

#### Getting cell values

A cell value is the value in a row element with a column name. To get a cell value, you need to provide both the row element and column. A cell value is an Element or row number. Use the following methods to retrieve a cell value.

##### Getting values from row element and column ID

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnId – a column ID.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element. 
Example code:

Prints the row, column, and value.

```text
$row.name 
#foreach($col in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn$col) : $table.getValue($row, $col) 
#end
#end
#end]]>
```

##### Getting values from row element and column name

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column name.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row element and column number

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers

**getValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row element and column IDs as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnId – a column ID.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.

##### Getting values from row element and column name as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element
- columnName – a column name

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.

##### Getting values from row element and column number as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers as string

**getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers as string with a full type of element in a property

**getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.
- showFullType – the default value is true for showing a full type of element in a cell in String form.

The returned value is the value in a cell, converted into String, and shown in a full type or normal form.

##### Getting cell value from returned object as string

**getCellValueAsString( object : Object ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- object – an object returned from the getValue function.

The returned value is the object in cell value converted into String.

For example:

```text

```

#### Getting visible column and cell values

A visible cell value is the column which is visible on a diagram. Unlike the methods described in section 23.1.5 Getting Cell Values, the following methods will retrieve only the value which is visible on a diagram.

##### Getting all visible column IDs

**getVisibleColumnIds() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of visible column IDs.

##### Getting visible column names from column numbers

**getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the name of a visible column.

##### Getting visible values from row elements and column number

**getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting visible values from row number and column number

**getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting visible values from row element and column number as string

**getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.

##### Getting visible values from row and column numbers as string

**getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.

ac:macro-id="fe9fc602-4063-4fc7-aa70-76006b3386d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generic Table Tool Diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">GenericTableTool diagram.</h6><p>Class GenericTableTool:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">Methods:</p><ul><li>+getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table</li><li>+getTable( diagramName : String) : Table</li><li>+closeTables() : Void</li><li>+closeTable(diagram : Diagram) : Void</li><li>+closeTable(diagramName : String) : Void</li></ul><p>Class Table:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">Methods:</p><ul><li>+getRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</li><li>+getRow( rowNumber : int ) : Element </li><li>+getFilteredRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</li><li>+getColumn( columnId : String) : String </li><li>+getColumn( columnNumber : int) : String </li><li>+getColumnNames() : List&lt;String&gt; </li><li>+getColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt; </li><li>+getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object </li><li>+getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object </li><li>+getValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object </li><li>+getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String </li><li>+getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String </li><li>+getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</li><li>+getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String</li><li>+getVisibleColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt;</li><li>+getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String </li><li>+getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object </li><li>+getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object </li><li>+getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String </li><li>+getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</li></ul><h2>Getting generic table data</h2><p>A Generic Table is a special diagram. You can retrieve it by using the variable $Diagram. Thus, every method must accept the diagram instance or diagram name.</p><p>Use the following method to get the Generic Table instance. We use Generic Table instances to retrieve table information such as row elements and column names.</p><h3>Getting generic table instances from diagram elements</h3><p>To get a Generic Table instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.</p><p><strong>getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="401326ad-61b0-4b2f-9b06-6b315d0790e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagram – the diagram element.</li></ul><p>Return the instance of a Generic Table.</p><p>Example code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3fd0d5a4-9003-4cff-b281-e42936590529"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Generic Table")) 
````html
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram)) 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting generic table instances from diagram names</h3><p>To get a Generic Table instance from a specific diagram name, use the following code.</p><p><strong>getTable( diagramName : String) : Table</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2b7c206-ff7a-4972-bfe8-591384984d9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>diagram – the diagram name.</li></ul><p>Return the instance of a Generic Table.</p><p>Example code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c44b1827-1a27-4be3-a2ab-e83a2080c4e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($diagram = "diagram name") 
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Closing tables</h2><p>Use the following method to close a table diagram.</p><h3>Closing all diagram tables</h3><p><strong>CloseTables() : Void</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55b9c58c-e1d6-4a4e-bad5-e34597d195ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$generic.closeTables()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Closing a specific table diagram</h3><p><strong>closeTable(diagram: Diagram) : Void</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47029cbc-5629-4c72-aaf2-0284e0a17df4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$generic.closeTable($diagram)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagram – a table diagram you want to close.</li></ul><h3>Closing a table diagram with a specific diagram name</h3><p><strong>closeTable(diagramName: String) : Void</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff2ef186-cc73-403f-b341-8ee3af074835"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$generic.closeTable($diagramName)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagramName – the name of a table diagram you want to close.</li></ul><h2>Getting row elements</h2><p>The Generic Table consists of a series of row elements and column names. Use the following methods to retrieve a list of row elements.</p><h3>Getting all row elements</h3><p><strong>getRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37d80f8b-2a3b-4548-97a8-e7e82f7105dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getRows()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b13c6b9-344d-4be4-b80f-ef9f17db6d8c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($row in $table.rows)
$row.name 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting all row elements including collapsed</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><strong>getRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1efb64d5-3e85-4f36-be29-64b63f9c5fe3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getAllRows()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;">The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names including collapsed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="069a96a6-6306-438a-83f9-d8aa09d6f933"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($row in $table.getAllRows())
$row.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Getting all filtered row elements</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">getFilteredRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</span></strong></p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><pre>$table.getFilteredRows()</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The returned value is a list of Elements that have been filtered by the Filter field.</span></p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><pre>#foreach($row in $table.getFilteredRows())
$row.name 
#end</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The figure below shows the rows returned after applying the filter.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Rows returned after filtering" ac:alt="Rows returned after filtering"><ri:attachment ri:filename="107225_filter.png" /></ac:image><br />Rows returned after filtering.</span></h6><h3>Getting row elements in specific row numbers</h3><p><strong>getRow( rowNumber : int ) : Element</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c640a179-774f-4da8-9f3a-c2f0c2b991c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getRow($rowNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – the row number starts with 0.</li></ul><p>The returned value is an Element.</p><h2>Getting column names</h2><p>Column names are referred by column IDs. A column is a MagicDraw QProperty ID that is not readable or accessible by the user. We need to create other methods that provide better usability than using QProperty ID. Use the following methods to retrieve column names.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02dfa638-10bf-4d00-b492-65f7a750bf91"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The methods in this section retrieves column names from the list returned by Show Columns menu item from generic table diagram. The methods in the preceding section, however, retrieves column names from the diagram itself and not from the list returned by Show Columns menu item.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting column names from column ID</h3><p><strong>getColumn( columnId : String) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2e2d30a-8b3f-4485-b329-c15634cd6880"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getColumn($columnId)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>columnId – the ID of a column. Using column IDs has a benefit of consistency between different languages, for example, French and English. The column ID can be retrieved by the method getColumnIds().</li></ul><p>The returned value is the name of a column.</p><h3>Getting column names from column numbers</h3><p><strong>getColumn(columnNumber : int) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12c6ff41-2cef-4125-8727-29231eccd723"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getColumn($columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the name of a column.</p><h3>Getting all column names</h3><p><strong>getColumnNames() : List&lt;String&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="391985c6-c0fe-4f48-9af4-c06fc8ad0942"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getColumnNames()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of column names. </p><p>Example code:</p><p>Prints all column names.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5257286-f3e0-4c28-9db1-ccae9504d77c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($colname in $table.getColumnNames()) 
$colname 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting all column IDs</h3><p><strong>getColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54acafa0-3251-42bd-9946-35fa9a2b2878"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getColumnIds()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of column IDs.</p><p>Example code:</p><p>Print all columns IDs</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46810854-76df-416a-af46-5c962c95cef4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($colid in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn($colid) 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Getting cell values</h2><p>A cell value is the value in a row element with a column name. To get a cell value, you need to provide both the row element and column. A cell value is an Element or row number. Use the following methods to retrieve a cell value.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column ID</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d596b18b-096f-4636-a62e-c7caa173605f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowElement, $columnId)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnId – a column ID.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element. <br />Example code:</p><p>Prints the row, column, and value.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="398c12e9-23d8-471a-9d49-28d353364645"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Generic Table")) 
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram)) 
#foreach($row in $table.getRows()) 
<h1>$row.name</h1> 
#foreach($col in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn$col) : $table.getValue($row, $col) 
#end
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting values from row element and column name</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd5c5321-cbfc-4a9e-839c-ccbd7815640c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowElement, $columnName)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnName – a column name.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row and column numbers as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1d1e090-4dba-4e48-a1c6-0ae908c5b35f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row and column numbers as string with a full type of element in a property</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96d189e3-9821-4731-81e4-caa568d08794"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber, showFullType)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li><li>showFullType – the default value is <strong>true</strong> for showing a full type of element in a cell in String form.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell, converted into String, and shown in a full type or normal form.</p><h3>Getting cell value from returned object as string</h3><p><strong>getCellValueAsString( object : Object ) : String<br /></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2361d9cf-dd0c-4bc2-a6b5-856a38528202"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getCellValueAsString($object)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li><p>object – an object returned from the getValue function.</p></li></ul><p>The returned value is the object in cell value converted into String.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59d0c265-e696-4d03-82ce-9cc6aca99734"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($result = $table.getValue($row, $id))
#if($list.isArray($result))
	#foreach($r in $result)
	- $table.getCellValueAsString($r)
	#end
#else
	$table.getCellValueAsString($result)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Getting visible column and cell values</h2><p>A visible cell value is the column which is visible on a diagram. Unlike the methods described in section 23.1.5 Getting Cell Values, the following methods will retrieve only the value which is visible on a diagram.</p><h3>Getting all visible column IDs</h3><p><strong>getVisibleColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f10f816f-aba0-4ab2-b6b7-1544621f9deb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleColumnIds()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of visible column IDs.</p><h3>Getting visible column names from column numbers</h3><p><strong>getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="064732bf-88e3-4d8b-851d-c512ef9fc6ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleColumn($columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the name of a visible column.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row elements and column number</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e0c1a9c-f9f8-4575-8ba1-72772f971e82"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValue($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row number and column number</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a44a419-d63e-4b05-97ce-4164d2fc9c26"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValue($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row element and column number as string</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edda4a6c-9395-450d-90c5-b09cb8244bdd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValueAsString($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row and column numbers as string</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e5fe694-94b0-4143-9945-7235cf9881f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are:</p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by MagicDraw text representation API.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712961 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00611: Generic Table Wizard

- page_id: `136712961`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712961/Generic+Table+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:11.604+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editions

639875940

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editions

639875942

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editions

639875941

#### NOTE: Editions

Editions

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='MD2024x'] allows you to display a list of selected elements and their properties in a single place.

The Generic Table Wizard helps you create a table when model elements and their relationships are already created and specified. You can select which elements (e.g., classes, packages, use cases, and relationships) will be included in a new table. You can also select properties of the selected element types, which will be displayed as columns in the table.

The Generic Table Wizard guides you through several steps and collects information along the way. It will automatically create a table with your selected elements, freeing you from creating the table manually.

To create a Generic Table using the Generic Table Wizard

1. Open the Generic Table Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Generic Table Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open The Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Generic Table Wizard and click Start .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

639875939

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='MD2024x']

## PAGE 00612: Getting a value from a MagicDraw element to a template

- page_id: `136714521`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714521/Getting+a+value+from+a+MagicDraw+element+to+a+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:39.158+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Creating Your First Template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355429125

1355429128

1355429124

**On this page**

3

1355429128

#### Getting element values to a template

You can access the value of an element, and include it in a report in Report Wizard.

To get a value from a modeling tool element to a template

1. First you need to know the element type and attributes. Open the Specification dialog to see the type and attributes. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Specification dialog showing the element type on the title bar and the attributes in the properties specification table.
2. Open Microsoft Word and type, for example: text The example above shows that **$Class** is an array that contains all the Class elements in a **Selected Element Scope** (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD190SP1'] for more information). **$class** is the individual Class element inside the array. To access the value of a property in **$class**, type: **$class** followed by **"."** and the attribute's name ("**.name**", "**.visibility**", and "**.isAbstract**" are the names of the **$class** attribute). The syntax for accessing the attribute value can be represented by: **$[Referenced object].[Attribute's name]**.

When you generate the output of the template for a project with a single Class element, the result will be shown as follows:

```text

```

#### Glossary

- Array An array is a container object that holds a fixed number of values of a single type. The length of an array is established when the array is created. After it is created, its length is fixed.
- Attribute Attribute defines values that can be attached to instances of the class or interface. For example, an attribute of a class represents the characteristics of the class.
- Class Class is the concrete realization of EncapsulatedClassifier and BehavioredClassifier. The purpose of a class is to specify a classification of objects, and to specify the Features that characterize the structure and behavior of those objects.
- Element Element is a constituent of a model. Every element has the inherent capability of owning other elements. When an element is removed from a model, all its ownedElements are also removed from the model. The abstract syntax for each kind of element specifies what other kind of elements it may own. Every element in a model must be owned by exactly one other element of that model, with the exception of the top-level Packages of the model.

#### More information about working with values in Report Wizard

To learn more about working with values in Report Wizard, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Retrieving Slot Values' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting property value' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5294ab9b-0dbd-4c35-8e21-a339488bd832"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429125</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1c9fce2-185b-4bb6-9dcb-d67262f12cce"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429128</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0309a6cd-d039-4d5b-856e-9ed3865c247f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429124</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c830c14c-1892-431b-a7f2-00db02d796c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d5422f7-7d55-4ac3-a689-fd1b84c6cd89"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429128</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>Getting element values to a template</h2><p>You can access the value of an element, and include it in a report in Report Wizard.</p><p><br /></p><p>To get a value from a modeling tool element to a template</p><hr /><ol><li><p>First you need to know the element type and attributes. Open the Specification dialog to see the type and attributes.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="classspec.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Getting a value from a MagicDraw element to a template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Specification dialog showing the element type on the title bar and the attributes in the properties specification table.</h6></li><li><p>Open Microsoft Word and type, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68018f45-ecc9-4f44-898d-539c24d3e41c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($class in $Class)
 Name: $class.name
 Owner: $class.owner.name
 Visibility: $class.visibility
 Is Abstract: $class.isAbstract
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The example above shows that <strong>$Class</strong> is an array that contains all the Class elements in a <strong>Selected Element Scope</strong> (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190SP1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Wizard user guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more information). <strong>$class</strong> is the individual Class element inside the array. To access the value of a property in <strong>$class</strong>, type: <strong>$class</strong> followed by <strong>&quot;.&quot;</strong> and the attribute's name (&quot;<strong>.name</strong>&quot;, &quot;<strong>.visibility</strong>&quot;, and &quot;<strong>.isAbstract</strong>&quot; are the names of the <strong>$class</strong> attribute). The syntax for accessing the attribute value can be represented by: <strong>$[Referenced object].[Attribute's name]</strong>. </p></li></ol><p>When you generate the output of the template for a project with a single Class element, the result will be shown as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a810927-61b0-4b82-8bd9-273ff4b33faa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Name: Customer
 Owner: com
 Visibility: public
 Is Abstract: false]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Array</strong><br />An array <span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);">is a container object that holds a fixed number of values of a single type. The length of an </span><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);">array</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> is established when the </span><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);">array</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> is created. After it is created, its length is fixed.</span></li><li><strong>Attribute</strong><br />Attribute defines values that can be attached to instances of the class or interface. For example, an attribute of a class represents the characteristics of the class.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Class</strong><br />Class is the concrete realization of EncapsulatedClassifier and BehavioredClassifier. The purpose of a class is to specify a classification of objects, and to specify the Features that characterize the structure and behavior of those objects.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Element</strong><br />Element is a constituent of a model. Every element has the inherent capability of owning other elements. When an element is removed from a model, all its ownedElements are also removed from the model. The abstract syntax for each kind of element specifies what other kind of elements it may own. Every element in a model must be owned by exactly one other element of that model, with the exception of the top-level Packages of the model.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with values in Report Wizard</h2><p>To learn more about working with values in Report Wizard, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Retrieving Slot Values" /></ac:link></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Getting property value" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712745 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00613: Getting comparison summary information and changes legend

- page_id: `136712745`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712745/Getting+comparison+summary+information+and+changes+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:50.103+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['summary-and-legend', 'comparison-summary', 'comparison-result']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see how many differences have been detected between the base and compared project version on the **Summary and legend** panel that is located at the top right of the **Difference Viewer** window. The panel also helps to understand the decorations and highlighting of elements in both the **Differences**tree and the **Specification** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can see how many differences have been detected between the base and compared project version on the <strong>Summary and legend</strong> panel that is located at the top right of the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window. The panel also helps to understand the decorations and highlighting of elements in both the <strong>Differences </strong>tree and the <strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fa21c6bb-741a-4318-83ff-bf732f4ae486">Specification</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fa21c6bb-741a-4318-83ff-bf732f4ae486"> panel.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:title="Project comparison summary and legend" ac:alt="Project comparison summary and legend"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_legend.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Getting comparison summary information and changes legend" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711923 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00614: Getting merge summary information and changes legend

- page_id: `136711923`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711923/Getting+merge+summary+information+and+changes+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:01.623+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merge-summary', 'summary-and-legend', 'merged-result', 'merge-legend']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1767089336

INLINE

1767089338

INLINE

1767089337

INLINE

The **Summary/Legend** panel is located at the bottom left of the **Merge** window. The panel helps to understand the marking and highlighting of elements in both the **Merged Result** tree/list and the **Specification** panel.

On the **Summary/Legend** panel, for 3-way merge, you can see how many differences have been detected between the ancestor and the source, as well as between the ancestor and the target.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For 2-way merge, you can see how many differences have been detected between two versions, and what kind of changes were detected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1767089335

INLINE

detected. </p><p><ac:image ac:height="110"><ri:attachment ri:filename="2-way_summary and legend.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Getting merge summary information and changes legend" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37b66711-5821-4d5b-85b0-0d6111e42368"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1767089335</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713993 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00615: Getting property value

- page_id: `136713993`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713993/Getting+property+value
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-01T13:55:03.466+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template variables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can directly get the property value of the element by calling property name:

```text

```

**propertyName** should be typed in camel case style. The name and returned type of property can be found in UMLMetaModel UserGuide. It lists all the properties which can be called through each element type.

Please be aware of the type of the returned value, as some properties may return an array. You can get each value by using a **#foreach** directive or by specifying array index number. For example:

```text

```

```text

```

Moreover, the customized elements are able to get their property value immediately by calling property name:

```text

```

You can see the representation text of the element by opening the Specification dialog.

The dialog name pattern is

```text
]]>
```

While the property name is shown in the same dialog.

If the property value is not printed out to your report, you can assume the property that you call is not the property of the customized element. It may be the property of the applied stereotype. You would rather check the stereotype owner of that property by going to the Applied Stereotype property of the element, then right click on the right column, and select the stereotype in the containment tree.

If you navigate to the stereotype element on the containment tree, then you can expand it to see its owned properties. If you can see your desired property in the stereotype, you can get its value by:

```text

```

We also provide helper module in section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Helper tools' space='']*. These modules are utility modules for getting property values. More details about openAPI of each element can be found at:

*{md_install}\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\api\javadoc.zip*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can directly get the property value of the element by calling property name:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4704d028-1cde-4b3f-aa73-4ccaa0daed78"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.propertyName]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>propertyName</strong> should be typed in camel case style. The name and returned type of property can be found in UMLMetaModel UserGuide. It lists all the properties which can be called through each element type.</p><p>Please be aware of the type of the returned value, as some properties may return an array. You can get each value by using a <strong>#foreach</strong> directive or by specifying array index number. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c63d14e2-10d4-42d2-9839-cfdb8d143030"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($c in $class.classifier)
$c.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02b425b7-c111-4c29-a335-f9603f84261e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($classifier = $class.classifier)
$classifier.get(0)
$classifier.get(1)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Moreover, the customized elements are able to get their property value immediately by calling property name:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="817fc16a-028f-4179-8361-0ddafae72d98"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$RepresentationText.propertyName]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can see the representation text of the element by opening the Specification dialog.</p><p>The dialog name pattern is </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b36348c4-070a-46ff-9aab-a36d797f7a4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Specification of <RepresentationText> <ElementName>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>While the property name is shown in the same dialog.</p><p>If the property value is not printed out to your report, you can assume the property that you call is not the property of the customized element. It may be the property of the applied stereotype. You would rather check the stereotype owner of that property by going to the Applied Stereotype property of the element, then right click on the right column, and select the stereotype in the containment tree.</p><p>If you navigate to the stereotype element on the containment tree, then you can expand it to see its owned properties. If you can see your desired property in the stereotype, you can get its value by:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af6d8949-f382-44c9-bb2d-ebd943467c0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getStereotypePropertyString($element, "stereotypeName", "propertyName")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />We also provide helper module in section <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Helper tools" /></ac:link></em>. These modules are utility modules for getting property values. More details about openAPI of each element can be found at:</p><p><em>{md_install}\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\api\javadoc.zip</em></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712289 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00616: Getting started with specifying criteria

- page_id: `136712289`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712289/Getting+started+with+specifying+criteria
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:24.513+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2217659847

2217659849

1759777648

**On this page:**

3

2217659848

##### Opening the dialog for specifying criteria

The dialog for specifying criteria can be opened from several different places.

To open the dialog for specifying criteria, do one of the following

- In the Criteria area of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024x'] , click the ... button next to the Dependency Criteria box. The Dependency Criteria dialog opens.
- In the Criteria area of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space='MD2024x'] , click the ... button next to the Relation Criteria box. The Relation Critera dialog opens.
- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart Package' space='MD2024x'] , under the Content category on the right, click the cell of the Query property value, and then click the ... button. The Query dialog opens.
- Open the Specification window of a selected element, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Traceability' space='MD2024x'] on the left, and then click Create on the right. The Expression dialog opens.
- On the toolbar of a generic table, click the Columns button, and then select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom traceability rules' space='MD2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with columns' space='MD2024x'] . The Expression dialog opens.
- Open the Specification window of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating executable opaque behaviors' space='MD2024x'] , click the cell of the Body and Language property value, and then click the ... button. When the Body and Language dialog opens, select StructuredExpression from the Language drop-down list.
- Open the Specification window of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='MD2024x'] , click the specification cell of the Elements by Condition property, and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] . The Elements by Condition dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The layout of the dialog is the same in all the described cases. On the left side of the dialog, you can select an operation type that best suits for specifying the needed criterion. Selecting an operation type opens the criteria specification panel on the right side of the dialog.

##### Specified and unspecified operation display

The Simple Navigation, Metachain Navigation, Find, and Implied Relation operations are always displayed on the left side of the dialog, even if they were removed during the previous edits of the expression. For this reason, empty template operation nodes are displayed in the italic format below the specified operations. Once you specify an expression for a template operation, a template operation of that type will not appear during subsequent edits. In such a case, only the specified operation is displayed. You can create a new operation via *Create operation...* option.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']On the left: empty template operations in italic format. 
On the right: template operations with specified operations in roman format. Empty template operations of these types no longer appear during the subsequent edit.

If an operation is specified, it is displayed in roman format and at the top of the operations list. You can manually rearrange the specified operations by dragging and dropping them anywhere on the list. In such cases, the arrangement of the specified operations stays the same when you open the expression again.

Also, be advised that before starting to specify criteria it is useful to read the tips and warnings (if there are any), which appear after the pointer is hovered over the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button or the [IMAGE alt='' src='']button respectively.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
'Find' operation is specified, appearing in roman formatand at the top of the operations list. Other operations under 'Find' are not specified and are thus italicized.

2217659846

**Related pages**

- Specifying criteria for querying model
  - Using Simple Navigation
  - Using Metachain Navigation
  - Using Find operation
  - Using Implied Relations
  - Creating new operations
    - Calling operations from the model
    - Creating script operations

On the left side of the dialog, you can select an operation type that best suits for specifying the needed criterion. Selecting an operation type opens the criteria specification panel on the right side of the dialog.</p><h3>Specified and unspecified operation display</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f72e8f0b-eea7-49e3-a373-0c508b60b738">The Simple Navigation, Metachain Navigation, Find, and Implied Relation operations are always displayed on the left side of the dialog, even if they were removed during the previous edits of the expression. In such a case, only the specified operation is displayed.
