<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713333 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00617: Global element usage search

- page_id: `136713333`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713333/Global+element+usage+search
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-09-22T13:09:27.942+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Prerequisites

1674906535

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Prerequisites

1674906537

1231783541

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Prerequisites

1674906536

The global element usage search allows you to check the usages of elements within the Teamwork Cloud repository. This makes it easier to examine how deleting or modifying an element can impact the projects that use it. The following demo shows how this functionality works:

700394

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- You can only use the Global Element Usage Search feature if [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space='TWCloud2024x'] on the server.
- You can investigate element usages only in projects that have been [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating indexing configurations' space='TWCloud2024x'].
- Your web application platform URL must be entered in the environment options . If this URL is not added to the environment options, once you select Related Elements > Used by in Server, a window appears, prompting you to enter the web application platform URL.

The command is only available for the elements in an open (main) project. You cannot execute it if:

- the selected element is from a Used Project
- the selected element has not yet been committed to the server
- multiple elements are selected

To find element usages

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] or diagram pane, right-click an element and select Related Elements > Search in Server > Used by in Server . Choose this option if you want to search solely for the usages of the element.
  - In the Containment tab or diagram pane, right-click an element and select Related Elements > Search in Server > Used by in Server Recursively . Choose this option if you want to search for the usages of both the element and all of the elements that are owned by it.

The element usage search results panel opens with a list of server projects that use the selected element:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Only the latest indexed branch versions are found. For example, if an element is used in the historical version but is no longer in use, it is not displayed in the element usage search results panel.

If you see a warning message (see below), it means that you do not have permission to read the project(s) using the element you performed the usage search for.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Press the Refresh button [IMAGE alt='' src='']to see the latest information on element usage.

2. Select a project to see a list of project branches and versions thatuse the element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Press **Actions [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > Open project**to open the specific project branch that uses the element.

3. To investigate element usage in more detail, select a specific branch. This shows usage details at the element level:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1674906533

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space='TWCloud2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating indexing configurations' space='TWCloud2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4e1873e-49ec-448f-ad5d-af8da43ddc69"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1674906535</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0ad1f4e-ea0e-4a3e-b24a-322a8c12a0fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1674906537</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da713ceb-8e50-41ee-a32b-a86d054dae7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1231783541</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d4ac85b-448f-4632-949d-09dd5f170460"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ab8fbf0-3b88-404a-b094-55d5174405db"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1674906536</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The global element usage search allows you to check the usages of elements within the Teamwork Cloud repository. This makes it easier to examine how deleting or modifying an element can impact the projects that use it. The following demo shows how this functionality works:<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dd12022-28f4-4e1f-a4ef-94439f6c184e"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1TUWPUtM4Pc" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">394</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a596da95-c8bd-4ec6-932d-e8132d0bcfc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can only use the Global Element Usage Search feature if <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Elasticsearch is installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the server.</li><li><p>You can investigate element usages only in projects that have been <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Creating indexing configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[indexed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Your web application platform URL must be entered in the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Environment+Options">environment options</a>. If this URL is not added to the environment options, once you select Related Elements &gt; Used by in Server, a window appears, prompting you to enter the web application platform URL.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command is only available for the elements in an open (main) project. You cannot execute it if:</p><ul><li>the selected element is from a Used Project</li><li>the selected element has not yet been committed to the server</li><li>multiple elements are selected</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To find element usages</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link><span> </span>or diagram pane, right-click an element and select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt;<span> </span><strong>Search in Server &gt; Used by in Server</strong>. Choose this option if you want to search solely for the usages of the element.</li><li>In the <a class="external-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2022xR1/Containment+tab">Containment tab</a><span> </span>or diagram pane, right-click an element and select <strong>Related Elements</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Search in Server &gt; Used by in Server Recursively</strong>. Choose this option if you want to search for the usages of <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">both the element and </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">all of the elements that are owned by it.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p>The element usage search results panel opens with a list of server projects that use the selected element:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_element_usage_search_results.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c567ba2-6e9a-436f-83f3-77a6c65a5ea1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only the latest indexed branch versions are found. For example, if an element is used in the historical version but is no longer in use, it is not displayed in the element usage search results panel.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you see a warning message (see below), it means that you do not have permission to read the project(s) using the element you performed the usage search for.</p><p><ac:image ac:width="674"><ri:attachment ri:filename="some_usages_cannot_be_displayed.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b21078d-3a35-432c-b9ec-f8b2f0be9ee2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Press the Refresh button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png" /></ac:image>t<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">o see the latest information on element usage</span><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>2. Select a project to see a list of project <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">branches and versions that </span>use the element.</p><p><ac:image ac:width="674"><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_usage_in_project_branches.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23ec14e1-8db2-4969-a4f4-4b855a177c91"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Press <strong>Actions <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions_icon.png" /></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">&gt; Open project</span> </strong>to open the specific project branch that uses the element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>3. To investigate element usage in more detail, select a specific branch. This shows usage details at the element level:</p><p><ac:image ac:width="674"><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_usage_details.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f285c9e4-3767-4c3e-b255-83724aa9f5b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1674906533</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating indexing configurations" ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715378 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00618: Global options for Code Engineering

- page_id: `136715378`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715378/Global+options+for+Code+Engineering
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:33:57.553+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

419230510

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

419230512

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

419230511

##### Code Engineering Options for all Sets in your Project

Choose**Project**from the**Options**menu. TheProject Optionsdialog box appears.You can set the Code Engineering options in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x']**Project Options** dialog. The **Project Options** dialog box has two main collections of customizable options, represented by the hierarchy tree on the left side of the dialog box. The figure below illustrates **Code Engineering** related options.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Code Engineering options in the Project Options dialog.

In the option list, select **Code engineering**and specify the following items:

| Item name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Default Working Directory | Enter the name of your working directory. |
| Default Working Package | Define any package for the reverse output or code generation. Your model will be reversed or the code will be generated from this specified package. |
| Default Paths for References | Add specific profiles, used projects, or libraries to define where to search for paths for references during reverse/code generation. |
| Default Encoding | Click to open a list of available encodings. |
| Default language | When selected, the syntax checker of the selected language runs while Code Engineering is executed. |
| Use Syntax Checker | When selected, the syntax checker runs while Code Engineering is executed. |
| Directory for Temporal Files | You can specify Active Directory, System, or you can select another directory. |

The following table describes other **Code Engineering**options:

| Option | Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Code Generation |  | Set code generation options using the fields listed in the right side of the Project options dialog box. The Code generation area contains boxes with the same functionality as the Code generations options dialog box. See . |
| Reverse |  | Set reverse options for all reverse actions of the project using the options listed on the right side of the Project options dialog box. The area contains boxes that have the same functionality as in the Reverse options dialog box. |
| Java Language Options Set the generated code style for Java programming languages in the Default language field found on the right side of the Project Options dialog box. | Generate opening bracket in new line | Opens a bracket in the new line being generated. |
|  | Generate spaces | Generate spaces inside an assignment and other operators. |
|  | Generate empty documentation | Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless class in the model has no documentation. |
|  | Automatic “import” generation | Automatic generation of "import" sentences according to classes referenced in the generated class. |
|  | Class count to generate import on demand | Specify the number of classes imported from a single package until all statements importing a single class are substituted with a statement importing an entire package. |
|  | Documentation Processor | After selecting the Java Doc processor, click to open the . |
|  | Style | Two styles are available for documentation. |
|  | Use CLASSPATH | The button is activated. Search a classpath for importing sentence generation in the Set classpath dialog box.Click Get from System to get the CLASSPATH variable defined by the operating system or click Add and select the classpath directory. |
|  | Java Source | Available choices are 1.4, 5.0, 7.0, or 8.0. |
|  | Header | Add a specific header to all your code files. Click and enter the header text in the Header dialog. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. |
| C++ Language Options Set the generated code style for C++ programming languages. | Generate opening bracket in new line | Opens a bracket in the newly generated line. |
| Generate spaces | Spaces inside an assignment and other operators are generated. |  |
| Generate empty documentation | Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless the class in the model has no documentation. |  |
|  | Generate methods body into class | Select check box to generate methods body into class. |
|  | Generate field initialization into class | Generates the value initialization for the attribute. |
|  | Automatically remove unnecessary usage relationship | Tracks the use of classes and removes the unnecessary include directives. |
|  | Documentation Style | Four styles are available for documentation. |
|  | Use include path | Click and then specify the path for the #include directives in the Set Include Path dialog box. |
|  | Use explicit macros | Select the check box. The button is activated. Click it and use a set of predefined macros in the C++ Macros dialog box. |
|  | Header | Add the specific header to all your code files. Click and enter the header text in the Header screen. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. |
| CORBA IDL 3.0 language options | Generate documentation | Includes the documentation of an element in the comment. |
|  | Generate opening bracket in new line | Opens a bracket in the newly generated line. |
|  | Generate spaces | Spaces inside an assignment and other operators are generated. |
|  | Generate empty documentation | Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless the class in the model has no documentation. |
|  | Generate imports | Generation of "import" statements for classes referenced in the generated class. |
|  | Generate preprocessor directives | Generates pre-processors directives. |
|  | Generate end comments for composite elements | Generates end comments with composite element names. |
|  | Generate operation parameters on the new line | Generates operation parameters on the new line. |
|  | Documentation Style | Three styles are available for documentation. |
|  | Header “...” | Add the specific header to all your code files. Click and enter the header text in the Header dialog. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. Since MagicDraw version 17.0, the variable $DOCUMENTATION can be used to export the documentation of the file component (from the File View package) as a header of the IDL file.Functionality AvailabilityThe variable $DOCUMENTATION is available with MagicDraw 17.0 Service Pack 4 and later versions. |
|  | Set Include Path | Specify the path for the #include directives. Click to open the Select Folder dialog box. |
| DDL Language Options | Generate opening bracket in new line | Opens a bracket in the newly generated line. |
|  | Generate spaces | Spaces inside an assignment and other operators are generated. |
|  | Generate documentation | Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless the class in the model has no documentation. |
|  | Header | Add the specific header to all your code files. Click the button and enter header text in the Header dialog. You may also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. |

statements for classes referenced in the generated class.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate preprocessor directives </strong></td><td colspan="1">Generates pre-processors directives.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate end comments for composite elements</strong></td><td colspan="1">Generates end comments with composite element names.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate operation parameters on the new line</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p>Generates operation parameters on the new line.</p><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Documentation Style </strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Three styles are available for documentation.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Header “...” </strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Add the specific header to all your code files. Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and enter the header text in the </span><strong>Header </strong><span>dialog. You can also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header. </span></p><p>Since MagicDraw version 17.0, the variable $DOCUMENTATION can be used to export the documentation of the file component (from the File View package) as a header of the IDL file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a0c4e0e-b9f7-4dcf-92ee-500dec1899d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Functionality Availability</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>The variable $DOCUMENTATION is available with MagicDraw 17.0 Service Pack 4 and later versions.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Set Include Path </strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Specify the path for the <span>#include directives</span>. Click  <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  to open the </span><strong>Select Folder </strong><span>dialog box. </span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>DDL Language Options</strong></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate opening bracket in new line</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Opens a bracket in the newly generated line.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate spaces </strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Spaces inside an assignment and other operators are generated.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><strong>Generate documentation </strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Comment brackets are placed in your code, unless the class in the model has no documentation. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Header </strong></p></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Add the specific header to all your code files. Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and enter header text in the <strong>Header</strong> dialog. You may also define $DATE, $AUTHOR, and $TIME in the header.</span></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a74b13f-fa26-482c-b521-507bdc912657"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419230507</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7ff21439-4c52-49b5-bbda-c9546c4dc6b1" /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="6a72c68b-7821-4223-869d-bc84e62d7a25"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712918 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00619: Global validation rules

- page_id: `136712918`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712918/Global+validation+rules
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:07.475+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Sample model

INLINE

Global validation rules check the result of an element set, while validation rules contained in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined validation suites' space='MD2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation suites' space='MD2024x'] are dedicated to check a separate element. You can create global validation rules in the same way as other validation rules by defining an appropriate condition in the**Specification** property.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new validation rules' space='MD2024x']]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining Constraint condition' space='MD2024x']>]]>

For example, the *balanced budget* validation rule (Constraint specification is shown in the following figure) is a global validation rule. It is created for the classifier of the *ReseachProject* Class. The *balanced budget* validation rule condition, defined by using OCL2.0 language in the **Specification** property, expresses that the sum of the expenses of all projects must be less than the sum of all *Sponsor* contributions. This validation rule is global because it includes all instances in the model, rather than only the particular instance of *ResearchProject* that violates the rule.

#### TIP: Sample model

Sample model

An example of a global validation rule can be found in the **model validation** sample model > *Classifiers Validation Rules* [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='model validation.mdzip'].*
- Find in the modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\model validation.mdzip.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The specification of the global validation rule named balanced budget.

After the validation finishes, the global validation rule is marked as <model> in the****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024x']**Validation Results** panel > **Element** column. This means that the entire model, not the separate element, violates the rule.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The representation of global validation rule in the Validation Results panel.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710328 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00620: Glossary table

- page_id: `136710328`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710328/Glossary+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:28:07.120+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: ['terms']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

219319399

219319402

219328322

**On this page**

219319400

##### **Description**

The Glossary saves time by ensuring consistent usage of terminology in the organization. It improves communication between team members, as terms are understood to have the same definitions and usages everywhere they appear.

Each row in the table represents a term. A word, phrase, or any element of the model can be a term. Words and phrases defined as terms are underlined. This enables you to identify terms in your model.

The word or phrase is underlined only if it completely matches the defined term. For example, if you defined the word *Project* in your glossary, the word *Projects* would not be underlined because it is plural.

##### **Purpose**

The Glossary Table allows you to:

- Create a list of all the specific terms used in the project.
- Define your terms in one place.
- Use them everywhere in your project.
- Export your glossary to a new project and use it in other projects.
- Export your glossary into .html, .csv, or .xlsx files.
- Have several glossaries in one project.
- Customize the representation of the glossary in the table.

##### **Usage**

The video below demonstrates how to use the glossary in MagicDraw when working with Requirements.

1000550

##### **Example**

The following figure illustrates the terms defined in the Glossary Table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The figure below shows the term visibility on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Specific tasks in the Glossary Table**

The following outlines the specific procedures when working with Glossary Tables:

219319398

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714421 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00621: Groovy script tool

- page_id: `136714421`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714421/Groovy+script+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:35.617+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1357810331

1357810332

1357810332

Groovy Script tool enables report templates to evaluate or run Groovy codes from templates and external Groovy files.

The Groovy tool includes two methods:

**(i) 'eval' method** 
 This method will evaluate Groovy text, and then return the result. 
**(ii) 'execute' method** 
 This method will execute a Groovy file, and then return the result.

Like other Script Tools, the Groovy Script Tool '*scripttool.jar*' must be installed in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. The same '*groovy-all-1.7.9.jar*' of the Automaton plugin is used, so make sure the Automaton plugin is enabled and loaded. For further information about Custom Tools and the installation, see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom tool' space='MD2024x']*.

To import Groovy Script tool to a template, type the following code:

```text

```

1357810330

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3b53526-2dd9-436e-82b5-7a620293e551"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357810331</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="869d5d50-802f-4108-b555-e38338f61835"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357810332</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="174b2de8-f5f0-4eb3-85dc-ce3600ab8f99"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357810332</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Groovy Script tool enables report templates to evaluate or run Groovy codes from templates and external Groovy files.</p><p>The Groovy tool includes two methods:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><strong>(i) 'eval' method</strong> <br />     This method will evaluate Groovy text, and then return the result.<br /><strong>(ii) 'execute' method</strong> <br />     This method will execute a Groovy file, and then return the result.</p><p>Like other Script Tools, the Groovy Script Tool '<em>scripttool.jar</em>' must be installed in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. The same '<em>groovy-all-1.7.9.jar</em>' of the Automaton plugin is used, so make sure the Automaton plugin is enabled and loaded. For further information about Custom Tools and the installation, see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Custom tool" /></ac:link></em>.</p><p>To import Groovy Script tool to a template, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d52fdb3f-26d9-4509-9180-f02fbf465806"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('groovy', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.script.GroovyTool')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c0f5b2f-4439-4633-99c9-eeb069610521"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357810330</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="987fab6b-993d-42a2-a0f9-7a7759bd6216" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714422 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00622: Groovy script tool API

- page_id: `136714422`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714422/Groovy+script+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:35.986+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Groovy script tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### 'eval' Method

**eval(String script)**

This method will evaluate a Groovy code from a string and return the result.

```text

```

**eval(String script, String bindingName, Object bindingObject)**

This method will evaluate a Groovy code with a single binding object and specified binding name. The code will be evaluated from a string. The binding name will be used as the name for this object.

```text

```

**eval(String script, Map bindingMap)**

This method will evaluate a Groovy code with a set of binding arguments (a name and an object). The code will be evaluated from a string. The binding map consists of key-value pairs for the binding name and the binding object.

```text

```

Or

```text

```

The second code contains curly brackets; '{' and '}' characters, which are not allowed to be used in any *RTF* template. For the *RTF* template, use the first code instead.

#### 'execute' method

**execute(String filename)**

This method will execute a Groovy file. The 'filename' parameter refers to a name of the Groovy file, an absolute path to the Groovy file, or a relative path starting from the template to the Groovy file.

```text

```

If the Groovy file contains Groovy functions, you can recall the functions by using 'eval()' methods.

```text

```

**execute(String filename, String bindingName, Object bindingObject)**

This method will execute a Groovy file with a single binding object and specified binding name. The 'filename' parameter refers to a name of the Groovy file, an absolute path to the Groovy file, or a relative path starting from the template to the Groovy file. The binding name will be used as the name for this object.

File filename.groovy

```text

```

The template code

```text

```

**execute(String filename, Map bindingMap)**

This method will execute a Groovy file with a set of binding arguments (a name and an object). The 'filename' parameter refers to a name of the Groovy file, an absolute path to the Groovy file, or a relative path starting from the template to the Groovy file. The binding map consists of key-value pairs for the binding name and the binding object.

File filename.groovy

```text

```

The template code

```text

```

- Absolute Path : If the 'filename' is provided with an absolute path, Groovy Tool will read the Groovy file from an absolute location such as $groovy.execute(' c:/mycode/readclass.groovy ') .
- Relative Path : If the 'filename' is provided with a relative path, Groovy Tool will read the template from a relative location. This relative location starts from the current directory in which the template is located such as $groovy.execute(' readclass.groovy ') .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h2>'eval' Method</h2><p><strong>eval(String script)</strong></p><p>This method will evaluate a Groovy code from a string and return the result.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a33a1f1-f29e-4307-81b9-57862248176f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$groovy.eval("println 'Hello World!'")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>eval(String script, String bindingName, Object bindingObject)</strong></p><p>This method will evaluate a Groovy code with a single binding object and specified binding name. The code will be evaluated from a string. The binding name will be used as the name for this object.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58fe7cf5-28c9-4058-8c49-b196e383723f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($c in $Class)
$groovy.eval("println classname", "classname", $c.name)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>eval(String script, Map bindingMap)</strong></p><p>This method will evaluate a Groovy code with a set of binding arguments (a name and an object). The code will be evaluated from a string. The binding map consists of key-value pairs for the binding name and the binding object.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9f6d2fe-35b7-4026-ab7c-eddd9e604484"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($dict = $map.createHashMap())
#set ($void = $dict.put("first", "foo"))
#set ($void = $dict.put("last", "bar"))
$groovy.eval("println first + last", $dict)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Or</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78fb6923-74a4-42f2-80ed-7dd51974bb4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$groovy.eval("println first + ' ' + last", {"first":"foo", "last":"bar"})]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65cfc62e-bc62-457d-9602-34b93ca8b902"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The second code contains curly brackets; '{' and '}' characters, which are not allowed to be used in any <em>RTF</em> template. For the <em>RTF</em> template, use the first code instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>'execute' method</h2><p><strong>execute(String filename)</strong></p><p>This method will execute a Groovy file. The 'filename' parameter refers to a name of the Groovy file, an absolute path to the Groovy file, or a relative path starting from the template to the Groovy file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c45cd41-14fe-49c2-b655-ba8d72fabcca"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$groovy.execute("filename.groovy")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If the Groovy file contains Groovy functions, you can recall the functions by using 'eval()' methods.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23471ea8-420f-4626-ac33-ceee8da6a8e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$groovy.execute('filename.groovy')
$groovy.eval("new groovy().functionname()")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>execute(String filename, String bindingName, Object bindingObject)</strong></p><p>This method will execute a Groovy file with a single binding object and specified binding name. The 'filename' parameter refers to a name of the Groovy file, an absolute path to the Groovy file, or a relative path starting from the template to the Groovy file. The binding name will be used as the name for this object.</p><p>File filename.groovy</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8419f1c5-de5f-440f-ae12-f0bad450ecdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA["Class name is " + $c.name]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The template code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1977f794-8fcd-4669-8b9c-2dcab9cdb9be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($c in $Class)
$groovy.execute("filename.groovy", 'c', $c)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>execute(String filename, Map bindingMap)</strong></p><p>This method will execute a Groovy file with a set of binding arguments (a name and an object). The 'filename' parameter refers to a name of the Groovy file, an absolute path to the Groovy file, or a relative path starting from the template to the Groovy file. The binding map consists of key-value pairs for the binding name and the binding object.</p><p>File filename.groovy</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be28b4ed-e9b6-4192-a2e9-286c4e728837"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[first + " " + last]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The template code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfecb802-5ca5-4f91-a3e4-78cec3eeb108"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($dict = $map.createHashMap())
#set ($void = $dict.put("first", "foo"))
#set ($void = $dict.put("last", "bar"))
$groovy.execute("filename.groovy", $dict)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e460a72-333c-46ee-b0de-6d1efa48f9c6"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><em>Absolute Path</em>: If the 'filename' is provided with an absolute path, Groovy Tool will read the Groovy file from an absolute location such as <strong>$groovy.execute('</strong><em>c:/mycode/readclass.groovy</em><strong>')</strong>.</li><li><em>Relative Path</em>: If the 'filename' is provided with a relative path, Groovy Tool will read the template from a relative location. This relative location starts from the current directory in which the template is located such as <strong>$groovy.execute('</strong><em>readclass.groovy</em><strong>')</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

###### **$group.create()**

Create a new instance of a group tool.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | GroupTool | An instance of a group tool. |

****

###### **$group.init()**

Initialize a group tool.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | void | - |

****

###### **$group.groupNames()**

Return a set of group names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.Set | A set of group names. |

****

###### **$group.contains(groupName)**

Test whether a group name is contained in a set of group names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | groupName | java.lang.String | A group name. |
| Return | - | - | True if the group name is contained in a set of group names, otherwise false. |

****

###### **$group.put(groupName, object)**

Add an object into a group.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | groupName | java.lang.String | A group name. |
|  | object | java.lang.Object | An object being added. |
| Return | - | void | - |

****

###### **$group.get(groupName)**

Return a list of group objects.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | groupName | java.lang.String | A group name. |
| Return | - | java.util.List | A list of group objects. |

###### ****

###### **$group.remove(groupName)**

Remove a group from the specified group name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | groupName | java.lang.String | A group name. |
| Return | - | java.util.List | A list of group objects previously associated with a specified group name, or null if there is no group corresponding to the key. |

****

###### **$group.removeAll()**

Remove all groups.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | void |  |

****

###### **$group.clear()**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714838 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00624: Grouping Tags inside Stereotype

- page_id: `136714838`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714838/Grouping+Tags+inside+Stereotype
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:51.988+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles > Creating Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When a stereotype has many properties, it is very useful to group these properties into named groups. For this purpose, every property could have a tagged value with its group name.

To group tags

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] of the stereotype property.
2. Click the **Tags** property group.
3. Select the **groupName** property and assign a value for it:
  1. Click the **Edit Value** button
  2. In the **Value** area, type a group name. . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_property_group.png']
4. Repeat these steps for every stereotype property.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Stereotypes' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

For this purpose, every property could have a tagged value with its group name. </span></p><p><span>To group tags</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the stereotype property.</span></li><li><span>Click the <strong>Tags</strong> property group.</span></li><li><span>Select the <strong>groupName</strong> property and assign a value for it:</span><ol><li><span>Click the <strong>Edit Value</strong> button</span></li><li><span>In the <strong>Value </strong> area, type a group name.</span><br /><br />.<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_property_group.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Grouping Tags inside Stereotype" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li><li>Repeat these steps for every stereotype property. </li></ol></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b14401e6-4bad-4948-8ab5-88b81d7034fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Applying Stereotypes" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Tag" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00625: Guard on decision

- page_id: `136712804`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712804/Guard+on+decision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:54.777+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

DecisionGuar

**Description**

This validation rule checks if a Flow from a Decision Node has a Guard specified.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

ActivityEdge

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure to specify a Guard for a Flow from a Decision Node.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Decision Node has two outgoing Object Flows, one of which has no Guard. The error is resolved by specifying the Guard for the second Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

