Th<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">e <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><em>init()</em></span> meth</span>od can add GUI components using the actions architecture or do other activities and return from the method. T<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">he <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><em>init()</em></span> met</span>hod is called only if <em>isSupported()</em> returns <em>true</em>. </li></ul><p>The following figure illustrates how do plugins <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="820d05a4-ab69-4d48-84c7-ec08aad275c6">work</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="How plugins work" ac:alt="How plugins work" ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="how_plugins_work.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be6237d0-46ae-44d8-ae47-134ddfc9ad1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Plugins directories" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709650 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00636: How to avoid triggering element recovery

- page_id: `136709650`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709650/How+to+avoid+triggering+element+recovery
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:03.147+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:

1. Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.
2. Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).
3. Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.
4. Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions

To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , set the Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects property value to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

inspection

To select the relevant level of project composition inspection

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , specify the Composition inspection option value:
  - Select Standard if you need the active validation to search for issues only in the project, but not in the projects it uses.
  - Select Advanced if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.
4. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:</p><ol><li>Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.</li><li>Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).</li><li>Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.</li><li>Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="avoid_recovery.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="How to avoid triggering element recovery" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions</h6><p>To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong> Composition Inspection</strong>, set the <strong>Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects </strong>property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="detect_illegal_model_references_in_shared_projects.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="How to avoid triggering element recovery" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb30e2f-6a0b-40da-97d1-2126e8b29b05"><ac:parameter ac:name="">inspection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To select the relevant level of project composition inspection</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong>Composition Inspection</strong>, specify the <strong>Composition inspection</strong> option value:<ul><li>Select <strong>Standard</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues <em>only</em> in the project, but not in the projects it uses.</li><li>Select <strong>Advanced</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715093 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00637: How to distribute resources

- page_id: `136715093`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715093/How+to+distribute+resources
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:05.063+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Distributing Resources
- labels: ['resource-builder', 'resource-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can distribute custom resources in the following two ways:

- Using Custom Resource Building Wizard :

1. Install the Development Tools plugin. Click Help > Resource/Plugin Manager , download the plugin and restart the program.
2. Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of the resource, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='MD2024x']. The structure must not contain the "data/resourcemanager" folders yet.
3. Start Resource Builder (**Tools** > **Development Tools** >******Build Custom Resource...**), select a *MagicDraw* folder structure you have constructed in the step #2 step.
4. Fill the properties in the provided form.
5. Select the desired resource (a ZIP archive) output file, and click **Finish**. Resource Builder will create the "data/resourcemanager" folder containing an XML file with the properties you provided and assemble everything inside the *MagicDraw* folder into the ZIP file you specified as the output file. The resource (ZIP) file you have created can be distributed to your team members and installed using the Resource/Plugin Manager.

- Distribute manually. The manual distribution consists of the following steps:

1. Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of resource, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Create a Resource descriptor file. For more information about the Resource descriptor file, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space='MD2024x'] .
3. Archive created files to a zip file. The zip file should include the required files, as well as folders that have a structure matching the structure of a modeling tool. Information about the folders structure for each type of resource and the general view of the file structure is depicted in the following figure: [ATTACHMENT filename='Structure_of_directories_and_files_that_could_be_distributed_through_ResourceManager.png']
4. Import your prepared data to your modeling tool through the Resource Manager. For more information about the MagicDraw Resource Manager, see*MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*.

Resource Manager supports zip archives only!

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can distribute custom resources in the following two ways:</p><ul><li>Using <strong>Custom Resource Building Wizard</strong>:</li></ul><ol><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Install the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin. Click <strong>Help </strong>&gt; <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>, download the plugin and restart the program.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of the resource, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60754bd1-08fe-4b22-8e1c-f3706d2cc6a8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The structure must not contain the &quot;data/resourcemanager&quot; folders yet.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Start Resource Builder (<strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Development Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> </strong><strong>Build Custom Resource...</strong>), select a <em>MagicDraw</em> folder structure you have constructed in the step #2 step.</p></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Fill the properties in the provided form.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Select the desired resource (a ZIP archive) output file, and click <strong>Finish</strong>. Resource Builder will create the &quot;data/resourcemanager&quot; folder containing an XML file with the properties you provided and assemble everything inside the <em>MagicDraw</em> folder into the ZIP file you specified as the output file. The resource (ZIP) file you have created can be distributed to your team members and installed using the Resource/Plugin Manager.</p></li></ol><ul><li>Distribute manually. The manual distribution consists of the following steps:</li></ul><ol><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of resource, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link>.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Create a Resource descriptor file. For more information about the Resource descriptor file, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Resource manager descriptor file" /></ac:link>.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Archive created files to a zip file. The zip file should include the required files, as well as folders that have a structure matching the structure of a modeling tool. Information about the folders structure for each type of resource and the general view of the file structure is depicted in the following figure:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structure_of_directories_and_files_that_could_be_distributed_through_ResourceManager.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="How to distribute resources" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><em><br /></em></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Import your prepared data to your modeling tool through the Resource Manager.<p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fe2b8d4-4f2f-4d32-8a11-ff2b796c6db8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>For more information about the MagicDraw Resource Manager, see </span><em>MagicDraw UserManual.pdf</em><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cf381e0-b667-4158-ada3-e128fab3c32d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Resource Manager supports zip archives only!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc9101d3-2965-4f05-a251-d4bbaa295297"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Resource manager descriptor file" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715298 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00638: How to provide a solution for a problem found during validation?

- page_id: `136715298`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715298/How+to+provide+a+solution+for+a+problem+found+during+validation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:50:34.130+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The validation rule returns annotations as validation results to the validation engine. Each annotation can contain a list of action objects that implements how a particular problem found by the validation rule can be solved. In order to create an action for solving, the validation rule’s provider must create a Java class that extends the*com.nomagic.actions.NMAction* class and implement the *actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)* method. A user will be able to invoke the action from the validation results table or from the browser. In order to enable performing the action on multiple targets, the action class must implement the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationAction* interface.

See *MyAction.java*, *FixJavaConstantNamesAction.java*, *MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java* and *JavaConstantNameValidationRuleImpl.java* examples in <program installation directory>\openapi\examples\validation.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation rules' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Annotating the elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The validation rule returns annotations as validation results to the validation engine. Each annotation can contain a list of action objects that implements how a particular problem found by the validation rule can be solved. In order to create an action for solving, the validation rule’s provider must create a Java class that extends the<em> com.nomagic.actions.NMAction</em> class and implement the <em>actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)</em> method. A user will be able to invoke the action from the validation results table or from the browser. In order to enable performing the action on multiple targets, the action class must implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationAction</em> interface.</p><p>See <em>MyAction.java</em>, <em>FixJavaConstantNamesAction.java</em>, <em>MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java</em> and <em>JavaConstantNameValidationRuleImpl.java</em> examples in &lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\validation.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e957a260-479d-4d40-9eea-ef16acd7c6f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Annotating the elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711809 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00639: HTML editor

- page_id: `136711809`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711809/HTML+editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:57.085+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

630043998

630044000

630043999

You can easily add or edit HTML text using the HTML editor. It also allows you to preserve the text format when copying formatted text.

To set the note, text box, or separator text as HTML

Do one of the following:

- Select a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MD2024x'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Text Box' space='MD2024x'] shape and click the HTML button when Switch To HTML Text appears on the lower-left corner of the shape. [ATTACHMENT filename='switch_text_to_html.png']

- Draw the note/text box, using the Note(HTML text) or Text Box(HTML text) buttons on the diagram palette.

Click the text area on the selected shape or start typing to open the HTML editor toolbar. The buttons on the HTML editor toolbar have the same functions as a normal editing toolbar.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar

You can write HTML text in various dialogs, but you must turn on the HTML mode first.

To turn on the HTML mode in a dialog

- Simply click to select the HTML check box. The following scenarios show the various ways of reaching the check box:
  - Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] :
    1. Open an element's specification window.
    2. Click the Documentation/Comments tab.
    3. Check HTML. Click Close . See the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### HTML editor toolbar in Documentation/Hyperlinks tab of element Specification window

- In the Model Browser:
  1. Click the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024x'] .
  2. Click the **Documentation** tab.
  3. Click **HTML**, then click anywhere on the diagram pane. See the figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] HTML editor toolbar in Documentation panel on Model Browser
- Element Specification window:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. Click the Tags property group, when editing a tagged value.
  3. Click HTML . Then click Close . See the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='customer_user_is_added.png'] HTML editor toolbar in Tags tab of element Specification window when editing tag value
- In the **To Do** field:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. In the dialog, click the**To Do** field to edit it.
  3. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to open the **To Do** text dialog.
  4. In the **To Do** text dialog, click **HTML**. Edit the text as you wish, then click **OK** and click **Close**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar in To Do dialog

You can also use the HTML editor toolbar when editing a tagged value directly on the element shape.

To use the HTML editor toolbar for editing a tagged value

1. Click the HTML button on the element shape to switch to the HTML mode.
2. On the element shape, click the tagged value. The HTML editor toolbar opens for editing the tagged value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

630043997

**Related Pages:**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711729 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00640: Hyperlinks in texts

- page_id: `136711729`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711729/Hyperlinks+in+texts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:52.195+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Descriptions of boxes and tabs

760290566

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Descriptions of boxes and tabs

760290568

1348070756

**On this page**

32

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Descriptions of boxes and tabs

760290567

#### Introduction

You can link text directly to internal or external resources by adding hyperlinks. This enables you to open a document, wizard, diagram, or other [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) by clicking the hyperlink.

#### Places to add hyperlink in text

You can add a hyperlink in the following places:

- In all elements of the text format (e.g. Note, Anchor, Comment).
- In all element property values of the text format (e.g. **To Do**, **Documentation**, **Body**, **Body** **and Language**, **Text**,**Min**,**Max**). You can insert a hyperlink only for LiteralString and OpaqueExpression [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MD2024x'] (e.g **Default Value** property, **Value** property, and **Argument** property values).
- In all element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] > Documentation/Hyperlinks property groups.

#### Adding hyperlinks in text

Before adding a hyperlink, select the text format, either HTML or Plain text. If you select:

- HTML text - you can add any type of hyperlink . >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024x']
- Plain text - you can add a hyperlink only to an element/symbol. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024x']

##### all typesAdding a hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type

To add a hyperlink in text to any [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes)

1. Select a place to add a hyperlink. [All places to add hyperlinks in text >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Hyperlinks+in+texts#Hyperlinksintexts-Placestoinserthyperlinkintext)
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or press Ctrl+K.
3. In the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog , select the tab according to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024x'] .
4. Specify the boxes and options in the selected tab. Descriptions of boxes and tabsPlease see the following for more in-depth information about the boxes and options on each tab: 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Address tab' space='MD2024x']**Address** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='File tab' space='MD2024x']**File** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Symbol tab' space='MD2024x']**Element/Symbol** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Linking model elements to OSLC resources' space='CDH2024x']**OSLC Link** tab.
5. Click **OK**.The hyperlink added in text becomes underlined and blue. For example, the figure below shows where the hyperlink on the text "Car" will open the Specification window of the *Car* Class. The hyperlinked text will be represented with icon and will be automatically updated after changes. [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Element/Symbol tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog where the text "Car" refers to the Car Class from the model. It is represented with an icon and will be automatically updated after changes.

##### elementAdding hyperlink in text to element/symbol

Two additional ways to add a hyperlink in text to an element/symbol include:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='MD2024x']

****

###### **drag**Adding hyperlink to element by dragging element on Note or Text box shape

This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful only when inserting a hyperlink to element on Note or Text box shapes.

To add a hyperlink to an element by dragging an element on a Note or Text box shape

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] , select one or more elements.
2. Drag the selected elements onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MD2024x'] or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Text Box' space='MD2024x'] shapes on the diagram pane. 
The hyperlink to the element Specification window is created on the Note or Text Box. The text become underlined and blue. The **Update Mode** and **Text to display** values are selected according default settings. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements' space='MD2024x']How to change the default settings of hyperlinks to model elements >>

###### **guards**Adding hyperlinks to elements on Guard expressions, Change Event expressions, and State Invariant

This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful when inserting hyperlinks to properties on:

- A Transition Guard in a State Machine diagram.
- A Flow Guard in an Activity diagram.
- A State Invariant in a Sequence diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Event Type' space='MD2024x']

To add a hyperlink on a guard expression, State Invariant, and Change Event

1. Select one of the following:
  - guard expression on Transition in State Machine diagram.
  - guard expression on Flow in Activity diagram.
  - State Invariant in Sequence diagram.
  - Change Event expression in State Machine diagram.
2. Press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+****Backspace. If you press Ctrl+K, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog_old' space='MD2024x']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog opens and you can insert a hyperlink to an appropriate property. Makesure the selected property is correct.
3. Select the property from the list. The list provides all parameters, structural members of behavior, and structural members of the contextual classifier of behavior. The hyperlinked property is added according to the default setting of the **Update Mode** and **Text to display** list boxes. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements' space='MD2024x']How to change the default settings of hyperlinks to model elements >> For example, in the figure below, the hyperlink to the *currentSpeed* Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the *currentSpeed* Property to *speed*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The hyperlink to the currentSpeed Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the currentSpeed Property to speed in the Model Browser.

760290565

**Related pages**

3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MD2024x']

ac:ref="573c55ab-6fca-4178-9071-2732cf056e88">wo additional ways</ac:inline-comment-marker> to add a hyperlink in text to an element/symbol include:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="drag"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[By dragging element on Note or Text box shape.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Hyperlinks in texts" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="guards"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[By pressing Ctrl+Space on Transition, Flow Guards, State Invariant, or Change Event expressions.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Hyperlinks in texts" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="confluence-link"><strong><br /></strong></p><h4 class="confluence-link"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd55f9d8-159b-4773-8126-7eba57c91c83"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Adding hyperlink to element by dragging element on Note or Text box shape</h4><p>This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful only when inserting a hyperlink to element on Note or Text box shapes.</p><p>To add a hyperlink to an element by dragging an element on a Note or Text box shape</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, select one or more elements.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag the selected elements onto the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Note" /></ac:link> or the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Text Box" /></ac:link> shapes on the diagram pane. <br />The hyperlink to the element Specification window is created on the Note or Text Box. The text become underlined and blue.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cffdb39f-bfe9-491f-9648-f676f1521eec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span class="current"><strong>Update Mode</strong> and <strong>Text to display</strong> values are selected according default settings</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements" /><ac:link-body>How to change the default settings of <span class="current">hyperlinks to model elements</span> &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4 class="with-breadcrumbs"><strong><span class="current"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2179cfa6-bd59-4956-ad24-230dc82cb51c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">guards</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></strong><span class="current">Adding hyperlinks to elements on Guard expressions, Change Event expressions, and State Invariant</span></h4><p>This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful when inserting hyperlinks to properties on:</p><ul><li>A Transition Guard in a State Machine diagram.</li><li>A Flow Guard in an Activity diagram.</li><li>A State Invariant in a Sequence diagram.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Assigning Event Type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change Event expression in a State Machine diagram.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add a hyperlink on a guard expression, State Invariant, and Change Event</p><hr /><ol><li>Select one of the following:<br /><ul><li>guard expression on Transition in State Machine diagram.</li><li>guard expression on Flow in Activity diagram.</li><li>State Invariant in Sequence diagram.</li><li>Change Event expression in State Machine diagram. </li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+<strong> </strong>Backspace.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57e731a0-9fbf-4ba6-94c2-467137bc7b40"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you press Ctrl+K, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Edit Hyperlink dialog_old" /><ac:link-body><strong>Edit Hyperlink</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens and you can insert a hyperlink to an appropriate property. Make<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> sure the selected property is correct.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the property from the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6f19df2-7086-4d4b-bd80-70b5a3f59e16"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The list provides all parameters, structural members of behavior, and structural members of the contextual classifier of behavior.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The hyperlinked property is added according to the default setting of the <span class="current"><strong>Update Mode</strong> and <strong>Text to display</strong> list boxes</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements" /><ac:link-body>How to change the default settings of <span class="current">hyperlinks to model elements</span> &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">For example, in the figure below, the hyperlink to the <em>currentSpeed</em> Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the <em>currentSpeed</em> Property to <em>speed</em>.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="updated_hyperlink_on_guard_expression.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Hyperlinks in texts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The hyperlink to the currentSpeed Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the currentSpeed Property to speed in the Model Browser.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28233b6d-3d9a-4514-b139-ee3cb7619bda"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">760290565</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="36cf85bd-3f90-4b7a-b409-d391719f1434"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Linking+model+elements+to+OSLC+resources" class="current">Linking model elements to OSLC resources</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+hyperlinks">Managing hyperlinks</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711734 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00641: Hyperlinks on elements

- page_id: `136711734`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711734/Hyperlinks+on+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:52.573+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

756999583

756999585

756999584

#### Introduction

Hyperlinked elements or diagrams allow you to quickly navigate through the model, to other resources, or between them. If an element or diagram refers to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks on elements' space='MD2024x'], you can access that content by double clicking an element or an entire diagram. You can add all [types of hyperlinks](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) on elements and diagrams.

#### Adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram

To add a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram

1. Select the element or diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the smart manipulator [IMAGE alt='' src='']**Go To** > **Manage Navigation**.
  - From the shortcut menu, choose **Go To**> **Manage Navigation**.
3. The **Specification** window opens. Choose the **Navigation/Hyperlinks** property group, and click the **Add** button.
4. In the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024x']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog, select a tab according to the [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) you want to insert.
5. Specify the boxes and options according to the selected tab. The meaning of boxes and options of each tab is described in the following pages: 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Address tab' space='MD2024x']**Address** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='File tab' space='MD2024x']**File** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Symbol tab' space='MD2024x']**Element/Symbol** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Linking model elements to OSLC resources' space='CDH2024x']**OSLC Link** tabThe **Text to display** box does not exist in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024x']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
6. Select the **Active** check box to activate the hyperlink. Only one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. You can also:
  - Click the **Open** button, to open the content of the hyperlink, e.g., a webpage or another external resource, a file on your filesystem, or an element/symbol.
  - Click the **Edit** button, to edit an existing hyperlink in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024x']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
  - Click the **Add** button, to add a new hyperlink in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='MD2024x']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
  - Click the **Remove** button, to delete the hyperlink. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **OK**. 
The hyperlink is added on the selected element or diagram. In the Model Browser, you will see the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hyperlinks/Go To icon on the element. On the diagram pane, on the symbol, you will see the icon representing the subject the hyperlink is added to. You can instantly open a hyperlink by double clicking an element or diagram.

756999582

**Related pages**

**3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']**

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715158 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00642: Identifying elements

- page_id: `136715158`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715158/Identifying+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:31:47.940+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Two methods provide an identifier of an *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*. Either method can be used depending on the situation. This section explains the difference between the two:

1. com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.base.ModelObject.getLocalID()
2. com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation.MDObject.getID()

The meaning of the returned identifiers depends on the project type.

###### Local (.mdzip) projects

Both methods return the same identifier, which is permanent and unique in the scope of the project. In most cases, this ID is unique among different projects, unless you create a copy of the project by copying the file or using the **Save As** command.

#### TIP: Examples

Examples

1. If a project A is used in projects B and C , then the elements of the project A have the same identifiers in both projects B and C .
2. If a project A is copied as a project A1 , and a project B uses the project A , using the project A1 additionally will not be allowed since IDs must remain unique in the scope of the project B .

###### Teamwork Cloud projects

If a local project is added to the Teamwork Cloud (or vice versa), *getLocalID()* consistently returns the same identifier value. Note that several elements with the same local ID may exist in the scope of a single Teamwork Cloud project.

*getID()* returns UUID, which is unique both in the project and in the scope of the whole Teamwork Cloud server repository.

The Teamwork Cloud imports (clones) the contents of used projects. This means copies of the elements are created inside the project. While **getLocalID()** returns the same value for different copies, **getID()** will return a unique value for each copy (project usage).

#### TIP: Example

Example

If a project *A* is copied as a project *A1*, and a Teamwork Cloud project *B* uses both *A* and *A1*, **getLocalID()** returns the same value for corresponding elements of *A* and *A1*, but **getID()** returns different identifiers for the same elements.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

Both identifiers can be used to retrieve the element using the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getElementByID(java.lang.String)*. Be aware that if several elements exist in the project by the provided local ID, only a single one of them is returned.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with UML model' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Either method can be used depending on the situation. This section explains the difference between the two:</p><ol><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.base.ModelObject.getLocalID()</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation.MDObject.getID()</em></li></ol><p>The meaning of the returned identifiers depends on the project type.</p><h4>Local (.mdzip) projects</h4><p>Both methods return the same identifier, which is permanent and unique in the scope of the project. In most cases, this ID is unique among different projects, unless you create a copy of the project by copying the file or using the <strong>Save As</strong> command.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f85048c-a7fe-416c-9fe4-09c7f255ba14"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Examples</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>If a project <em>A</em> is used in projects <em>B</em> and <em>C</em>, then the elements of the project <em>A</em> have the same identifiers in both projects <em>B</em> and <em>C</em>.</li><li>If a project <em>A</em> is copied as a project <em>A1</em>, and a project <em>B</em> uses the project <em>A</em>, using the project <em>A1</em> additionally will not be allowed since IDs must remain unique in the scope of the project <em>B</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Teamwork Cloud projects</h4><p>If a local project is added to the Teamwork Cloud (or vice versa), <em>getLocalID()</em> consistently returns the same identifier value. Note that several elements with the same local ID may exist in the scope of a single Teamwork Cloud project.</p><p><em>getID()</em> returns UUID, which is unique both in the project and in the scope of the whole Teamwork Cloud server repository.</p><p>The Teamwork Cloud imports (clones) the contents of used projects. While <em><em>getLocalID()</em></em> returns the same value for different copies, <em><em>getID()</em></em> will return a unique value for each copy (project usage).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2ebaf56-5344-4ab6-8041-7ccd46a590a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a project <em>A</em> is copied as a project <em>A1</em>, and a Teamwork Cloud project <em>B</em> uses both <em>A</em> and <em>A1</em>, <em><em>getLocalID()</em></em> returns the same value for corresponding elements of <em>A</em> and <em>A1</em>, but <em><em>getID()</em></em> returns different identifiers for the same elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fa22685-01cf-4ee1-a211-13fec2213938"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Both identifiers can be used to retrieve the element using the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getElementByID(java.lang.String)</em>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712715 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00643: Identifying Package Dependencies

- page_id: `136712715`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712715/Identifying+Package+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:48.196+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In a model, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x'] can be associated with other packages or model elements in a project scope or outside the project scope. In other words, packages can have relations with external elements in used projects, shared libraries, profiles, etc. An element depends on the used project when at least one of its metaproperties reference elements from at least one share of that used project. In this case, the element depends on that used project.

To avoid any violations on editing a package, especially on removing or exporting a package to another project, it is always good to know all of the package dependencies. Dependency analysis allows users to detect cyclic dependencies that impede reusing the package in the current or other projects because it can cause an infinite recursion. This analysis manages the impact of changes to other related elements.

In the modeling tool, the Dependency Checker allows you to analyze package dependencies in the whole project scope or analyze dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages. The dependency severity level status shows how a package depends on a particular element. There are three severity levels:

- Info . The lowest severity.
- Warning . Conflicts that require your attention are displayed.
- Error . The highest severity.

#### NOTE: Severity levels

Severity levels

While checking dependencies, conflicts of the selectedseverity (and higher) are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024x']**Package Dependencies** panel.

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x']**Project Options** dialog, you can define the default values for checking dependencies. You can specify the following:

- The Check for Cyclic Dependencies among Used Projects option. Click to select true if you want the cyclic dependencies to be checked while exporting or sharing the packages.
- The Dependency Checker Severity Level option. Specify the severity level. The dependencies of the specified severity (and higher) will be displayed.
- The Ignore Standard/System Profiles option. Click to select true if you want to exclude the standard/system profiles from checking for cyclic dependencies in used projects.

Each time you start the dependency checking, you can change the default settings.

In the following procedures, learn how to work with the Dependency Checker:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024x']

setDefaultTo set default values for the Dependency Checker

1. On the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Expand the General option in the option group list.
3. Click the **Dependency Checker** option. The list of options opens.
4. In the Dependency Checker options list, set the desired values and click **OK** when you are done.

#### NOTE: Description of an option

Description of an option

Each option has a short description. Click the option to see the description in the description area of the dialog.

analyzeWholeTo analyze package dependencies of the whole project or between the selected package/model and shared packages

1. Choose one of the following:
  - To analyze package dependencies of the whole project, on the main menu, click **Analyze** > **Dependency Checker**.
  - To analyze dependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages, right-click the package or model in the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, point to **Tools**and then click**Dependency Checker**.
2. The Dependency Checker dialog opens. Select the Minimal Severity from the list, check ignore or not standard and system profiles while checking and click OK to execute the dependency checker. If some dependencies are found, they are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024x']**Package Dependencies** panel .

analyzeExportTo identify package dependencies while exporting packages

1. From the package/model shortcut menu, choose Project Usages > Export Packages to New Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog appears.
2. Select the packages you want to export. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting packages to new projects' space='MD2024x'] .
3. Click OK .
4. In the question dialog, click **Yes**. NotesDependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages will be analyzed and shown in the opened[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024x']**Package Dependency** panel.If you want to discover *cyclic dependencies*, select the **Check for cyclic dependencies among used projects** check box. This enables for the dependencies which have **Error** and **Warning** severity levels to be displayed.

analyzeSharingTo identify package dependencies while sharing packages

1. From the package/model shortcut menu, choose Project Usages > Share Packages . The Shared Packages dialog appears.
2. Select the packages you want to share. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing project data' space='MD2024x'].
3. Click OK .
4. In the question dialog, click **Yes**. NotesDependencies between the selected package/model and shared packages will be analyzed and shown in the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024x']**Package Dependency** panel.If you want to discover *cyclic dependencies*, select the **Check for cyclic dependencies among used projects** check box. This enables for the dependencies which have**Error**and**Warning**severity levels to be displayed.

#### INFO: Cyclic dependency

Cyclic dependency

If there is a chain of dependencies such that A → M(1), M(1) → M(2), M(2) → M(3), ..., M(X) → M(A), where:

- A is an element from the used project M(A)
- M(1..X) are other used projects
- A → M(x) is element A dependency on used project M(x)
- M(y) → M(x) is a dependency of at least one element in used project M(y) on used project M(x),

then this chain is called a cyclic dependency and every atomic dependency in this chain is considered as part of cyclic dependency.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715075 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00644: Identifying plugin dependencies

- page_id: `136715075`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715075/Identifying+plugin+dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:03.672+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some Open API classes may come from plugins (like the SysML plugin, for example). In this case, make sure that *jar*files from a plugin directory are added to the compiled classpath. Also, your *plugin.xml* must define the required plugin too.

To find dependencies

- In the Main toolbar, click Tools > Development Tools > Find Plugin Dependency by Class Name...

The Development Tools menu item is available once the Development Tools Plugin is installed. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL2024x'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Some Open API classes may come from plugins (like the SysML plugin, for example). In this case, make sure that <em>jar </em>files from a plugin directory are added to the compiled classpath. Also, your <em>plugin.xml</em> must define the required plugin too.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To find dependencies</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Main toolbar, click <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Tools &gt; Development Tools &gt; Find Plugin Dependency by Class Name...<br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="715abddf-cc51-4d32-8a21-36295559c0a5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Development Tools menu item is available once the Development Tools Plugin is installed. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024x" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Installing plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715356 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00645: Idle job service

- page_id: `136715356`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715356/Idle+job+service
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:48:48.285+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Multi-threading
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.job.IdleJobService* allows to add *com.nomagic.magicdraw.job.Job(s)* which can be executed when a program is idle. The program decides when it is the most appropriate to execute the *Job*without getting the program into an inconsistent state. The example of usage:

```java

```

For more information please refer to *<installation_directory>**\openapi\examples\JobExample*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Multi-threading' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.job.IdleJobService</em> allows to add <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.job.Job(s)</em> which can be executed when a program is idle. The program decides when it is the most appropriate to execute the <em>Job </em>without getting the program into an inconsistent state. The example of usage:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32521763-ef0e-4a80-8c58-4ad91ed41973"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Job job = new Job()
{  
    public boolean needsExecute()
    {
        return true;
    }
   
    public void execute(ProgressStatus progressStatus) throws Exception
    {
        // Do necessary work in the session.
    }
  
    public void finished()
    {
        // Cleanup after job is finished.
    }
   
    public String getName()
    {
        return "My Job";
    }
};
  
IdleJobService.getInstance().addJob(job);
  
// Remove the job when it is no longer necessary.
IdleJobService.getInstance().removeJob(job);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information please refer to <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em></span></span><em style="line-height: 1.42857;">\openapi\examples\<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">JobExample</span></em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.</span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="294c0d2e-31e0-4a1a-9602-9d6874a96fa9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Multi-threading" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715301 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00646: IJavaNumberPart interface

- page_id: `136715301`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715301/IJavaNumberPart+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:21.639+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Custom elements numbering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The provided NumberParts for Numeric and Character implement the numbering based on the natural ordering of integers or the alphabetic ordering of strings. Examples for this would be P1.1, P1.2, P1.3 or P1.A, P1.B, P1.C, etc. What if we wanted to create numbers of the following kind: P1.1, P1.4, P1.9, P1.16, etc. with all intermediate numbers omitted?

The [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.IJavaNumberPart*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/autoid/IJavaNumberPart.html) interface provides a way to create a slice for a generated number that does not follow these natural sequences.

The parameter that the [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.IJavaNumberPart.generateNextId*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/autoid/IJavaNumberPart.html#generateNextId-java.lang.String-) method receives, namely *lastNumberPart*, is the value that this method is generated as a result in the previous call. If the method has not been called before this *lastNumberPart* value equals an empty string.

This method should adhere to the contract that equal inputs must produce equal outputs. If, for example, the input is ABC and the method computes a value of XYZ then the return value for ABC must always be XYZ.

The return value of this method will be incorporated by the Numbering Framework to create a complete number together with all other *NumberParts* that are part of the same *NumberingScheme*.

###### **Example**

Let's assume the *SquareScheme* is used as target for UML activities. According to this *NumberingScheme*, the first part of a number will be taken from a numbered parent (in this case, the package *sample* as shown in the following figure), then a dot is inserted and the final slice is generated by the *SquareNumberPart* expression.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

We have created a few activities inside the package named *sample*. The owning package is also customized and has a generated number P1. If we number the activities with this *NumberingScheme*, the *SquareNumberPart* class, being set as *part3*of the type expression, will create the following sequence: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25... These pieces are then added to the results from the other *NumberParts*, resulting in the activities being numbered as shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The source code for this simple implementation should be self-explaining. It will simply try to transform the string value of *lastNumberPart* into an integer value called *square*. Subsequently, the following mathematical operation is performed and returned [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

```java

```

Examples for this would be P1.1, P1.2, P1.3 or P1.A, P1.B, P1.C, etc. What if we wanted to create numbers of the following kind: P1.1, P1.4, P1.9, P1.16, etc. with all intermediate numbers omitted?</p><p>The <a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/autoid/IJavaNumberPart.html"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.IJavaNumberPart</em></a> interface provides a way to create a slice for a generated number that does not follow these natural sequences.</p><p>The parameter that the <a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/autoid/IJavaNumberPart.html#generateNextId-java.lang.String-"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.IJavaNumberPart.generateNextId</em></a> method receives, namely <em>lastNumberPart</em>, is the value that this method is generated as a result in the previous call. If the method has not been called before this <em>lastNumberPart</em> value equals an empty string.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1f4bc1f-60d7-49f8-b5e3-55d31acaae74"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This method should adhere to the contract that equal inputs must produce equal outputs. If, for example, the input is ABC and the method computes a value of XYZ then the return value for ABC must always be XYZ.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>The return value of this method will be incorporated by the Numbering Framework to create a complete number together with all other <em>NumberParts</em> that are part of the same <em>NumberingScheme</em>.</p><h4><strong>Example</strong> </h4><p>Let's assume the <em>SquareScheme</em> is used as target for UML activities. According to this <em>NumberingScheme</em>, the first part of a number will be taken from a numbered parent (in this case, the package <em>sample</em> as shown in the following figure), then a dot is inserted and the final slice is generated by the <em>SquareNumberPart</em> expression.</p><p><ac:image ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SquareScheme_numbering_scheme.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="IJavaNumberPart interface" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>We have created a few activities inside the package named <em>sample</em>. The owning package is also customized and has a generated number P1. If we number the activities with this <em>NumberingScheme</em>, the <em>SquareNumberPart</em> class, being set as <em>part3 </em>of the type expression, will create the following sequence: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25... These pieces are then added to the results from the other <em>NumberParts</em>, resulting in the activities being numbered as shown in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="numbering.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="IJavaNumberPart interface" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><div class="WordSection1"><p> </p><p>The source code for this simple implementation should be self-explaining. It will simply try to transform the string value of <em>lastNumberPart</em> into an integer value called <em>square</em>. Subsequently, the following mathematical <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">operation is performed and returned <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="formula.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="IJavaNumberPart interface" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="680f947d-9029-4df1-82a6-91f43cc6647f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public class SquareNumberPart implements IJavaNumberPart 
````html
	{
        private final int initialValue = 1;
        @Override
        public String generateNextId(String lastNumberPart) 
		{
            int nextSquare = initialValue;
            try 
			{
                if ("".equals(lastNumberPart))
				{
                    lastNumberPart = "0";
                }
                int square = Integer.valueOf(lastNumberPart);
                int number = (int) Math.sqrt(square) + 1;
                nextSquare = (int) Math.pow(number, 2);
            } 
            catch (NumberFormatException ex) 
			{
                // already set nextSquare as initialValue
            }
            return Integer.toString(nextSquare);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709281 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00647: Image Library tab

- page_id: `136709281`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709281/Image+Library+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:40.603+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started > Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Image formats

INLINE

The**Image Library** tab displays images grouped in logical sets. You can use a bundled image collection or add your own set of images.

To open the **Image Library** tab

- From the Window menu, select Image Library. The Image Library tab opens on the right side of the window.

To add your own set of images into **Image Library**, do either

- Compress a folder (.zip) with a set of desired images and place it in the MagicDraw installationdirectory: *\data\imagelibraries\commonsymbols*. Image formatsYou can add images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.
- Create an Image Library plugin. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Writing plugins' space='MD2024x'] page.

Using the **Image Library** tab, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying image' space='MD2024x'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Image Library** tab consist of three components:

##### Quick filter

You can search for images in the **Whole hierarchy** or **Recently used** tabs using one of the following way:

- Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.
- Pattern Search: type * to find any image. For example, type "*Bank" if you want to find "Bank" somewhere in the hierarchy.

##### Image Library tree

You can drag an image from the **Image Library** tree directly:

- On the symbol (image is applied for symbol).
- To the diagram pane (image shape is created).

##### Image Library Preview area

You can preview the image before applying it on the symbol in the **Image Library** Preview area.

## PAGE 00648: Image Shape

- page_id: `136711445`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711445/Image+Shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:40.871+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

490233696

490233698

490233697

The image shape provides a simple and quick way to insert a image into a diagram. Images may include logos, graphs, tables, or others. The default shape size after the insertion is the actual image size.

You can format image shape representation properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties**dialog.

To insert an image on the diagram pane

1. On the diagram palette, in the Common category, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 12.58.27 PM.png'] Image Shape button or press the I key.
2. Click on the diagram pane. The Open dialog opens.
3. Browse for an image in the *.gif, *.jpg, *.jpeg, *.svg, or *.png format and click Open .

490233695

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714027 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00649: Image tool

- page_id: `136714027`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714027/Image+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:02.149+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1267909359

1267909360

1267909360

$image provides a rich set of image manipulation methods that enable you to transform images during report generation. With **$image**, you can scale, rotate, split, and resize images, include external images, and export them for TXT and HTML templates.

**Syntax**

These manipulation methods can be broadly divided into seven categories:

1. Scaling images
2. Rotating images
3. Splitting images
4. Resizing fixed-pixel images
5. Resizing fixed-measurement images
6. Including external images
7. Exporting images for TXT and HTML templates
8. Limiting image size

Multiple image transformations will have cumulative effects. Desired sizes can be specified either as pixel counts or as measurements in inches, centimeters, or millimeters. While the parameters for resizing an image with a specific measurement (in, cm, mm, pt, or px) must be specified in a pair of quote marks, all other size parameters do not need to be specified in a pair of quote marks.

The **keepRatio** parameter takes either a true or false value; a true value will resize the image and keep the original image height/width ratio; a false value will only resize one axis, as set by the method name, resulting in a stretched image.

1267909358

**Related pages**

Desired sizes can be specified either as pixel counts or as measurements in inches, centimeters, or millimeters. ### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ImplOp

**Description**

This validation rule checks if all operations of realized interfaces are implemented.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Class

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure all operations of realized interfaces are implemented.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The realized interface *Air Control*contains the *CoolAir*operation that is not implemented. The error is resolved by implementing the operation.
