<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715295 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00651: Implementing a binary validation rule

- page_id: `136715295`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715295/Implementing+a+binary+validation+rule
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:27:16.402+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### **caseAImplementing Custom Validation Behaviour**

If you want to control when the validation rule has to be executed and you want to control what must be validated and how, then you have to create a Java class and implement the interface *com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.ValidationRule*. The class must have the public *no-arg* constructor. A value of the implementation property of the validation rule must be a qualified name of the implemented class.

This case is not recommended for validating model elements because the validation rule provider must implement not only validating of model elements but also other details that are provided by the modeling tool validation engine. In this case, the validation rule provider must correctly implement validating of model elements that are from the validation scope defined in project options, should take care that implementation would respect the project property *Exclude element from used read-only project*, and is responsible for implementing when the validation has to be executed.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation rules' space='']Creating validation rules

**caseBImplementing Rule to Validate Model Elements**

If you want to concentrate on implementation of validating model elements and delegate a task of detecting model element changes and project property changes to the validation engine then you have to create a Java class and implement***com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.ElementValidationRuleImpl* and the interface. The class must have the public *no-arg* constructor. The value of the implementation property of the validation rule must be a qualified name of the implemented class. Note that If this rule is used in active validation, then it will be repeatedly executed after every single change in the model.

**This example shows how to add custom solver action and set dynamic error message to validated element:**

```java
run(Project project, Constraint constraint, Collection extends Element> elements)
{
   Set result = new HashSet<>();

   for (Element element : elements)
   {
      if (!isValid(element))
      {
         List actions = new ArrayList<>();
         actions.add(new MyAction("MY_ACTION_ID", "Test", null));
         result.add(new Annotation(element, constraint, getErrorText(element), actions));
      }
   }

   return result;
}

private static String getErrorText(Element element)
{
   return "Name for [" + element.getID() + "] should be longer than " + MIN_NAME_LENGTH + " symbols.";
}

private static boolean isValid(Element element)
{
   NamedElement el = (NamedElement) element;
   return el.getName().length() > MIN_NAME_LENGTH;
}]]>
```

**#caseCOptimizing Rule Execution for Active Validation**

If the validation rule is designed for the active validation then the validation performance becomes very important. In this case we want that the validation rule would be executed only when it is actually required, so the implementation of the validation rule must somehow inform the active validation engine which model element property changes are important to the validation rule. In order to create such validation rule you have implement *ElementValidationRuleImpl* and *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfigProvider* interfaces. The class must have the public *no-arg* constructor. The value of the implementation property of the validation rule must be a qualified name of the implemented class.

The *SmartListenerConfigProvider* interface defines a single method:

```java
, Collection> getListenerConfigurations()]]>
```

The implementation of this method should return a map of classes derived from the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* class to collection(s) of *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfig* objects. Another way would be to extend the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.DefaultValidationRuleImpl* class and override the following method:

```java
, Collection> getListenerConfigurations()]]>
```

In the Constraint Specification dialog box, the Constrained Element property should be specified. A constrained element is the element for which the validation rule is created.

###### Example of listener configuration for the validation rule that is interested in name changes of Class and Interface model elements:

```java
, Collection> getListenerConfigurations() 
    {
        Map, Collection> configMap = 
					new HashMap, Collection>();
        Collection configs = new ArrayList();
        configs.add(SmartListenerConfig.NAME_CONFIG);
        configMap.put(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class.class, configs);
        configMap.put(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdinterfaces.Interface.class, configs); 
        return configMap;
    }]]>
```

```text

```

For the given example, In the specification dialog of the **Constraint**element the Constrained Element property should be set to Class and Interface metaclasses from the UML Standard Profile.

###### Example of listener configuration for the validation rule that is interested in Activity parameters and Activity parameter node changes

```java
, Collection> getListenerConfigurations() 
    {
        Map, Collection> configMap = 
					new HashMap, Collection>();
        Collection configs = new ArrayList();
        SmartListenerConfig parameterConfig = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        Collection parameterPropertiesList = new ArrayList();
        parameterPropertiesList.add(PropertyNames.DIRECTION);
        parameterPropertiesList.add(PropertyNames.TYPE); 
        parameterPropertiesList.add(PropertyNames.OWNED_TYPE);
        parameterConfig.listenToNested(PropertyNames.OWNED_PARAMETER).listenTo(parameterPr opertiesList);
        SmartListenerConfig cfg = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        Collection argumentCftList = new ArrayList(); 
        argumentCftList.add(PropertyNames.PARAMETER); 
        argumentCftList.add(PropertyNames.TYPE); 
        argumentCftList.add(PropertyNames.OWNED_TYPE); 
        cfg.listenTo(argumentCftList);
        SmartListenerConfig argumentConfig = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        argumentConfig.listenTo(PropertyNames.NODE, cfg); 
        configs.add(parameterConfig);
        configs.add(argumentConfig); configMap.put(Activity.class, configs); return configMap;
    }]]>
```

See the *JavaConstantNameValidationRuleImpl.java* example in *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\validation*.

**caseDCase D**

If you want to create a validation rule that will not be used in the active validation and you do not need to provide solving actions then you can create a Java class that contains the static method with the signature:

```java
( param)]]>
```

and specify *<qualifiedClassName>.<method_name>* as a body value of the validation rule’s specification.

The referred java method must be a static method and it must take exactly one parameter. Several validating methods can be placed into the one Java class or use the one class per validating method – this is irrelevant.

The type of the parameter MUST match the type of the constrained element of the validation rule. For validation rules on metaclasses, use the appropriate class from the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.** package. For example, to write the rule for the metaclass *DataType*, use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.DataType*as a type of the parameter.

For the validation rules on stereotypes, use the same class as you would use when specifying the validation rule for a metaclass of that stereotype. For validation rules on the classifiers of the model, use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.InstanceSpecification* as the type of the parameter.

The Java code example of a trivial validation rule, which always returns *true* (that is, all elements are valid):

```java

```

Compile such code into the Java bytecode, locate it where the modeling tool can load it (a classpath, plugin), and then you can use it for the validation:

1. Create the validation rule in our model.
2. Select the DataType metaclass as the constrained element of the rule.
3. Select the Binary language for the expression of the rule.
4. Specify com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.TestRule.testMeta as the body of the expression of the rule.

Run the validation suite with the validation rule included. The method will be invoked for each datatype model element that is found in the scope of the validation. For each element, where this method returns *false*, an entry will be placed in the validation results table.

**caseEImplementing Rule to Validate Presentation Elements (Symbols)**

In case you need a validation rule that would check and annotate individual presentation elements (symbols) in diagrams, then a Java class extending *com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.DiagramValidator*should be implemented. This rule can be used for example to annotate symbols with overlapping bounds, or to detect multiple representations of the same model element in the same diagram in case only a single representation is desired.

Instead of receiving model element to validate as a parameter, use *DiagramValidator.getDiagrams()***method to get diagrams that are in the currently validated scope. To annotate individual symbols, create *com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.Annotation* object with the target being the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement* which does not pass your validation conditions.

Extend a subclass *com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.OpenedDiagramValidator* instead for your rule to always restrict the validated diagrams to the currently opened ones only.

Also see: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation elements (symbols)' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h4><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5580d3f5-bfb5-4bae-8ed3-82536da3b52d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">caseA</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Implementing Custom Validation Behaviour</strong></h4><p>If you want to control when the validation rule has to be executed and you want to control what must be validated and how, then you have to create a Java class and implement the interface <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.ValidationRule</em>. The class must have the public <em>no-arg</em> constructor. A value of the implementation property of the validation rule must be a qualified name of the implemented class.</p><p>This case is not recommended for validating model elements because the validation rule provider must implement not only validating of model elements but also other details that are provided by the modeling tool validation engine. In this case, the validation rule provider must correctly implement validating of model elements that are from the validation scope defined in project options, should take care that implementation would respect the project property <em>Exclude element from used read-only project</em>, and is responsible for implementing when the validation has to be executed.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating validation rules" /><ac:link-body>Creating validation rules<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb77a568-eb82-40f9-95dc-db4b6f8a809b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">caseB</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Implementing Rule to Validate Model Elements</strong></p><p>If you want to concentrate on implementation of validating model elements and delegate a task of detecting model element changes and project property changes to the validation engine then you have to create a Java class and implement<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.ElementValidationRuleImpl</em> and the interface. The class must have the public <em>no-arg</em> constructor. The value of the implementation property of the validation rule must be a qualified name of the implemented class. Note that If this rule is used in active validation, then it will be repeatedly executed after every single change in the model.</p><p><strong>This example shows how to add custom solver action and set dynamic error message to validated element:</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7aed81cf-d2d6-4167-b69b-4e69eab0505b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[public Set<Annotation> run(Project project, Constraint constraint, Collection<? extends Element> elements)
{
   Set<Annotation> result = new HashSet<>();

   for (Element element : elements)
   {
      if (!isValid(element))
      {
         List<NMAction> actions = new ArrayList<>();
         actions.add(new MyAction("MY_ACTION_ID", "Test", null));
         result.add(new Annotation(element, constraint, getErrorText(element), actions));
      }
   }

   return result;
}

private static String getErrorText(Element element)
{
   return "Name for [" + element.getID() + "] should be longer than " + MIN_NAME_LENGTH + " symbols.";
}

private static boolean isValid(Element element)
{
   NamedElement el = (NamedElement) element;
   return el.getName().length() > MIN_NAME_LENGTH;
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e85a76b1-a50f-46c3-a592-4941711c22e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#caseC</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Optimizing Rule Execution for Active Validation</strong></p><div class="WordSection1"><p>If the validation rule is designed for the active validation then the validation performance becomes very important. In this case we want that the validation rule would be executed only when it is actually required, so the implementation of the validation rule must somehow inform the active validation engine which model element property changes are important to the validation rule. In order to create such validation rule you have implement <em>ElementValidationRuleImpl</em> and <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfigProvider</em> interfaces. The class must have the public <em>no-arg</em> constructor. The value of the implementation property of the validation rule must be a qualified name of the implemented class.<span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span></p></div><p class="WordSection2">The <em>SmartListenerConfigProvider</em> interface defines a single method: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="521b89c3-4f25-430e-b4c6-a07d18537029"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> getListenerConfigurations()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The implementation of this method should return a map of classes derived from the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> class to collection(s) of <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfig</em> objects. Another way would be to extend the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.DefaultValidationRuleImpl</em> class and override the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f1076f7-888a-45c4-baac-1b9ccc069579"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> getListenerConfigurations()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33575394-8def-4bca-a913-5f3530863e7b"><ac:rich-text-body> In the Constraint Specification dialog box, the Constrained Element property should be specified. A constrained element is the element for which the validation rule is created.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4 class="WordSection2">Example of listener configuration for the validation rule that is interested in name changes of Class and Interface model elements:</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="beb5e6b7-88be-4073-bbcc-909673e29699"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> getListenerConfigurations() 
    {
        Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> configMap = 
					new HashMap<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>>();
        Collection<SmartListenerConfig> configs = new ArrayList<SmartListenerConfig>();
        configs.add(SmartListenerConfig.NAME_CONFIG);
        configMap.put(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class.class, configs);
        configMap.put(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdinterfaces.Interface.class, configs); 
        return configMap;
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><pre class="WordSection2">    </pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2379330-6d2b-448d-a2ff-88d13b560d76"><ac:rich-text-body><p> For the given example, In the specification dialog of the <strong>Constraint</strong><span> element</span> the Constrained Element property should be set to Class and Interface metaclasses from the UML Standard Profile.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 class="WordSection2">Example of listener configuration for the validation rule that is interested in Activity parameters and Activity parameter node changes</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd7897dd-18c0-4057-b4cc-0d0e445ade85"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> getListenerConfigurations() 
    {
        Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> configMap = 
					new HashMap<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>>();
        Collection<SmartListenerConfig> configs = new ArrayList<SmartListenerConfig>();
        SmartListenerConfig parameterConfig = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        Collection<String> parameterPropertiesList = new ArrayList<String>();
        parameterPropertiesList.add(PropertyNames.DIRECTION);
        parameterPropertiesList.add(PropertyNames.TYPE); 
        parameterPropertiesList.add(PropertyNames.OWNED_TYPE);
        parameterConfig.listenToNested(PropertyNames.OWNED_PARAMETER).listenTo(parameterPr opertiesList);
        SmartListenerConfig cfg = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        Collection<String> argumentCftList = new ArrayList<String>(); 
        argumentCftList.add(PropertyNames.PARAMETER); 
        argumentCftList.add(PropertyNames.TYPE); 
        argumentCftList.add(PropertyNames.OWNED_TYPE); 
        cfg.listenTo(argumentCftList);
        SmartListenerConfig argumentConfig = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        argumentConfig.listenTo(PropertyNames.NODE, cfg); 
        configs.add(parameterConfig);
        configs.add(argumentConfig); configMap.put(Activity.class, configs); return configMap;
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>See the <em>JavaConstantNameValidationRuleImpl.java</em> example in <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\validation</em>.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f68aad8a-c7b0-4779-b4fc-d5970a540991"><ac:parameter ac:name="">caseD</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Case D</strong></p><div class="WordSection1"><p>If you want to create a validation rule that will not be used in the active validation and you do not need to provide solving actions then you can create a Java class that contains the static method with the signature:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b8a2590-6e2c-469b-85a2-0c43354621e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     public static Boolean <method_name>(<Element_type> param)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>and specify <em>&lt;qualifiedClassName&gt;.&lt;method_name&gt;</em> as a body value of the validation rule’s specification.</p><p>The referred java method must be a static method and it must take exactly one parameter. Several validating methods can be placed into the one Java class or use the one class per validating method – this is irrelevant.</p><p>The type of the parameter MUST match the type of the constrained element of the validation rule. For validation rules on metaclasses, use the appropriate class from the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.*</em> package. For example, to write the rule for the metaclass <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">DataType</em>, use <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.DataType </em>as a type of the parameter.</p><p>For the validation rules on stereotypes, use the same class as you would use when specifying the validation rule for a metaclass of that stereotype. For validation rules on the classifiers of the model, use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.InstanceSpecification</em> as the type of the parameter.</p><p>The Java code example of a trivial validation rule, which always returns <em>true</em> (that is, all elements are valid): </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="773b3298-5052-4e15-9057-0864b8eba6de"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    package com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation;
    import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.DataType;
    public class TestRule
    {
        public static Boolean testMeta(DataType exp)
        {
            return Boolean.TRUE;
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>Compile such code into the Java bytecode, locate it where the modeling tool can load it (a classpath, plugin), and then you can use it for the validation:</p><ol><li>Create the validation rule in our model.</li><li>Select the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">DataType </em>metaclass as the constrained element of the rule.</li><li>Select the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Binary </em>language for the expression of the rule.</li><li>Specify <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.TestRule.testMeta</em> as the body of the expression of the rule.</li></ol><p>Run the validation suite with the validation rule included. The method will be invoked for each datatype model element that is found in the scope of the validation. For each element, where this method returns <em>false</em>, an entry will be placed in the validation results table.</p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7aa8c9be-cb72-4fc2-bb40-5bef532a7640"><ac:parameter ac:name="">caseE</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Implementing Rule to Validate Presentation Elements (Symbols)</strong></p><p>In case you need a validation rule that would check and annotate individual presentation elements (symbols) in diagrams, then a Java class extending <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.DiagramValidator </em>should be implemented. This rule can be used for example to annotate symbols with overlapping bounds, or to detect multiple representations of the same model element in the same diagram in case only a single representation is desired.</p><p>Instead of receiving model element to validate as a parameter, use <em>DiagramValidator.getDiagrams()</em><em> </em>method to get diagrams that are in the currently validated scope. To annotate individual symbols, create <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.Annotation</em> object with the target being the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement</em> which does not pass your validation conditions.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24e45205-b748-4ae8-a1a6-bbb8f83a4cf2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Extend a subclass <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.OpenedDiagramValidator</em> instead for your rule to always restrict the validated diagrams to the currently opened ones only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Also see: <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Presentation elements (symbols)" /></ac:link> </p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715331 space=MD2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00652: Implementing command line launchers

- page_id: `136715331`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715331/Implementing+command+line+launchers
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-06-12T11:30:26.158+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Running programs in batch mode
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

While launching the command line action, the single sign-on connection to the server is not supported.

Implementation of command line launchers depends on the type of the batch mode program (Core related or Plugin related).

Both modes provide two ways of implementation:

- CommandLine and CommandLineAction (for Core and Plugin respectively) are general launchers meant for developers that will not be opening projects or needs full control of argument parsing and opening of projects.
- ProjectCommandLine and ProjectCommandLineAction (for Core and Plugin respectively) are convenience launchers simplifying work with projects as they provide list of arguments that will be parsed and used to open one or more projects removing great deal of boilerplate code needed.

To create command line launcher:

1. Core related batch program:
  - For general command line extend `com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine`.
    - Override com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#parseArgs to parse your arguments
    - Override com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#execute to execute your action
  - For project command line action extend com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLine
    - Override com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLine#parseArguments to parse custom arguments you might be using
    - Override com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLine#execute(java.util.Properties, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project) to execute your action. This method will be called for every project opened using available project command line arguments.
2. Plugin related batch program:
  - For general command line implement com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction
    - Override com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction#execute to parse your arguments and do your action
  - For project command line action extend `com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLineAction`
    - Override `com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLineAction#execute(java.lang.String[], java.util.Properties, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project)` to execute your action. This method will be called for every project opened using available project command line arguments. Full array of originally passed arguments and parsed project command line arguments specific to the project (default arguments that were parsed from the command line or project-specific properties file) are also passed along with opened project.

Available parameters for project command line launcher:

- project - Project name or path
- projectDescriptor - Project descriptor. For a local project, it is a file URI in the format file:/ *<full encoded path>*, for example file:/C:/Users/Public/Documents/activity%20diagram.mdzip . For Teamwork Cloud or **3D**EXPERIENCE project, it is URI in format *twcloud:/<project resource ID>/<project branch ID>*, for example *[twcloud:/a8002c51-5e57-420e-9832-a0a54f600028/77cad478-cd51-4e4d-8666-b74a6ba95f9a](http://twcloud/a8002c51-5e57-420e-9832-a0a54f600028/77cad478-cd51-4e4d-8666-b74a6ba95f9a)*. The descriptor of an already opened project can be found and copied from the menu **File**> **Project Properties**, or created manually.
- server - Server URL. For **3D**EXPERIENCE, it is the URL of the server. For Teamwork Cloud, it can be only the name of the server or *name:port*, where the name is a host name, or full host name with domain, or IP address of the server, for example, *myserver1*, *myserver1:3579*, *[myserver1.mydomain.com](http://myserver1.mydomain.com)*, *[myserver1.mydomain.com](http://myserver1.mydomain.com):3579, 192.168.42.42, 192.168.42.42:3579*.
- username - Username on server
- password - Password for the provided username
  - By default provided password should be encrypted. ( 3D EXPERIENCE projects accept only plain text passwords)
    - To generate an encrypted password, run any command line launcher with a single argument generateServerPassword=yourPassword or generateServerPassword without a password for the interactive prompt.
  - In order to use the password in plain text form argument encryptPassword needs to be provided and set to true . (Not needed for 3D EXPERIENCE projects)
- serverType - If launching a project stored in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform, must specify the platform deployment type
  - OnPremise deployment serverType=PLATFORM_ON_PREMISE
  - Cloud deployment serverType=PLATFORM_CLOUD
- enableSSL - To use SSL
- encryptPassword - Set to true if the provided password is in plain text (Not needed for 3D EXPERIENCE projects)
- properties - Path to a properties file containing properties. Multiple properties files can be provided "properties=prop1.properties;prop2.properties"
- projectPassword - Project password
- version - Project version
- branch - Project branch

All parameters for project command line launchers, both in the command line and properties files, should be provided in the format "`property1=value1 property2=value2`".

To open multiple projects, you must define properties for each project (in case the project is local, path or name is enough) in separate properties files. These files are passed as "properties" arguments e.g. "properties=prop1.properties;prop2.properties".

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45bd0239-7c24-431c-8c8f-c0add76b666b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">While launching the command line action, the single sign-on connection to the server is not supported.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Implementation of command line launchers depends on the type of the batch mode program (Core related or Plugin related).</p><p>Both modes provide two ways of implementation:</p><ul><li><code>CommandLine</code> and <code>CommandLineAction</code> (for Core and Plugin respectively) are general launchers meant for developers that will not be opening projects or needs full control of argument parsing and opening of projects.</li><li><code>ProjectCommandLine</code> and <code>ProjectCommandLineAction</code> (for Core and Plugin respectively) are convenience launchers simplifying work with projects as they provide list of arguments that will be parsed and used to open one or more projects removing great deal of boilerplate code needed.</li></ul><p>To create command line launcher:</p><ol><li>Core related batch program:<ul><li><p>For general command line extend <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine</code>.</p><ul><li>Override <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#parseArgs</code> to parse your arguments</li><li>Override <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#execute</code> to execute your action</li></ul></li><li>For project command line action extend <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLine</code><ul><li>Override <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLine#parseArguments</code> to parse custom arguments you might be using</li><li>Override <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLine#execute(java.util.Properties, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project)</code> to execute your action. This method will be called for every project opened using available project command line arguments.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Plugin related batch program:<ul><li>For general command line implement <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction</code><ul><li>Override <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction#execute</code> to parse your arguments and do your action</li></ul></li><li><p>For project command line action extend <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLineAction</code></p><ul><li><p>Override <code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.ProjectCommandLineAction#execute(java.lang.String[], java.util.Properties, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project)</code> to execute your action. This method will be called for every project opened using available project command line arguments. Full array of originally passed arguments and parsed project command line arguments specific to the project (default arguments that were parsed from the command line or project-specific properties file) are also passed along with opened project.</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ol><p>Available parameters for project command line launcher:</p><ul><li><code>project</code> - Project name or path</li><li data-uuid="57d84826-f91f-4401-9040-5bdfb7cdb492"><code>projectDescriptor</code> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Project descriptor. For a local project, it is a file URI in the format<span> </span></span><em>file:/</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>&lt;full encoded path&gt;</em>, for example<span> </span></span><em>file:/C:/Users/Public/Documents/activity%20diagram.mdzip</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. For Teamwork Cloud or <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE project, it is URI in format <em>twcloud:/&lt;project resource ID&gt;/&lt;project branch ID&gt;</em>, for example <em><a href="http://twcloud/a8002c51-5e57-420e-9832-a0a54f600028/77cad478-cd51-4e4d-8666-b74a6ba95f9a">twcloud:/a8002c51-5e57-420e-9832-a0a54f600028/77cad478-cd51-4e4d-8666-b74a6ba95f9a</a></em>. The descriptor of an already opened project can be found and copied from the menu <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Properties</strong>, or created manually.</span></li><li data-uuid="20b38fde-3783-4294-9d60-7fb3291d97f4"><code>server</code><span> </span>- <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Server URL. For <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE, it is the URL of the server. For Teamwork Cloud, it can be only the name of the server or <em>name:port</em>, where the name is a host name, or full host name with domain, or IP address of the server, for example, <em>myserver1</em>, <em>myserver1:3579</em>, <em><a href="http://myserver1.mydomain.com">myserver1.mydomain.com</a></em>, <em><a href="http://myserver1.mydomain.com">myserver1.mydomain.com</a>:3579, 192.168.42.42, 192.168.42.42:3579</em>.</span></li><li><code>username</code> - Username on server</li><li><code>password</code> - Password for the provided username<ul><li>By default provided password should be encrypted.  (<strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE projects accept only plain text passwords)<br /><ul><li>To generate an encrypted password, run any command line launcher with a single argument <code>generateServerPassword=yourPassword</code> or <code>generateServerPassword</code> without a password for the interactive prompt.</li></ul></li><li>In order to use the password in plain text form argument <code>encryptPassword</code> needs to be provided and set to <code>true</code>.  (Not needed for <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE projects)</li></ul></li><li><code>serverType</code> - If launching a project stored in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, must specify the platform deployment type<ul><li>OnPremise deployment <code>serverType=PLATFORM_ON_PREMISE</code></li><li>Cloud deployment <code>serverType=PLATFORM_CLOUD</code></li></ul></li><li><code>enableSSL</code> - To use SSL</li><li><code>encryptPassword</code> - Set to true if the provided password is in plain text (Not needed for <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE projects)</li><li><code>properties</code> - Path to a properties file containing properties. Multiple properties files can be provided &quot;properties=prop1.properties;prop2.properties&quot;</li><li><code>projectPassword</code> - Project password</li><li><code>version</code> - Project version</li><li><code>branch</code> - Project branch</li></ul><p>All parameters for project command line launchers, both in the command line and properties files, should be provided in the format &quot;<code>property1=value1 property2=value2</code>&quot;.</p><p>To open multiple projects, you must define properties for each project (in case the project is local, path or name is enough) in separate properties files. These files are passed as &quot;properties&quot; arguments e.g. &quot;properties=prop1.properties;prop2.properties&quot;.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712078 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00653: Implementing or Overriding Operations

- page_id: `136712078`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712078/Implementing+or+Overriding+Operations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:07.982+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Shortcut Key

356006158

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Shortcut Key

356006160

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Shortcut Key

356006159

#### NOTE: Modeling Tools

Modeling Tools

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect and Enterprise editions only.

When you inherit Classes from the base Class that has abstract functions, you have to redefine them in the inherited Classes. The implement/override operations tool will help you generate operations that are defined as abstract in the base Class.

The**Implement/Override Operations**command can be invoked in two cases:

- When one classifier inherits operations from the base classifier ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case study #1: Adding a rule to create a Generalization relationship on drag-and-drop' space='MD2024x'] ).
- When some classifiers implement an Interface ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forward traceability - realization' space='MD2024x'] ).

To start the Implement/Override Operations****tool

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH2024x'] of the selected Class, select Tools > Code Engineering .
2. Select Implement/Override Operations.
3. The Select Operations to Implement/Override dialog box opens.

| Box | Function |
| --- | --- |
| All | Contains names of all operations defined within the selected class. |
| Selected | Contains the selected operations. |
| > | Moves the selected operation from the All list to the Selected list. |
| < | Moves selected operation from the Selected list to the All list. |
| >> | Moves all operations from the All list to the Selected list. |
| << | Moves all operations from the Selected to the All list. |
| OK | Generates operations that are in the Selected list. |
| Cancel | Exits the dialog box without any changes. |
| Help | Displays the MagicDraw Help. |

#### TIP: Shortcut Key

Shortcut Key

Double-click the item name and it will be moved to the opposite list.

356006156

**Related Pages**

- Tools
- Class
- Class diagram
- Generalization relationship
- Realization relationship

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711504 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00655: Import Maps migration from CSV Import Plugin

- page_id: `136711504`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711504/Import+Maps+migration+from+CSV+Import+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:43.432+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- labels: ['import-map-migration', 'show-excel-csv-import-migration-message', 'csv-import-map-migration']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page is for those who used the CSV Import Plugin to import data into the model. As of the 2021x version, this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by [Excel/CSV Sync](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files) (import to table) and [Excel/CSV Import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files) (import to model) features.

All Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin can be reused by migrating them. The procedure outlined below shows how to import those maps:

To import data from the Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin

1. Open the project containing the Import Maps/Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin.
2. In the top left corner of the modeling tool, click the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Import Using Existing Map** or ******Import Using Existing Map Group****** .
3. In the **CSV Import Map Migration** message, select **Yes**. To perform the migration of Import Maps or Map Groups later, make sure the **Do not ask again** check box is clear.
4. In the **Select Import Map** or **Select Map Group**dialog, select Import Map or Map Group.
5. Click OK . The **Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
6. Select the file, check mapping options. How to >>
7. Click the **Import** button. 
The data is imported. A Map Group is migrated only if all Import Maps belonging to that Map Group are migrated at the same time. If at least one is not migrated, the whole Map Group will not be migrated.

If the **CSV Import Map Migration** message does not show again when trying to migrate the Import Map or Map Group, you can enable this option in the **Project Options** dialog. For this, follow the procedure below.

To enable the **CSV Import Map Migration** message

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the search box of the Project Options dialog, type " mig ".
3. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, set the Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message option value to true to enable the functionality.
4. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page is for those who used the CSV Import Plugin to import data into the model. As of the 2021x version, this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files">Excel/CSV Sync</a> (import to table) and <a class="ancestor-link parent-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files">Excel/CSV Import</a> (import to model) features.</p><p>All Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin can be reused by migrating them. The procedure outlined below shows how to import those maps:</p><p><br /></p><p>To import data from the Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project containing the Import Maps/Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin.</li><li>In the top left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; <strong>Import Using Existing Map </strong></strong>or <strong><strong><strong><strong>Import Using Existing Map Group</strong></strong></strong></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message, select <strong>Yes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d34b7df2-dfb8-43dd-aa7f-895a7782a5b7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To perform the migration of Import Maps or Map Groups later, make sure the <strong>Do not ask again</strong> check box is clear.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Import Map</strong> or <strong>Select Map Group </strong>dialog, select Import Map or Map Group. </p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> opens. </li><li>Select the file, check mapping options. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea" rel="nofollow">How to &gt;&gt;</a></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.<br />The data is imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="903c2d06-1978-4902-a2a1-54dc3404e22c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Map Group is migrated only if all Import Maps belonging to that Map Group are migrated at the same time. If at least one is not migrated, the whole Map Group will not be migrated.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p>If the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message does not show again when trying to migrate the Import Map or Map Group, you can enable this option in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. For this, follow the procedure below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the search box of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, type &quot;<em>mig</em>&quot;.</li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message </strong>option value to <em>true</em> to enable the functionality.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714414 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00656: Import syntax

- page_id: `136714414`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714414/Import+syntax
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:33.793+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Import tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

715763235

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

715763237

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

715763236

To use the Import tool syntax

1. To declare Import tool in the template, type #import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool') in the document that you want to import its content to the report. The #import directive will load Import tool so that it can be accessed inside the template.
  - The first parameter 'import' is for identifying the alias of Import tool (in this case, 'import' , but it can be named anything).
  - The second parameter 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool' has to be copied as it is (do not rename it). Otherwise, Import tool will not be loaded.
2. To import any content from a child template, type the following in the document that you want to import its content to the report:

- $import.include('child.docx') to import a complete document at the current position of the template, and/or
- $import.includeSection('child.docx', 'Section A') to include only the text contained in the named section of the document.

****

**$import**

This part names the alias assigned to Import tool when declaring it to the template.

****

**$import.include(FileName)**

**FileName** in [$import.include(FileName)](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample1) specifies the location of a file in the file system. **FileName** can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. The **FileName** parameter can be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, **FileName** would be searched as an Attached File element in a MagicDraw project in the **File** property.

****

**$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)**

**FileName** in [$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample2) functions the same as in the sub-section above. **FileName** can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. **SectionName** denotes a paragraph in the document named **FileName**, which is delimited by the **#sectionBegin(SectionName)** and **#sectionEnd** identifiers. The **SectionName** variable can also be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, **FileName** would be searched as an Attached File element in a MagicDraw project in the **File** property.

#### INFO: Information

Information

You can also use **$attachedFile**in the parameter of **$import.include(FileName)** and **$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3207e9a8-b8f7-45e5-82d3-f4e215d37b1d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715763235</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33426169-fbae-4ee7-ae1b-ff54e4249423"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715763237</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d241ced-125c-4edf-ac6e-33e903ae2d2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715763236</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use the Import tool syntax</p><hr /><ol><li>To declare Import tool in the template, type <strong>#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')</strong> in the document that you want to import its content to the report. The <strong>#import</strong> directive will load Import tool so that it can be accessed inside the template.<br /><ul><li>The first parameter<strong> 'import'</strong> is for identifying the alias of Import tool (in this case, <strong>'import'</strong>, but it can be named anything).</li><li>The second parameter <strong>'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool'</strong> has to be copied as it is (do not rename it). Otherwise, Import tool will not be loaded.</li></ul></li><li>To import any content from a child template, type the following in the document that you want to import its content to the report:    </li></ol><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>$import.include('child.docx')</strong> to import a complete document at the current position of the template, and/or</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>$import.includeSection('child.docx', 'Section A')</strong> to include only the text contained in the named section of the document.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import</strong></p><p>This part names the alias assigned to Import tool when declaring it to the template.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import.include(FileName)</strong></p><p><strong>FileName</strong> in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample1">$import.include(FileName)</a> specifies the location of a file in the file system. <strong>FileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. The <strong>FileName</strong> parameter can be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>FileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File element in a MagicDraw project in the <strong>File</strong> property.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</strong></p><p><strong>FileName</strong> in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample2">$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</a> functions the same as in the sub-section above. <strong>FileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. <strong>SectionName</strong> denotes a paragraph in the document named <strong>FileName</strong>, which is delimited by the <strong>#sectionBegin(SectionName)</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong> identifiers. The <strong>SectionName</strong> variable can also be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>FileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File element in a MagicDraw project in the <strong>File</strong> property.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd51469a-e548-4f4f-83ef-ac7e3af3ab2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also use <strong>$attachedFile </strong>in the parameter of <strong>$import.include(FileName)</strong> and <strong>$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714413 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00657: Import tool

- page_id: `136714413`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714413/Import+tool
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:56:08.209+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1175109811

1175109813

1175109812

Import tool is a module of functions that allows importing documents to be in generated reports. Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.

The Import tool allows you to:

- Parse and import RTF , DOCX, HTML , and text templates from any location in the file system
- Reuse the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space='MD2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space='MD2024x'] syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes #sectionBegin and #sectionEnd , introduced with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space='MD2024x'] .

1175109810

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43a306aa-5cf3-487d-b5ab-7dc55be2e4b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109811</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5906bd29-43fe-4769-ba0e-a0555c875317"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109813</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9338f24e-8903-4191-82d2-3e615044c314"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109812</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Import tool is a module of functions that allows importing documents to be in generated reports. Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.</p><p>The Import tool allows you to:</p><ul><li>Parse and import <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX, HTML</em>, and text templates from any location in the file system</li><li>Reuse the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionBegin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> and <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong>, introduced with the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#includeSection Directive]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28bafdf7-ad4a-426a-a529-a2db191eec84"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109810</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="baacf33e-71b7-4747-9f4b-01f0ff324713" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714415 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00658: Import usage

- page_id: `136714415`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714415/Import+usage
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:34.104+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Import tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains the examples of usage of the main and child templates. These examples allow you to create documents as the main template and reuse the content of child templates into it.

#### Preparatory Step

Define a child template that you will use as in Examples 1, 2, and 3. This child template contains the header line and named sections (**Section A** and **Section B**). Type the following lines in the document that you want to define the child template:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 1

This example shows how to statically include the complete child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include it, call the **$import.include('child.docx')** method. Note that there is no specified path. This path to the child template shows you that the main and child templates reside in the same directory. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include all of its content to the report:

```text

```

The output right below will include the entire text from the main template (right above) and from the child template minus the template directives. The included content will be then parsed and stripped of all velocity directives. The output is as follows:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 2

This example shows how to statically include a section of the child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include the section named **Section A** from the child template, call the **$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')** method. In this example, the main and child templates reside in different directories, which means that the child template will reside in a subdirectory called *templates*. This *templates* directory will thus reside in the same directory as the main template. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in **Section A** to the report:

```text

```

The output will be:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 3

This example shows how to dynamically include the section of a child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. Before dynamically including the section of the child template named **Section B**, set a variable for the file location of the child template **[#set ($child = "***C:/ImportTool/child.rtf***")]** and another one for the section name to be included**[#set ($section = "Section B")]**. To include the **Section B** section from the child template, call the **$import.includeSection($child, $section)** method. In this example, the child's template location is provided by an absolute path. Note that dynamic values must not be specified in a pair of quote marks, otherwise the section will not be included. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in **Section B** to the report:

```text

```

The output will be:

```text

```

The Import tool supports *DOCX, RTF, HTML, XML*, and*text* files only.

#### Usage in Example 4

This example shows how to include a document as an Attached File element in a project. First, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space='MD2024x'] element in a project. The **File** property will then be used as a parameter to make a reference to the Attached File element. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include the content of the Attached File element to the report:

```text

```

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Including a document as an Attached File element in a project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section explains the examples of usage of the main and child templates. These examples allow you to create documents as the main template and reuse the content of child templates into it.</p><h2>Preparatory Step</h2><p>Define a child template that you will use as in Examples 1, 2, and 3. This child template contains the header line and named sections (<strong>Section A</strong> and <strong>Section B</strong>). Type the following lines in the document that you want to define the child template:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5fc47c3-5cca-4eaa-b170-f0b958c676e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the child template.
#sectionBegin(Section A)
This is Section A.
#sectionEnd
#sectionBegin(Section B)
This is Section B.
#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 1</h2><p>This example shows how to statically include the complete child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include it, call the <strong>$import.include('child.docx')</strong> method. Note that there is no specified path. This path to the child template shows you that the main and child templates reside in the same directory. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include all of its content to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a66eea4e-eb0c-446b-ab4d-73b4d0b8e223"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
This is the 1st main template
Include child template
$import.include('child.docx')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output right below will include the entire text from the main template (right above) and from the child template minus the template directives. The included content will be then parsed and stripped of all velocity directives. The output is as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9edd6ed8-2585-454f-9e9a-5029cff67dea"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the 1st main template
Include child template
This is the child template.
This is Section A.
This is Section B.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 2</h2><p>This example shows how to statically include a section of the child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include the section named <strong>Section A</strong> from the child template, call the <strong>$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')</strong> method. In this example, the main and child templates reside in different directories, which means that the child template will reside in a subdirectory called <em>templates</em>. This <em>templates</em> directory will thus reside in the same directory as the main template. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in <strong>Section A</strong> to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ce50327-1838-4616-b129-4f4d56003298"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
This is the 2nd main template
Include Section A
$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32e6166f-cb4b-4901-a003-982722bd78e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the 2nd  main template
Include Section A
This is Section A.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 3</h2><p>This example shows how to dynamically include the section of a child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. Before dynamically including the section of the child template named <strong>Section B</strong>, set a variable for the file location of the child template <strong>[#set ($child = &quot;</strong><em>C:/ImportTool/child.rtf</em><strong>&quot;)]</strong> and another one for the section name to be included<strong> [#set ($section = &quot;Section B&quot;)]</strong>. To include the <strong>Section B</strong> section from the child template, call the <strong>$import.includeSection($child, $section)</strong> method. In this example, the child's template location is provided by an absolute path. Note that dynamic values must not be specified in a pair of quote marks, otherwise the section will not be included. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in <strong>Section B</strong> to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10683bc-cf02-44d9-ab7a-35406d92e52a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
This is the 3rd main template
Include Section B

#set ($section = "Section B")
$import.includeSection($child, $section)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be15115f-a695-4e24-9cb6-10022f943c67"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the 3rd main template
Include Section B
This is Section B.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e9e5e3b-600a-4d8a-b607-5ba5ccd20fdc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Import tool supports <em>DOCX, RTF, HTML, XML</em>, and<em> text</em> files only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 4</h2><p>This example shows how to include a document as an Attached File element in a project. First, you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create an Attached File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> element in a project. The <strong>File</strong> property will then be used as a parameter to make a reference to the Attached File element. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include the content of the Attached File element to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eade2b34-65c8-4540-8454-0bc694a8a8ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
#foreach($included in $AttachedFile)
	#if($included.file == "included.docx")
		$import.include($included)
	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Attach File Property.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Import usage" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Including a document as an Attached File element in a project.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714523 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00659: Importing a template to MagicDraw using Report Wizard

- page_id: `136714523`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714523/Importing+a+template+to+MagicDraw+using+Report+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:39.279+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Creating Your First Template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1356305390

1356305392

1356305389

**On this page**

3

1356305392

#### Importing a template to MagicDraw

Once you have created a template, you will need to import it into MagicDraw by using Report Wizard.

To import a template into a modeling tool using Report Wizard

1. Click Tools > Report Wizard on the MagicDraw main menu.
2. Click the **New** button in the **Select a report template** wizard. The **New Template** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Opening the Report Wizard dialog and creating a new custom template by clicking the New button.
3. In the **New Template** dialog, type the name and description. Select a category, and then click**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Entering the new template name and description, and then selecting a template file in the New Template dialog.
4. Find the template file that you have created, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Select_button.png'] .
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_button.png'] to import the template.

To generate a report based on a template, select the template (in this example, **My template)**from the template list, and follow the steps as instructed by the wizard. See [Report Wizard User Guide](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Report+Wizard) for more information.

#### Glossary

- Report template Ready-made report configurations that you can use to quickly create a specific type of report in Report Wizard.

#### More information about working with templates and reports

For more information about working with templates and reports, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Management Pane' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default templates' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Data Management Pane' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85e293df-3081-4921-82d9-af55eb14dc98"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356305390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25cdf189-a96c-4bef-9288-014b54a8324b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356305392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fae8f6e1-8872-46b1-9d01-4b935e03e17a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356305389</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cdabee79-46ea-4ddc-9bdb-2a2d0d8b5898"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c78b866-e562-466f-9a84-cc005e670d83"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356305392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>Importing a template to MagicDraw</h2><p>Once you have created a template, you will need to import it into MagicDraw by using Report Wizard.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import a template into a modeling tool using Report Wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong> on the MagicDraw main menu.</li><li><p>Click the <strong>New</strong> button in the <strong>Select a report template</strong> wizard. The <strong>New Template</strong> dialog will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="newTemplate.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing a template to MagicDraw using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Opening the Report Wizard dialog and creating a new custom template by clicking the New button.</h6></li><li><p>In the <strong>New Template</strong> dialog, type the name and description. Select a category, and then click<strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="button-1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing a template to MagicDraw using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="New Template Dialog" ac:alt="New Template Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-template-a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing a template to MagicDraw using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Entering the new template name and description, and then selecting a template file in the New Template dialog.</h6></li><li>Find the template file that you have created, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing a template to MagicDraw using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing a template to MagicDraw using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong> </strong>to import the template.</li></ol><p>To generate a report based on a template, select the template (in this example, <strong>My template) </strong>from the template list, and follow the steps as instructed by the wizard. See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Report+Wizard">Report Wizard User Guide</a> for more information.</p><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Report template</strong><br />Ready-made report configurations that you can use to quickly create a specific type of report in Report Wizard.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with templates and reports</h2><p>For more information about working with templates and reports, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Template Management Pane" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Default templates" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
