<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711509 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00660: Importing Blocks and their properties

- page_id: `136711509`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711509/Importing+Blocks+and+their+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:43.594+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets say, we need to import the engine structure including Blocks, their Part and Value Properties. Firstly, open an existing or create new project. In this case, we create new project. Next, do the following:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024x']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024x']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024x']
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024x']

###### Importing Blocks

To import Block properties, the an *Engine* and *Engine Type* Blocks must be created in the model. In this case, we import those blocks. For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify the following options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Block as Element Type option value.
3. Specify the location of imported elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024x'] by selecting the Target Scope option value. In this case, the Structure Package is selected.
4. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 1.
5. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
6. Click Import . The Engine and Engine Type Blocks are created in the model. [ATTACHMENT filename='importing_engine_blocks.png']

###### Saving properties Import Maps

After the Blocks are imported, their all properties can be imported at once by creating a Map Group which consists of 3 Import Maps: *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties*. Follow the procedures below to save Import Maps.

The *Engine* Block is decomposed to *Engine Type* Part Property. Let's create an Import Map for the Part Property. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify the following options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Part Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select the Engine Block as the Target Scope option value.
4. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 2.
5. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
6. Click Save Map .
7. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
8. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Part . Click OK > Close . The Engine Part Import Map is saved. [ATTACHMENT filename='saving_engine_parts_import_map.png']

The *Engine* Block contains *serialNum* and *year*Value Properties. To save this Import Map, in the already opened [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Value Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select Name and Type properties as Properties to Map option value.
4. Select the Engine Block as the Target Scope option value.
5. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 3.
6. Create mapping between element property Name, Type and excel table cell Name, Type.
7. Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.
8. Click Save Map .
9. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
10. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Value Properties . Click OK > Close . The Engine Value Properties Import Map is saved.

Also, the E*ngine Type* Block contains *make*, *model*, *hp*, and *fuel***Value Properties. To save this Import Map, in the already opened [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Value Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select Name and Type properties as Properties to Map option value.
4. Select the Engine Type Block as the Target Scope option value.
5. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.
6. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
7. Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.
8. Click Save Map .
9. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
10. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Type Value Properties . Click OK > Close . The Engine Type Value Properties Import Map is saved.

###### Creating Map Group

After, the properties Import Maps are prepared, let's create the *Engine Properties Import* Map Group and add the following Import Maps: *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties.* For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups.**In the **Manage Map Groups** dialog, do the following:

1. In the **Tree** tab, select the *Excel/CSV imports* Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
2. Click the **Create** button.
3. In the Specification window, type thename *Engine Properties Import* for the new Map Group and click **Close**. The newly created Map Group is selected as the Select Map Group for Editing option value.
4. Press Ctrl and in the Tree tab, select the *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties* Import Maps.
5. C lick [ATTACHMENT filename='add_import_map.png'] to add them to the *Engine Properties Import* Import Map.
6. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

###### Importing Map Group

Finally, import an *Engine Properties Import* Map Group. For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map Group.**In the **Select Map Group** dialog, select the *Engine Properties Import* Map Group and click **OK**. All properties are created for the *Engine* and *Engine Type* Blocks at once.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Lets say, we need to import the engine structure including Blocks, their Part and Value Properties. Firstly, open an existing or create new project. In this case, we create new project. Next, do the following:</p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Importing Blocks"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Blocks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Saving properties Import Maps"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Save properties Import Maps]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Map Group"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create Map Group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Map Group"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Map Group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ac:link></li></ol><h4>Importing Blocks</h4><p>To import Block properties, the an <em>Engine</em> and <em>Engine Type</em> Blocks must be created in the model. In this case, we import those blocks. For this, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. In t</span>he <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify the following options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the <em>Engine</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Block as <strong>Element Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Specify the location of imported elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link> by selecting the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value. In this case, the <em>Structure</em> Package is selected.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 1.</li><li>Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.</li><li>Click <strong>Import</strong>.<br />The <em>Engine</em> and <em>Engine Type</em> Blocks are created in the model.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_engine_blocks.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><h4>Saving properties Import Maps</h4><p>After the Blocks are imported, their all properties can be imported at once by creating a Map Group which consists of 3 Import Maps: <em>Engine Parts</em>, <em>Engine Value Properties</em>, <em>Engine Type Value Properties</em>. Follow the procedures below to save Import Maps.</p><p>The <em>Engine</em> Block is decomposed to <em>Engine Type</em> Part Property. Let's create an Import Map for the Part Property. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">I</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. In t</span>he <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify the following options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the <em>Engine</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Part Property as <strong>Element Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select the <em>Engine </em>Block as the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 2.</li><li>Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.</li><li>Click <strong>Save Map</strong>. </li><li>In the <strong>Save Import Map </strong>dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the <em>Excel/CSV Imports</em> Package.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - <em>Engine Part</em>. Click <strong>OK </strong>&gt; <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The <em>Engine Part</em> Import Map is saved.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="saving_engine_parts_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>The <em>Engine</em> Block contains <em>serialNum</em> and <em>year </em>Value Properties. To save this Import Map, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n t</span>he already opened <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the <em>Engine</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Value Property as <strong>Element Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select Name and Type properties as <strong>Properties to Map</strong> option value.</li><li>Select the <em>Engine </em>Block as the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 3.</li><li>Create mapping between element property Name, Type and excel table cell Name, Type.</li><li>Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.</li><li>Click <strong>Save Map</strong>. </li><li>In the <strong>Save Import Map </strong>dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the <em>Excel/CSV Imports</em> Package.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - <em>Engine Value Properties</em>. Click <strong>OK </strong>&gt; <strong>Close</strong>. <br />The <em>Engine Value Properties </em>Import Map is saved.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>Also, the E<em>ngine Type</em> Block contains <em>make</em>, <em>model</em>, <em>hp</em>, and <em>fuel</em><em> </em>Value Properties. To save this Import Map, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n t</span>he already opened <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the <em>Engine</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Value Property as <strong>Element Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select Name and Type properties as <strong>Properties to Map</strong> option value.</li><li>Select the <em>Engine Type </em>Block as the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.</li><li>Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.</li><li>Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.</li><li>Click <strong>Save Map</strong>. </li><li>In the <strong>Save Import Map </strong>dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the <em>Excel/CSV Imports</em> Package.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - <em>Engine Type Value Properties</em>. Click <strong>OK </strong>&gt; <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The <em>Engine Type Value Properties </em>Import Map is saved.</li></ol><h4>Creating Map Group</h4><p>After, the properties Import Maps are prepared, let's create the <em>Engine Properties Import</em> Map Group and add the following Import Maps: <em>Engine Parts</em>, <em>Engine Value Properties</em>, <em>Engine Type Value Properties.</em> For this, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups. </strong>In the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog, do the following:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong>Tree</strong> tab, select the <em>Excel/CSV imports</em> Package as the owner for the new Map Group.</span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the Specification window, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">type the</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> name <em>Engine Properties Import</em> for the new Map Group and click <strong>Close</strong>. </span></span>The newly created Map Group is selected as the <strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option value.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Press Ctrl and in the Tree tab, select the <em>Engine Parts</em>, <em>Engine Value Properties</em>, <em>Engine Type Value Properties</em> Import Maps.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">C</span>lick <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">to add them to the <em>Engine Properties Import</em> Import Map.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_map_group.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4>Importing Map Group</h4><p>Finally, import an <em>Engine Properties Import</em> Map Group. For this, i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using Existing Map Group. </strong>In the <strong>Select Map Group</strong> dialog, select the <em>Engine Properties Import</em> Map Group and click <strong>OK</strong>. All properties are created for the <em>Engine</em> and <em>Engine Type</em> Blocks at once.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_map_group.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00661: Importing custom Jython libraries

- page_id: `136715761`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715761/Importing+custom+Jython+libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:44.232+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation > Supported scripting languages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to import custom Python libraries.

To import and use a custom *.py* file

1. Create a Lib directory in the <tool_installation_directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib\engines\jython directory.
2. Add the custom .py file to the Lib directory.
3. To use the custom *.py* script, execute the following (e.g. *myscript.py* is the name of the *.py* file):

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to import custom Python libraries.</p><p>To import and use a custom <em>.py</em> file</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <em>Lib</em> directory in the <em>&lt;tool_installation_directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib\engines\jython</em> directory.</li><li>Add the custom <em>.py</em> file to the <em>Lib</em> directory.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To use the custom <em>.py</em> script, execute the following (e.g. <em>myscript.py</em> is the name of the <em>.py</em> file):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="114a7d2c-f334-462d-a92c-d86e3afd2bb9"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import myscript
myscript.calculate(1, 5)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714611 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00662: Importing custom tool to template

- page_id: `136714611`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714611/Importing+custom+tool+to+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:43.412+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard uses the import directive for importing a Custom Tool to a template.

An example of a directive:

```text

```

The import directive declares that the template uses the custom tool, names the tool that defines it, and specifies its tag prefix before the custom tool is used in a template page. You can use more than one import directive in a template, but the prefix defined in each template page must be unique.

#### Attributes

**name** (type:String) 
 The uniform or full qualified class name that locates the Tool class, for example, mytool.HelloTool

**prefix** (type:String) 
 The prefix that precedes the custom tool name such as a string in $hello.getHello(). Empty prefixes are not allowed. When developing or using custom tools, do not use tag prefixes such as bookmark, sorter, template, file, array, group, map, iterator, list, date, report, exporter, profiling, and project, as they are reserved by Report Wizard.

The code fragment indicated below declares a custom tool.

```text

```

The output from the above template is:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard uses the import directive for importing a Custom Tool to a template.</p><p>An example of a directive:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd50b953-d474-470e-a26b-45c8eefbac29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('prefix', 'name')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The import directive declares that the template uses the custom tool, names the tool that defines it, and specifies its tag prefix before the custom tool is used in a template page. You can use more than one import directive in a template, but the prefix defined in each template page must be unique.</p><h2>Attributes</h2><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>name</strong> (type:String)<br />            The uniform or full qualified class name that locates the Tool class, for example, mytool.HelloTool</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>prefix</strong> (type:String)<br />            The prefix that precedes the custom tool name such as a string in $hello.getHello(). Empty prefixes are not allowed. When developing or using custom tools, do              not use tag prefixes such as bookmark, sorter, template, file, array, group, map, iterator, list, date, report, exporter, profiling, and project, as they are reserved                by Report Wizard.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The code fragment indicated below declares a custom tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a6f2762-f7a5-40ea-9382-07cadf641971"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('hello', 'mytool.HelloTool')
Get Hello
$hello.getHello()
Get Hello Foo
$hello.getHello('foo')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output from the above template is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfe379ac-2d2c-4a4a-98ff-bd3a4fbdaf5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Get Hello
Hello World
Get Hello Foo
Hello foo]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711470 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00663: Importing data from Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `136711470`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711470/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:13:04.922+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2052901168

100.00002%

2052901183

2052901173

**On this page**

43

2052901166

INLINE

##### Introduction

You can import data from Excel (*.xls*and*.xlsx*) or*CSV*(*.csv*and*.txt*) format files to your model. You are able to specify the import mapping options between file columns and element properties of the model. Additionally, you can save your maps, collect them in groups, manage those groups, and use an entire group instead of a single map for data import. Before importing data from Excel or CSV files to model, create new or open an existing project.

700500

You can choose the following import commands:

- 
  - Import Using New Map - to import data from Excel or CSV file by specifying new mapping options and/or save an Import Map. >]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
  - Import Using Existing Map - to import data from an Excel or CSV file by selecting a previously saved map. >]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
  - **Import Using Existing Map Group** - to import data from Excel or CSV file by selecting the map group that collects as many Import Maps as you added. 
>]]>
  - Manage Map Groups - to create, delete or change the order of the m ap groups. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Map Groups' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All Excel/CSV file import commands from the File menu.

##### Preparing Excel or CSV file to import

To import data from Excel or CSV files, the files must met the following prerequisites:

- Heading is located in the first row.
- When creating relationships, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are the client and supplier of the relationship.

Recommended prerequisites:

- Eliminate merged cells.
- Every column has a unique column name.
- Every row has the same number and name of columns.
- Every cell has the element name or qualified element name.

##### Importing data by using a new map

To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using a new map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
4. Select the file and specify mapping options. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea) You must select the file in the **Select Excel/CSV file** areaand specify the**Element Type**,**Properties to Map,**and **Target Scope** mapping option values to import data.
5. In the Mapping area, create mapping between the Excel/CSV file data and modeling tool properties. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
6. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
7. Click the Import button. The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.

##### Importing data by using an existing map

To import data from Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map .
4. In the **Select Import Map** dialog, select existing Import Map. If you cannot see any Import Maps, you need to save it first. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
5. Click OK . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
6. (Optional) Select the file, change mapping options. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea)
7. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to override the selected Import Map or create a new one. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
8. Click the Import button. The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.

##### Importing data by using an existing map group

To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map group

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map Group .
4. In the **Select Map Group** dialog, choose the map group. If you cannot see any map group, you need to create it first. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Map Groups' space='']>]]>
5. Click **OK**. 
The data from Excel or CSV files are imported to the model according to Import Maps and their order in the Map Group. The import of a map group can fail because of the following reasons:the Excel or CSV file is missing;the element type does not exist in the model;properties do not exist in the selected element;the target scope does not exist in the model.

2052905962

**Related pages**

****

- [Sync with Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files)

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72929d53-ab7a-4c40-a79d-3b269563fde4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2052901168</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6210b4b8-d55f-4950-9964-53f01281f21a"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">100.00002%</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2052901183</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6dd4471-c0e4-406c-952c-fdc216e9295e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2052901173</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0db8ff6f-c13b-4f97-8018-adf22c91af93">page</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e905a8bb-0144-4b09-adf2-66e3a1410da0"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04797211-5505-45e5-a41e-718c0bc68397"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2052901166</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c89bfe7-681f-4e99-a2f2-1b4c9d6f58e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Introduction</h3><p>You can import <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">data from Excel (</span><em>.xls </em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and</span><em> .xlsx</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">) or </span><em>CSV</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> (</span><em>.csv</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and </span><em>.txt</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">) format files to your model. You are able to specify the import mapping options between file columns and element properties of the model.  Additionally, you can save your maps, collect them in groups, manage those groups, and use an entire group instead of a single map for data import. Before importing data from Excel or CSV files to model, create new or open an existing project.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a32c6d2-dd3b-4b6e-b54d-0811f8700266"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x18wXfSfeTM&amp;feature=youtu.be" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">500</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></span></p><p><br />You can choose the following import commands:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Import Using New Map</strong> - to import data from Excel or CSV file by specifying new mapping options and/or save an Import Map.<br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Importingdatabyusinganewmap"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import data by using a new map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to save a map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Import Using Existing Map</strong> - to import data from an Excel or CSV file by selecting a previously saved map. <br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Importingdatabyusingexistingmap"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import data by using existing map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to override an existing map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Import Using Existing Map Group</strong> - to import data from Excel or CSV file by selecting the map group that collects as many Import Maps as you added. <br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Importingdatabyusingexistingmapgroup"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import data by using existing map group >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li><strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> - to <span>create, delete or change the order of the m</span>ap groups. <br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing Map Groups" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to manage Map Groups >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Import_commands.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All Excel/CSV file import commands from the File menu.</h6><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Preparing Excel or CSV file to import</h3><p>To import data from Excel or CSV files, the files must met the following p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites</span>:</p><ul><li>Heading is located in the first row.</li><li>When creating relationships, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are the client and supplier of the relationship.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>Recommended prerequisites:</p><ul><li>Eliminate merged cells.</li><li>Every column has a unique column name.</li><li>Every row has the same number and name of columns.</li><li>Every cell has the element name or qualified element name. <br /><br /></li></ul><h3>Importing data by using a new map</h3><p>To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using a new map</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Create new or open an existing project.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure:<br /> - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. <ac:link><ac:link-body>How to &gt;&gt;<br /> </ac:link-body></ac:link>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Basic Prerequisites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span>lick the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the file and specify mapping options. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea">How to &gt;&gt;</a></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fed5b67b-4625-43a1-a3ff-52dc368f2a9b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You must select the file in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Select Excel/CSV file</strong> area </span>and specify the<strong> Element Type</strong>,<strong> Properties to Map,</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and <strong>Target Scope</strong> mapping option values to import data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the Mapping area, create mapping between<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> the Excel/CSV file data and <span>modeling tool</span> properties. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea">How to &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li>(Optional) Click the<strong> Save Map</strong> button <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">to save your map options as the Import Map</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.<br />The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.</li></ol><h3>Importing data by using an existing map</h3><p>To import data from Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>Create new or open an existing project.</span><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure:<br /> - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. <ac:link><ac:link-body>How to &gt;&gt;<br /> </ac:link-body></ac:link>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Basic Prerequisites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span>lick the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using Existing Map</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Import Map</strong> dialog, select existing Import Map. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69c538e8-90bb-4db3-8e65-c060114faded"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you cannot see any Import Maps, you need to save it first. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. </li><li>(Optional) Select the file, change <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">mapping options. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea">How to &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li>(Optional) Click the <strong>Save Map</strong> button to override the selected Import Map or create a new one. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.<br />The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.</li></ol><h3>Importing data by using an existing map group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map group</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>Create new or open an existing project.</span><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure:<br /> - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. <ac:link><ac:link-body>How to &gt;&gt;<br /> </ac:link-body></ac:link>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Basic Prerequisites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span>lick the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using Existing Map Group</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Map Group</strong> dialog, choose the map group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce5ad524-c962-4cc7-b721-c82462ca4f50"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you cannot see any map group, you need to create it first. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing Map Groups" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The data from Excel or CSV files are imported to the model according to Import Maps and their order in the Map Group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8b55090-d00b-494c-b107-4c36ef33b9f0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The import of a map group can fail because of the following reasons:</p><ul><li>the Excel or CSV file is missing;</li><li>the element type does not exist in the model;</li><li>properties do not exist in the selected element;</li><li>the target scope does not exist in the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf7bfeb-f929-4f45-9e1e-bd23895fdb8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2052905962</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9a2106fa-2900-4036-9ab2-2c5045c8476a" /></strong></p><ul><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><strong><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files">Sync with Excel or CSV files</a></strong></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709364 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00664: Importing data from other UML tools and formats

- page_id: `136709364`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709364/Importing+data+from+other+UML+tools+and+formats
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:45.586+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: ['importing-uml', 'importing-data']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you need to open a model of a different format or created with another UML tool, you have to import it to MagicDraw instead of simply opening it. MagicDraw allows importing projects from the following file formats:

- Another .mdzip project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x']
- UML 2.1/2.5 XMI File
- MagicDraw Native XML File
- MOF XMI File
- CA ERwin Data Modeler v7.x
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ReqIF files' space='CRMP2024x']
- OWL Ontology File
- Eclipse UML2 (v2.x, v3.x, v4.x, v5.x) XMI file
- XPDL File
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FMU import' space='SYSMLP2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulink import' space='SYSMLP2024x']
- Modelica Library

- Enterprise Architect UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 File. Data can be imported from an Enterprise Architect 7.1, 7.5, or 8.0 file. Please note that Enterprise Architect does not export 100% standard UML 2.1 XMI, and this causes some data loss during the import.

- Use[Sodius](https://www.sodiuswillert.com/en/home)to import data from the following products:
  - Publisher for Rational Software Architect
  - Publisher for System Architect
  - Publisher for Rhapsody

- UML diagrams from Microsoft Visio using XMI files. Microsoft Visio 2007 and earlier requires extra add-ons for exporting UML model to XMI files.

To import an external model

1. Select File > **Import** From .
2. Select a file format you want to import a model from.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you need to open a model of a different format or created with another UML tool, you have to import it to MagicDraw instead of simply opening it. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">MagicDraw allows importing projects from the following file formats:</span></p><p><br /></p><ul><li>Another .mdzip project</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>UML 2.1/2.5 XMI File</li><li>MagicDraw Native XML File</li><li>MOF XMI File</li><li>CA ERwin Data Modeler v7.x</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CRMP2024x" ri:content-title="Importing ReqIF files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Requirements Interchange Format (ReqIF) File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>OWL Ontology File</li><li>Eclipse UML2 (v2.x, v3.x, v4.x, v5.x) XMI file</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">XPDL File</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="FMU import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FMU File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="Simulink import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Simulink File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Modelica Library</span></li></ul><ul><li><p>Enterprise Architect UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 File. Data can be imported from an Enterprise Architect 7.1, 7.5, or 8.0 file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e76de8f-6f1d-46d0-a283-b9757cf03d8c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Please note that Enterprise Architect does not export 100% standard UML 2.1 XMI, and this causes some data loss during the import.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use </span><a href="https://www.sodiuswillert.com/en/home">Sodius</a><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> to import data from the following products:</span><br /></span><ul><li>Publisher for Rational Software Architect</li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Publisher for System Architect</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Publisher for Rhapsody</span></p></li></ul></li></ul><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">UML diagrams from Microsoft Visio using XMI files.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55b087f9-d2c1-461d-b087-fd7b5f19607d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Microsoft Visio 2007 and earlier requires extra add-ons for exporting UML model to XMI files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To import an external model</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">File</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><strong>Import</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">From</strong>.</li><li>Select a file format you want to import a model from. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709379 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00665: Importing Diagrams

- page_id: `136709379`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709379/Importing+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:46.845+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Enterprise Architect Import allows you to import diagrams. The imported diagram information includes:

33

##### Geometry Properties

The geometry properties imported to a UML model are:

- Positions on a diagram (for shape elements)
- Width and height (for shape elements)
- Path break points (for link elements)

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Other display properties can override geometry information. For example, if an imported element width is shorter than the required width to display text on the element, the width will be adjusted automatically.

##### Color Properties

Color properties will be imported along with the diagrams to a modeling tool developed by NoMagic. Each color property has a different name in a modeling tool. The following table shows color properties mapping.

| EA | Modeling tool by NoMagic |
| --- | --- |
| Background | Fill color |
| Border color (for shape element) | Pen color |
| Font color | Text color |
| Line color (for link element) | Pen color |

##### Display Properties

Display properties in EA can be categorized into three groups: shape, link, and diagram. Only those properties corresponding to MagicDraw will be imported. For example, the **Show Diagram Details**property in EA will be imported as the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table with diagram information' space='MD2024x']**Show Diagram Info**property in a modeling tool developed by NoMagic.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Enterprise Architect Import allows you to import diagrams. The imported diagram information includes:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6eeee738-6a1a-47fa-bb30-70cfafa46597"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Geometry Properties</h3><p>The geometry properties imported to a UML model are:</p><ul><li>Positions on a diagram (for shape elements)</li><li>Width and height (for shape elements)</li><li>Path break points (for link elements)</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9067b152-2de0-447c-a428-0923be62ccce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Other display properties can override geometry information. For example, if an imported element width is shorter than the required width to display text on the element, the width will be adjusted automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Color Properties</h3><p>Color properties will be imported along with the diagrams to a modeling tool developed by NoMagic. Each color property has a different name in a modeling tool. The following table shows color properties mapping.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 32.1145%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 64.0199%;" /><col style="width: 35.9801%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>EA</th><th><span>Modeling tool by NoMagic</span></th></tr><tr><td>Background</td><td><p>Fill color</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Border color (for shape element)</p></td><td><p>Pen color</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Font<span> </span>color</span></td><td><p>Text color</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Line color (for link element)</span></td><td><p>Pen color</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Display Properties</h3><p>Display properties in EA can be categorized into three groups: shape, link, and diagram. Only those properties corresponding to MagicDraw will be imported. For example, the <strong>Show Diagram Details </strong>property in EA will be imported as the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table with diagram information" /><ac:link-body><strong>Show Diagram Info </strong>property</ac:link-body></ac:link> in a modeling tool developed by NoMagic.</p><p> </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711519 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00666: Importing instances

- page_id: `136711519`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711519/Importing+instances
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:43.811+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets say, we need to import the nested instances with slots in the already prepared Engine model structure where the *Engine* is decomposed to *Engine Type*, as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *Engine* Block will be a classifier for three engine instances. Each engine instance will contain *serialNum*, *year*slots with defined values and nested *engine Type* instances*.* Each nested *engine Type* instances will contain four *fuel*, *hp*, *make* and *model* slots with defined values. In the following figure, the Excel spreadsheet is prepared for the instances import. [Learn more how to prepare the Excel or CSV file for import >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, begin the import: in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with instances. In this case, the Vehicle model import Excel file is selected.
2. Select Instances as Import Type option value.
3. Select Engine Block as Instance Classifier option value.
4. Select Value Properties of the Engine Block and engine Type Part Property as the Nested Properties To Map option values. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_nested_properties.png']
5. Specify the location of imported elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024x'] by selecting the Target Scope option value. In this case, the Instances Package is selected.
6. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.
7. Create mapping between model properties and excel table cells.
8. Click Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='instances_import_dialog_options.png']

As a result, three nested engine instances and their slots are created with defined values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Lets say, we need to import the nested instances with slots in the already prepared Engine model structure where the <em>Engine</em> is decomposed to <em>Engine Type</em>, as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="engine_decomposition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <em>Engine</em> Block will be a classifier for three engine instances. Each engine instance will contain <em>serialNum</em>, <em>year </em>slots with defined values and nested <em>engine Type</em> instances<em>.</em> Each nested <em>engine Type</em> instances will contain four <em>fuel</em>, <em>hp</em>, <em>make</em> and <em>model</em> slots with defined values. In the following figure, the Excel spreadsheet is prepared for the instances import. <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport">Learn more how to prepare the Excel or CSV file for import &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="engines_excel_spreadsheet.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Next, begin the import: i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. In t</span>he <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with instances. In this case, the <em>Vehicle model import</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Instances as <strong>Import Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select <em>Engine</em> Block as <strong>Instance Classifier</strong> option value.</li><li>Select Value Properties of the <em>Engine</em> Block and <em>engine Type</em> Part Property as the <strong>Nested Properties To Map</strong> option values.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_nested_properties.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Specify the location of imported elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link> by selecting the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value. In this case, the <em>Instances </em>Package is selected.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.</li><li>Create mapping between model properties and excel table cells.</li><li>Click <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instances_import_dialog_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>As a result, three nested engine instances and their slots are created with defined values. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="imported_instances.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709616 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00667: Importing models

- page_id: `136709616`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709616/Importing+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:00.616+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Composing model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Importing models from used projects

If you no longer need to have the set of elements in a separate project, you can import these elements into the main project.

To import a used project into the main project

1. In the Model Browser, select the package of this used project and open the shortcut menu .
2. Select Project Usages > Import .

All the elements of this used project are copied into the main project, and links to this used project are removed from the main project. The unlinked project and its elements continue to exist in the file system or server after the import.

- If the used project you want to import uses other projects, after the import these projects become directly used in the main project.
- The Standard/System Profiles cannot be imported according to our tool policy.

##### Importing models from other projects

Another way of composing your model, is to import a model from another project.

To import a model from another project

1. In the main menu, select File > Import From > Another Project .
2. Select a project you want to import.
3. Click **Import**. All elements from another project will be imported. If the model that you are importing also uses projects, these projects will be reused directly in your project after the model import.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Importing models from used projects</h3><p>If you no longer need to have the set of elements in a separate project, you can import these elements into the main project.</p><p> </p><p>To import a used project into the main project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, select the package of this used project and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cd108d97-84da-4865-8ccf-44b486b6e0c6">open the shortcut menu</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Import</strong>.</li></ol><p>All the elements of this used project are copied into the main project, and links to this used project are removed from the main project. The unlinked project and its elements continue to exist in the file system or server after the import.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4790239a-deb3-4f59-ba25-1ce2e1cdf086"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the used project you want to import uses other projects, after the import these projects become directly used in the main project.</li><li>The Standard/System Profiles cannot be imported according to our tool policy.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Importing models from other projects</h3><p>Another way of composing your model, is to import a model from another project.</p><p> </p><p>To import a model from another project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Another Project</strong>.</li><li>Select a project you want to import.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Import</strong>. All elements from another project will be imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8f81a64-5f1e-4400-a66f-28ac9ff8134f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the model that you are importing also uses projects, these projects will be reused directly in your project after the model import.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711506 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00668: Importing nested Requirements

- page_id: `136711506`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711506/Importing+nested+Requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:43.519+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets' say we need to import nested structure of requirements from the Excel file. For this, you must define the Owner property in the Excel spreadsheet with the name or id of each requirement. As example, see the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Requirements in the Excel spreadsheet with defined Owner property.

To import nested Requirements

1. Open the project in the modeling tool.
2. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x'] functionality.
3. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog), select the Excel file. Make sure the Excel file contains Owner column. See the example above.
4. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the Requirement type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - select Name, Text, id and Owner properties you want to fill in with data from Excel file.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported Requirements. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
5. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the column on the right to the property on the left. [Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
6. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
7. Click the Import button. The Requirements are imported. If any of the imported Requirement already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Lets' say we need to import <span>nested structure of requirements from the Excel file. For this, you must define the Owner property in the Excel spreadsheet with the name or id of each requirement. As example, see the following figure. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_req_excel_spreadsheet.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing nested Requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Requirements in the Excel spreadsheet with defined Owner property.</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To import nested Requirements</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Open the project in the modeling tool.</span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="550f0d57-9ef5-4e47-be80-3ea69ef5a3be"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For element import via tables, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exce/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> functionality.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a>, select the Excel file. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d12b70c5-f63a-40ff-b71b-6c5c7968ddbf"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure the Excel file contains Owner column.
````
