<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709693 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00669: Importing projects from Ecore files

- page_id: `136709693`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709693/Importing+projects+from+Ecore+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:04.133+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Ecore support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. 
 
To import an Ecore file

1. In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.
2. Use the Importing projects from MOF files procedure.

This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> To import an Ecore file</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.</li><li>Use the <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+projects+from+MOF+files">Importing projects from MOF files</a> procedure.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32276341-4f32-43be-be16-e7105068a796"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709671 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00670: Importing projects from MOF files

- page_id: `136709671`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709671/Importing+projects+from+MOF+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:03.681+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > MOF support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import a project from a MOF file

1. From the main menu, select File > Import From > MOF XMI File .
2. In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click Open . The file is imported as a separate project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To import a project from a MOF file</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF XMI File</strong>.</li><li>In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click <strong>Open</strong>. The file is imported as a separate project.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711515 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00671: Importing relationships

- page_id: `136711515`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711515/Importing+relationships
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:43.701+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

73869989

100.00002%

73870000

73869990

**On this page**

5

73869987

##### Main conditions

You can create relationships between existing model elements by importing them from Excel/CSV files. For thisthe following conditions must be met:

- 
  - The source/client and target/supplierelements you want to create relationships between must exist in the project.
  - The Excel/CSV source and target columns must be mapped with the Client/Source and Supplier/Target properties in the mapping area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x'].
  - The source and target elements must be legal in terms of Metamodel, DSL and code rules, e.g., the satisfy relationship client must be a requirement.

##### Preparing the Excel file to import relationships

When creating relationships in the model by importing them, you need to make sure the Excel file contains the source and target columns. The source column contains the names of elements that are the client of the relationship. The target column contains the names of the elements that are the suppliers of the relationship. In the example below, the Source column contains the names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Excel file content prepared with element names for the relationships import.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024x']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing nested Requirements' space='MD2024x']>]]>

If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, the elements are not under the same target scope, or you want to increase the import speed, use the following:

- Select to search elements and references in the Target Scope Only in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog wh en importing data.
- Define your source and target columns in the Excel file with qualified names of elements. In the example below, the Source column contains the qualified names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the qualified names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The The Excel file content prepared with qualified element names for the relationships import.

##### Relationships import procedure

To import relationships

1. Prepare the Excel file for relationships import. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing relationships' space='MD2024x']
2. Open the project in the modeling tool. Make sure the elements you want to create relationships between exist in the project. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='MD2024x']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing nested Requirements' space='MD2024x']>]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x'] functionality.
4. In the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog , select the Excel file. How to >>
5. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the relationship type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - the Client and Supplier properties are selected by default.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported relationships. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
6. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the Source column on the right to the Client property on the left. Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other columns/properties.[Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
7. (Optional) Cli ck the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
8. Click the Import button. The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x']>]]>

The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:

- The relationship end is empty in the data file.
- The source or the target elements do not exist in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.

In the following example, you can see specified import options in the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog before the import. After the import the Satisfy relationships are created between the defined Source and Target elements: Blocks and Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The import of Satisfy relationship.

73874634

**Related pages**

- Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- Saving an Import Map
- Managing Map Groups
- Excel and CSV Import dialog
- [Sync with Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c02a9414-08af-4090-9c66-98c064b298bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73869989</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3ee66f9-b631-47e4-8aef-68ccd20e6385"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">100.00002%</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73870000</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecbbbaeb-4c35-434a-b136-7bba992c2581"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73869990</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8094afaf-6b5f-472c-b44b-d1f6838fe2e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99351a8a-6864-4fa3-9597-f4a86f90068a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73869987</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Main conditions</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can create relationships between existing model elements by importing them from Excel/CSV files. For this </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">the following conditions must b<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="170073a5-01d5-48de-8278-606d3004334e">e met:</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The source<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">/client</span> and target/supplier </span>elements you want to create relationships between must exist in the project.</span><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Excel/CSV source and target columns must be mapped with the Client<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">/S</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ource</span> and Supplier/<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Target</span> properties in the mapping area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Import dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The source and target elements must be legal in terms of Metamodel, DSL and code rules, e.g., the satisfy relationship client must be a requirement.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Preparing the Excel file to import relationships </span></h3><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When creating relationships in the model by importing them, you need to make sure the Excel file contains the source and target columns. The source column contains the names of elements that are the client of the relationship. The t</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">arget column contains the names of the elements that are the suppliers of the relationship.  In the example below, the Source column contains the names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the names of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9c0b0b59-fcb1-4385-9b40-f83d60b8d996">Requireme</ac:inline-comment-marker>nts.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_file_content_element_names.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Excel file content prepared with element names for the relationships import.</span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8537137-5dd5-4660-9a7c-210ae5426b85"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Blocks and their properties >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing nested Requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Requirements >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br />If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, the elements are not under the same target scope, or you want to increase the import speed, use the following:</span></p><ul><li>Select to search elements and references in the <strong>Target Scope Only</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> wh<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c1e0d34a-fa2c-4683-9fe7-351af29daba7">en importing data.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Define your source and target columns in the Excel file with qualified names of elements. In the example below, the Source column contains the qualified names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the qualified names of Requirements.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_file_content_qualified_names.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Excel file content prepared with qualified element names for the relationships import.</span></span></h6><h3>Relationships import procedure</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To import relationships</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Prepare the Excel file for relationships import. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to prepare the Excel for the relationships import >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing relationships" /></ac:link></span></span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Open the project in the modeling tool. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c49ee855-2c1f-4fc8-b6b9-d286c8518d83"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure the elements you want to create relationships between exist in the project.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing Blocks and their properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Blocks and their properties >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing nested Requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Requirements >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="71e8ffc2-6548-4aba-9c64-c52e50cfb4b4">click</ac:inline-comment-marker> the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="550f0d57-9ef5-4e47-be80-3ea69ef5a3be"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For element import via tables, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exce/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> functionality.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a>, select the Excel file. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelecttheExcel/CSVfilearea">How to &gt;&gt;</a></li><li><span><span><span>Specify the mapping options:<br /></span></span></span><ul><li><strong>Import Type </strong>- select the Element value.</li><li><strong>Element Type </strong>- select the relationship type you want to create.</li><li><strong>Properties to Map</strong> - the Client and Supplier properties are selected by default.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Target Scope </strong>- select the Package you want to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="03469e68-1443-462c-b12c-dc3869e0edd5">contain imported relat</ac:inline-comment-marker>ionships. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the Owner property is selected as the <strong>Properties to Map</strong> option value, the data is imported according the owner.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="980f374f-bd9c-4ec1-aa28-1eccd62e0348"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea">Learn more about mapping<strong> </strong>options &gt;&gt;</a></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><p>Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the Source column on the right to the Client property on the left. Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other co<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a8279882-b14f-4017-9fdb-333f23229d0d">lumns/properties.<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea"> Learn more about mapping area &gt;&gt;</a></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li>(Optional) Cli<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ccfbd794-fb77-4e40-abf7-462fe69d4a95">ck the</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ccfbd794-fb77-4e40-abf7-462fe69d4a95"> Save Map</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong> button to save your map options as the Import Map. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Saving+an+Import+Map">Learn more how to save Import Map &gt;&gt;</a></li><li>Click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Import</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> button.<br /></span>The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">hey are <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9ad23a6a-1e5d-480f-a37d-9ea1fee47058">updated. To avoid</ac:inline-comment-marker> duplication of elements specify <strong>Identification Property</strong> value</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> or <strong>I</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>dentify Relationship (End) Source by</strong> and <strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">I</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">dentify Relationship (End) Target by</span></strong> values. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about mapping options in the Excel/CSV Import dialog>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11b36b6e-52a8-475f-92cc-71a4f243e323"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The relationship end is empty in the data file.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The source or the target elements <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7f13de85-ee44-446f-82a6-1e7e8d41f49b">do not exist</ac:inline-comment-marker> in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the following example, you can see specified import options in the <strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog before the import. After the import the Satisfy relationships are created between the defined Source and Target elements: Blocks and Requirements. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="result_of_relationships_import.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The import of Satisfy relationship.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e8be45c-fbdf-4bcb-b092-02d8fb73fcf8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73874634</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files">Importing data from Excel or CSV files</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Saving+an+Import+Map">Saving an Import Map</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Map+Groups">Managing Map Groups</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog">Excel and CSV Import dialog</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files">Sync with Excel or CSV files</a></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713070 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00672: Importing server project file

- page_id: `136713070`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713070/Importing+server+project+file
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-10-12T09:36:31.836+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Collaboration between disconnected teams > Using server project file format
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a project as a new server project, import the project*.szip*file.

To import a server project file

1. Select File > Import From > Server Project File.
2. Select the .szip file you want to use and click **Open**. This opens the **Import Project to the Server**dialog: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the Action column, you see the action that is performed with the project once you click Import . Possible actions include:
  - Add to Server – the project is added to the server.
    - If the action is Add to Server , you can modify the **Name on Server**column to choose a new name for the imported project.
    - If you want to skip certain projects, you can change the Add to Server action to Skip .
  - Skip – the server project is skipped.
  - Skip (Project Removed) – the project is removed. You must use Data Manager to either delete it completely from the server or restore the deleted version.
  - Skip (Project Exists) – the project already exists on the server and cannot be imported. If you still need to upload the .szip version of the project to Teamwork Cloud, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Updating a server project with local changes' space='MD2024x']**Update from Local Project**functionality.
4. To choose the category for the newly added project, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the dialog. This opens the **Select Category**dialog : 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Choose the category and click **OK**to return to the **Import Project to the Server**dialog.
6. Click **Import**.****The project will be added as a new server project.

**Please note:**

- If the project you are importing was saved together with used projects, all of the used projects will be visible in the import dialog. You can then decide if you want to include them in the import.
- If a main or used project you are importing already exists in the server, it will be skipped and not imported. You can see the results of the import action in the Notification Window: [ATTACHMENT filename='server_projects_notification.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To add a project as a new server project, import the project </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><em>.szip </em>file.</span></p><p>To import a server project file</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">File &gt; Import From &gt; Server Project File.</span></strong></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Select the .szip file you want to use and click <strong>Open</strong>. This opens the <strong>Import Project to the Server </strong>dialog:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_project_to_server.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you see the action that is performed with the project once you click <strong>Import</strong>. Possible actions include:<ul><li><strong>Add to Server</strong> – the project is added to the server.<ul><li>If the action is <strong>Add to Server</strong>, you can <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">modify the <strong>Name on Server </strong>column to choose a new name for the imported project.</span> </li><li>If you want to skip certain projects, you can change the <strong>Add to Server </strong>action to <strong>Skip</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Skip </strong>– the server project is skipped.</li><li><strong>Skip (Project Removed)</strong> – the project is removed. You must use Data Manager to either delete it completely from the server or restore the deleted version.</li><li><strong>Skip (Project Exists)</strong> – the project already exists on the server and cannot be imported. If you still need to upload the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.szip version of the project to Teamwork Cloud, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Updating a server project with local changes" /><ac:link-body><strong>Update from Local Project </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>functionality.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To choose the category for the newly added project, select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots_button.png" /></ac:image> at the bottom of the dialog. This opens the <strong>Select Category </strong>dialog</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="461"><ri:attachment ri:filename="server_project_category_selection.jpg" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Choose the category and click <strong>OK </strong>to return to the <strong>Import Project to the Server </strong>dialog.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Click <strong>Import</strong>.<strong> </strong>The project will be added as a new server project.<br /><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fdaf3a5-e1fc-4b1c-aac0-0eb146562935"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Please note:</strong></p><ul><li>If the project you are importing was saved together with used projects, all of the used projects will be visible in the import dialog. You can then decide if you want to include them in the import.</li><li>If a main or used project you are importing already exists in the server, it will be skipped and not imported.<br />You can see the results of the import action in the Notification Window:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="235"><ri:attachment ri:filename="server_projects_notification.png" /></ac:image> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=159940700 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00673: Importing the content of a used 3DEXPERIENCE project

- page_id: `159940700`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/159940700/Importing+the+content+of+a+used+3DEXPERIENCE+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-28T13:26:59.298+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.

Standard/system profiles, such as *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip*, cannot be imported.

To import the contents of a used project into your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE' space=''] .
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select the used project and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via the shortcut menu. Right-click the project and then select **Import**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Import dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old ones.
  1. Selecting**Import with New IDs** creates new IDs for the imported elements in the main project. This way, element IDs are not duplicated, ensuring the main project remains intact. However, if the elements of the used project are used in other projects, choosing **Import with****New IDs** may break those references. Importing elements with new IDs is highly recommended as it is the safest option regarding project consistency.
  2. Selecting **Import with Old IDs** imports the elements with their existing IDs, which can be useful if the elements are used in multiple other projects. However, this may cause duplicate element IDs, resulting in the main project being corrupted. To proceed with the import using the old IDs, click **Yes**in the **Question**dialog, acknowledging that the procedure might corrupt the data of the main project.
5. If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click **Yes** and wait while the import completes. This may take some time. NoteThe imported project no longer appears in the **Project Usages** dialog.
6. Click OK.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3d4aa3-1caa-4487-8226-47573b3c0e12"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Standard/system profiles, such as <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em>, cannot be imported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To import the contents of a used project into your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; Project Usages &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the used project and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac0e76a5-8ae3-4829-8bdb-a3a08b2944bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Shortcut menu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step can be done via the shortcut menu. Right-click the project and then select <strong>Import</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dexperience_project_usages.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li>In the <strong>Import</strong> dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old ones.<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Selecting<strong> Import with New IDs</strong> creates new IDs for the imported elements in the main project. This way, element IDs are not duplicated, ensuring the main project remains intact. However, if the elements of the used project are used in other projects, choosing <strong>Import with </strong><strong>New IDs</strong> may break those references.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f73d9e1f-6144-49a9-b69c-3c28a0861585"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Importing elements with new IDs is highly recommended as it is the safest option regarding project consistency.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Selecting <strong>Import with Old IDs</strong> imports the elements with their existing IDs, which can be useful if the elements are used in multiple other projects. However, this may cause duplicate element IDs, resulting in the main project being corrupted. To proceed with the import using the old IDs, click <strong>Yes </strong>in the <strong>Question </strong>dialog, acknowledging that the procedure might corrupt the data of the main project.</p></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click <strong>Yes</strong> and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17f6ab1e-9cfb-4f41-9fdf-79440af6a9a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The imported project no longer appears in the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK.</strong></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713297 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00674: Importing the content of a used project into your project

- page_id: `136713297`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713297/Importing+the+content+of+a+used+project+into+your+project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-27T08:57:01.843+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import the content of a used project into your project, you must have an **Administer Resources** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud185'].

If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.

Standard/system profiles, such as *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip*, cannot be imported.

To import the contents of a used project into your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space=''] .
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select the used project and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select **Import**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Import dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old.
5. If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click **Yes** and wait while the import completes. This may take some time. NoteThe imported project no longer appears in the **Project Usages** dialog.
6. Click OK .

The imported project also disappears from the package *Project Usages* and a new package named after the imported project appears under the root package in the Model Browser. If the imported project uses other projects, these projects will be directly used in your project.

After it is imported to the contents of your project, a used project will still exist in the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) repository.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="660e2072-9c67-4bfd-a314-4d8ce037351b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To import the content of a used project into your project, you must have an <strong>Administer Resources</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud185" ri:content-title="Permissions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[permission]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3d4aa3-1caa-4487-8226-47573b3c0e12"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Standard/system profiles, such as <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em>, cannot be imported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To import the contents of a used project into your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; Project Usages &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the used project and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac0e76a5-8ae3-4829-8bdb-a3a08b2944bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Shortcut menu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select <strong>Import</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_usages_import.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>In the <strong>Import</strong> dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click <strong>Yes</strong> and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17f6ab1e-9cfb-4f41-9fdf-79440af6a9a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The imported project no longer appears in the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The imported project also disappears from the package <em>Project Usages</em> and a new package named after the imported project appears under the root package in the Model Browser. If the imported project uses other projects, these projects will be directly used in your project.</p><p>After it is imported to the contents of your project, a used project will still exist in the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) repository.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709308 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00675: Improving performance

- page_id: `136709308`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709308/Improving+performance
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-26T15:35:41.395+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427324068

1427324070

1427324069

INLINE

When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space=''] (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space=''] to track memory usage.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space=''] and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.
- Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project partitioning' space=''].
- Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space=''] to free unused memory.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Background Task Manager' space=''] that run too long.
- Set the limit of open diagrams in the Environment Options dialog ( Options > Environment > General > General > Diagram Tabs Limit ).

1427324067

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw getting started' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing and Selecting Perspective' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Disabling inbound network activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC']

To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:</p><ul><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory allocation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase memory allocation value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[memory monitor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to track memory usage.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation period" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase an active validation period]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.</p></li><li><p>Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project partitioning" /></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Garbage Collector]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to free unused memory.</p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Background Task Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cancel the background tasks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that run too long.</li><li>Set the limit of open diagrams in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog (<strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagram Tabs Limit</strong>). </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bee057bf-13ac-4c87-a9c6-d0ef312493f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="MagicDraw getting started" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing and Selecting Perspective" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuration files" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Disabling inbound network activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711066 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00676: Include

- page_id: `136711066`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711066/Include
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:19.771+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Include relationship is used when there are common parts of the behavior among two or more Use Cases. Each common part is then extracted to a separate Use Case, to be included by all base Use Cases having this part in common. The Include relationship is represented by a base Use Case to an inclusion Use Case.

An Include relationship between Use Cases indicates that an instance of the including Use Case also contains the behavior as specified by the included Use Case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Include relationship

In the example above, the*Make Item Reservation*Use Case is a base Use Case. It includes the*Find Item*Use Case. The*Make Item Reservation*Use Case is the including Use Case and the*Find Item*Use Case is the included Use Case.

To use the Include relationship

1. Create two or more Use Cases by dragging and dropping the Use Case element [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.28.57 PM.png'] onto your diagram pane.
2. Click on the Include relationship [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.30.51 PM.png'] and connect it between two Use Cases.
3. The result should look like the aforementioned example.

You can format comment symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x'].

You can specify a comment in the Include Specification Window. In the same window, you can find the description of each Include property in the description area of the Specification Window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Include relationship is represented by a base Use Case to an inclusion Use Case.</p><p>An Include relationship between Use Cases indicates that an instance of the including Use Case also contains the behavior as specified by the included Use Case.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 11.35.50 AM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Include" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Example of Include relationship</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;">In the example above, the </span><em>Make Item Reservation</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is a base Use Case. In the same window, you can find the description of each Include property in the description area of the Specification Window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02202a08-01b8-4664-b9d1-3104c6e4ecc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about the Specification window usage, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff1aef2b-4429-4784-bb8f-aa49ccc1bf16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00677: Including external images

- page_id: `136714048`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714048/Including+external+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:03.748+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050285233

2050285245

2050285231

**On this page**

43

2050285234

You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are *BMP*, *JPG*, *WBMP*, *GIF*, and *PNG*.

###### **$image.include(location)**

Include an external image from a local file or *URL* in a report.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | location | java.lang.String | An image’s location. The location format can be either a URL or a local file. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image included in a report |

For example:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adcb2062-dfa2-4b21-9dcd-3e6517f349c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285233</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2aaa8a70-e325-4c59-8f8d-45f736106588"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285245</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="897d6481-21c5-4b5d-8590-c2a4a1b7ae7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285231</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd7876af-2c40-481d-9c44-715984bb4606"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285234</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are <em>BMP</em>, <em>JPG</em>, <em>WBMP</em>, <em>GIF</em>, and <em>PNG</em>.</p><h4><strong>$image.include(location)</strong></h4><p>Include an external image from a local file or <em>URL</em> in a report.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>location</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An image’s location. <br />The location format <br />can be either a URL <br />or a local file.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image included <br />in a report</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd86fd2e-acc9-4364-82e6-34794a984140"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$image.include("C:\Users\username\images\logo.gif")
$image.include("http://www.magicdraw.com/images/product_boxes/MD.gif")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711583 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00678: Incompatible properties and related elements

- page_id: `136711583`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711583/Incompatible+properties+and+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:46.116+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Refactoring > Converting Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.

Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.

For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.

To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.

##### Changes after conversion

###### Converting a property to a port

If the property visibility is *private*, *protected,* or *package* , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to *public*.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.</p><p>Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.</p><p>For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)" ac:title="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_elements_incompatible_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Incompatible properties and related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.</p><p>To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.</p><h3>Changes after conversion</h3><h4>Converting a property to a port</h4><p>If the property visibility is <em>private</em>, <em>protected,</em> or <em>package</em> , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to <em>public</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67b51616-1257-45d8-b179-df6555185bfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fc359cb-dee4-4291-9163-fcd677e10dd5" /> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709568 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00679: Indexing

- page_id: `136709568`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709568/Indexing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:56.851+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model elements representation text indexing allows users to find elements in the model more quickly. It starts automatically when a Teamwork Cloud project is opened for the first time; you can track the progress in the status bar (see the image below). The index is automatically updated when the model changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The indexing progress shown in the status bar.

Please note that indexing may take some time depending on the project size. The more elements the project contains, the longer it takes to index the model elements representation text.

If representation text building is in progress, searches performed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024x'] dialog may be slower. In this case, a warning appears next to the search progress bar (see an image below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a search does not return expected results, we recommend that you rebuild the model elements representation text index (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Indexing' space='MD2024x'] below).

The**Rebuild Text Index** action is available in the Expert mode only.

instructionsTo rebuild the text index

- In the main menu , click Tools > Rebuild Text Index . [ATTACHMENT filename='rebuild_text_index_dialog.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709609 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00680: Indexing of unloaded projects

- page_id: `136709609`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709609/Indexing+of+unloaded+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:59.642+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Decomposing model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Proxy

628699399

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Proxy

628699401

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Proxy

628699400

If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly.Indexing can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.

#### INFO: Proxy

Proxy

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']true

When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of necessary elements are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources. If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.

A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:

A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.

To specify elements for indexing

1. Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.
2. From the Options menu, select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
3. Select the Indexing property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_indexing.png']
4. Select the **Common Indexing** option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.
5. When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the **Custom Indexing** option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant. Click **OK**.
6. In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select **Options** > **Project Usages**) and select the **Use Index** check box.

628699398

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711070 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00681: Information Flow

- page_id: `136711070`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711070/Information+Flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:20.228+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Conveyed information

549967662

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Conveyed information

549967664

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Conveyed information

549967663

An Information Flow specifies that one or more information items circulate from its sources to its targets. Information flows require an “information channel” for transmitting information items from the source to the destination.

An information channel is represented in various ways, depending on the nature of its sources and targets. It can be represented by connectors, links, associations, or dependencies. For example, if the source and destination are parts in composite structure diagrams such as a collaboration, the information channel is likely to be represented by a connector between them. Or, if the source and target are objects (which are a kind of Instance Specification), they can be represented by a link that joins the two, and so on. The Information Flow and the information item notation are added. You can draw them using the Information Flows toolbar in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'].

##### Setting an Information Flow

Information Flows can be set on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024x']in the Class Diagram and on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024x']in the Composite Structure Diagrams.

To set an Information Flow

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x']) and an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024x'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024x']) relationship between them. Make sure the properties for displaying a Conveyed Information on relationships are selected.To display Conveyed Information:Right-click the Association (Connector) and choose the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']** command.In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property group toolbar' space='MD2024x'] to **Expert** or **All**.Set the **Show Conveyed Information A** and **Show Conveyed Information B** check boxes to *true*.
2. Specify a Conveyed Information in the following ways: 
- Drag the Class or Information Item on an Association (Connector) and select the **Set as Conveyed Information** command. 
- Select an Association (Connector) relationship and from the smart manipulator toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. In the **New Conveyed Information** dialog, select **Conveyed Information**, **Information Flow**, **Direction** and click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] If a new Information Flow is set on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024x'] that is represented in other diagrams, the new Conveyed Information is displayed only on the diagram on which you set it. You can reverse the direction of the [Information Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Information+Flow) on [Connectors](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Connector). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='MD2024x']>]]> An Information Flow is set.

#### TIP: Conveyed information

Conveyed information

One Information Flow may have several Conveyed Information items.

549967661

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Item' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<strong>All</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Conveyed Information A</strong> and <strong>Show Conveyed Information B</strong>  check boxes to ac:title="New Conveyed Information" ac:alt="New Conveyed Information"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_conveyed_information.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" is represented in other diagrams, the new Conveyed Information is displayed only on the diagram on which you set it. You can reverse the direction of the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Information+Flow">Information Flow</a> on <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Connector">Connectors</a>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Reversing Information Flow direction" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to reverse direction >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">An Information Flow is set.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb7a8d5-00d7-4e0c-8b73-96aed9eb4dd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Conveyed information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>One Information Flow may have several Conveyed Information items.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a3c009d-9ab9-4071-b41e-82a637b54df8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">549967661</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Reversing Information Flow direction" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Information Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
