## PAGE 00726: Java Enumeration

- page_id: `136715402`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715402/Java+Enumeration
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:54:36.565+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Java Code Engineering > Java Mapping to UML > Mapping to UML Rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

431929426

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

431929428

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

431929427

Java enumeration is mapped directly to the UML Class with the stereotype «JavaEnumeration». If no appropriate property is found in UML, enumeration modifiers are mapped into UML Class properties or to the Java language properties for the interface. Java enumeration literals are mapped to the UML Property with the stereotype «JavaEnumerationLiteral». All contained fields, operations, and inner classes are mapped to appropriate UML Properties, UML Operations, UML Classes. All mapping rules used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Java Class' space='MD2024x'] mapping apply to the Java enumeration.


## PAGE 00727: Java Enumeration Literal

- page_id: `136715404`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715404/Java+Enumeration+Literal
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:54:43.284+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Java Code Engineering > Java Mapping to UML > Mapping to UML Rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

431945117

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

431945119

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

431945118

Java Enumeration Literal is mapped directly to the UML Property with the stereotype «JavaEnumerationLiteral». It does not require specific modifiers.

| Java Element | UML Element |
| --- | --- |
| Literal declaration | UML Parameter with stereotype «JavaEnumerationLiteral», owned by UML Class with stereotype «JavaEnumeration» |
| Literal name | UML Property Name |
| Literal documentation | When using JavaDoc preprocessing, it is mapped to the UML Parameter Documentation; otherwise, it is part of the UML Operation Documentation. |

#### TIP: Example

Example

Java source code

```java

```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715397 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00728: Java Field

- page_id: `136715397`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715397/Java+Field
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:54:14.956+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Java Code Engineering > Java Mapping to UML > Mapping to UML Rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Examples

431872159

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Examples

431872162

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Examples

431872161

A Java field is mapped directly to the UML [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='MD2024x'] with the stereotype «JavaField». This stereotype is optional and if a UML Class has no stereotype, Java CE treats it as a Java field. If no appropriate property is found in UML, field modifiers are mapped into the UML Property properties or to the Java language properties.

Note that Java field type modifiers are mapped to the specific property *Type Modifier*, but not to the UML Multiplicity.

**Field mapping table**

| Java Element | MagicDraw-UML Element |
| --- | --- |
| Field Declaration | UML Property, owned by UML Class, with stereotype «JavaProperty» (optional) |
| Field Name | UML Property Name |
| Field Documentation | UML Field Documentation |
| Field Type | Mapped to the UML Type property. It is a reference to the UML Classifier; its package structure and name represent the referenced Java class. |
| Field Type Modifiers | Mapped to the specified property Type Modifier |
| Visibility Modifier | UML Property Visibility property |
| Final Modifier | UML Property Is Read Only property |
| Static Modifier | UML Property Is Static property |
| Transient Modifier | Java Language property Transient modifier |
| Volatile Modifier | Java Language property Volatile modifier |

#### TIP: Examples

Examples

Java source code

```java

```

UML model

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

431872158

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mapping to UML Rules' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3c304e6-4ddd-43da-bf6a-f8f64a11125e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">431872159</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b357fd4-5de3-46fb-981e-2270a82566ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">431872162</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="118509bb-a0a8-491f-8835-6c7a17bf32a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">431872161</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>A Java field is mapped directly to the UML <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Attribute" /></ac:link> with the stereotype «JavaField». This stereotype is optional and if a UML Class has no stereotype, Java CE treats it as a Java field. <span>If no appropriate property is found in UML, f</span>ield modifiers are mapped into the UML Property properties or to the Java language properties.</span></p><p><span>Note that Java field type modifiers are mapped to the specific property <em>Type Modifier</em>, but not to the UML Multiplicity. </span></p><p><strong>Field mapping table</strong></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Java Element</th><th>MagicDraw-UML Element</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Field Declaration</strong></td><td><p><span>UML Property, owned by UML Class, with stereotype «JavaProperty» (optional)</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Field Name</strong></td><td><p><span>UML Property Name<br /></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Field Documentation</strong></td><td><p><span>UML Field Documentation</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Field Type</strong></td><td><p><span>Mapped to the UML Type property.<span><span> It is a reference to the UML Classifier; its package structure and name represent the referenced Java class.</span></span><br /></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Field Type Modifiers</strong></td><td><p><span>Mapped to the specified property <em>Type Modifier</em></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Visibility Modifier</strong></td><td><p><span>UML Property <em>Visibility</em> property</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Final Modifier</strong></td><td><p><span>UML Property <em>Is Read Only</em> property</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Static Modifier</strong></td><td><p><span>UML Property <em>Is Static</em> property<br /></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Transient Modifier</strong></td><td><p><span>Java Language property <em>Transient modifier</em></span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Volatile Modifier</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Java Language property <em>Volatile modifier</em></span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b4719bb-b5a6-4cf6-b295-ec204ccecd45"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Examples</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Java source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6231941-323c-4032-b999-ec187bf31e34"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[public final class MyClass
{
/**
* myList comment
*/
public static java.util.List myList;
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_with_property_ce_expample.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Java Field" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;"><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71476269-a6e4-4d70-96e8-4bc27fb8b533"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">431872158</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e7f9a940-0b39-4669-9c98-23240e70e7b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Mapping to UML Rules" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00729: Java Imports

- page_id: `136715413`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715413/Java+Imports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:55:18.084+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Java Code Engineering > Java Mapping to UML > Mapping to UML Rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

432040070

432040072

432040071

| Java Element | MagicDraw-UML Element |
| --- | --- |
| Type Import | Mapped to the UML Element Import with the optional stereotype «JavaImport». The supplier is an imported UML Classifier, representing the imported Java type, and the client is a UML Classifier requiring an imported element. |
| Package Import | Mapped to the UML Package Import with the optional stereotype «JavaImport». The supplier is an imported UML Package, representing the imported Java package, and the client is a UML Classifier requiring imported elements. |
| Static import for all static members | Mapped to the UML Element Import with the stereotype «JavaStaticImport». The {JavaImportAll} tag of «JavaStat- icImport» must have a “true“ value. The supplier is an imported UML Classifier owner of imported static members, and the client is a UML Classifier requiring imported elements. |
| Static import for single static members | Mapped to the UML Element Import with the stereotype «JavaStaticImport». The {JavaImportAll} tag of «JavaStaticImport» must have a “false“ value and its tag {JavaImportedMember} must have reference to the imported member. The supplier is a UML Classifier owner of an imported static member, and the client is a UML Classifier requiring imported elements. If several static members are imported, {JavaImportedMember} can list all of them. |

432040069

The supplier is an imported UML Classifier, representing the imported Java type, and the client is a UML Classifier requiring an imported element.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Package Import</td><td><p><span>Mapped to the UML Package Import with the optional stereotype «JavaImport». The supplier is an imported UML Package, representing the imported Java package, and the client is a UML Classifier requiring imported elements. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Static import for all static members</td><td><p><span>Mapped to the UML Element Import with the stereotype «JavaStaticImport». The {JavaImportAll} tag of «JavaStat- icImport» must have a “true“ value. The supplier is an imported UML Classifier owner of imported static members, and the client is a UML Classifier requiring imported elements.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Static import for single static members</td><td><p><span>Mapped to the UML Element Import with the stereotype «JavaStaticImport». The {JavaImportAll} tag of «JavaStaticImport» must have a “false“ value and its tag {JavaImportedMember} must have reference to the imported member. The supplier is a UML Classifier owner of an imported static member, and the client is a UML Classifier requiring imported elements. </span>If several static members are imported, {JavaImportedMember} can list all of them.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e226d806-5a67-47c2-a536-3f95969927e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">432040069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e7f9a940-0b39-4669-9c98-23240e70e7b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Mapping to UML Rules" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00730: Java Interface

- page_id: `136715401`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715401/Java+Interface
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:54:29.298+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Java Code Engineering > Java Mapping to UML > Mapping to UML Rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

431916479

431916481

431916480

The Java interface is mapped directly to the UML Interface with the stereotype «JavaInterface». This stereotype is optional, and if the UML Interface has no stereotype, Java CE treats it as a Java interface. If no appropriate property is found in the UMLinterface, modifiers are mapped into UML Interface properties or to the Java language properties for the interface. All mapping rules used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Java Class' space='MD2024x'] mapping apply to the Java interface.

431916478

This stereotype is optional, and if the UML Interface has no stereotype, Java CE treats it as a Java interface. If no appropriate property is found in the UMLinterface, modifiers are mapped into UML Interface properties or to the Java language properties for the interface. All mapping rules used in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Java Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Java class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> mapping apply to the Java interface.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2909ff05-bcc2-4cb3-82ff-a953e25d0029"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">431916478</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e7f9a940-0b39-4669-9c98-23240e70e7b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Mapping to UML Rules" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715417 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00731: Java Language Options

- page_id: `136715417`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715417/Java+Language+Options
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:52:48.070+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Java Code Engineering > Java CE Options
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

432105795

432105798

432105796

You can find these options at the **Options** > **Project** > **Code Engineering** > **Java Language Options**.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Java language options

##### Generate Opening Bracket In New Line

By default is turned on. If element (Java type or operation) is generated for the first time into source, curly bracket is generated from the new line, if options is on, or in the same line as declaration header ends, if option is off.

##### Generate Spaces

By default it is turned on. If option is on - adds additional space after open bracket and before close bracket in parameter declaration list.

##### Generate Empty Documentation

By default is turned off. If option is turned on, MagicDraw is generating documentation to the source even, if there are no documentation in model. Just adds Java documentation start and ending symbols.

Here is generated class header with options turned on. UML Class A doesn’t have documentation, but still in code is added documentation elements.

```text
/* *
```

```text
*
```

```text
*/
```

```text
class A
```

##### Automatic “import“ Generation

By default is turned on. If option is turned on, all required imports are added automatically by used references on code generation. If this option is off, than imports are managed by user using specific mapping, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Java Imports' space='MD2024x'].

Note, that if option is turned off, than no imports are created automatically and all imports, retrieved from source code on reverse engineering, are mapped into UML relationships as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Java Imports' space='MD2024x'].

##### Class Count To Generate Import On Demand

By default value is 10. It means, that if there will be 9 references from the same package to different types, then imports will be generated explicitly to these classes, but if there will be 10 and more references, than it will be generated one import to the package.

This option is valid only, when “Automatic import generation“ is turned on.

##### Documentation

Java documentation has two options by itself. It has **Processor** and **Style**. Style is used to define how to format documentation by adding some comments.

There are predefined two styles:

- Style 1

```text
/* *
```

```text
*
```

```text
*/
```

- Style 2

```text
/* *
```

```text
*
```

```text
*/
```

Processor is responsible for analyzing documentation context. There are two types <none> and Java Doc. <none> options does nothing with documentation and just set it as is on element.

Java Doc is processing documentation by resolving parameter tags or on code generation building documentation by collecting comments from the UML Parameters and adding missed tags for thrown exceptions or return.

There are additional Java Doc options (button “...”). These options can be used to declare what tags would not be generated or what order to use on code generation or perhaps to add some additional tag to documentation for all elements.

Note, that Java Doc processor splits operation documentation for UML Parameter and UML Operation on source code reverse and on code generation UML Parameter documentation is used to build Java documentation for operation.

##### Classpath

You can define classpath here by referencing **jar** files or **class** files directories.

This options is used by Java reverse engineering. If referenced element is not found in model for some reasons, that it is searched in this defined path. And if class is matched by name, this class is added into model.

By default, MagicDraw imports boot classpath of the JVM, on which is running.

##### Java Source

By default is Java 5.0. There are options **1.4** and **5.0**. If you are reversing older specification source code, where, for example “**enum**“ is not a keyword and can be a variable name, then you will need to choose **1.4** Java source, else MagicDraw parser can emit error.

##### Header

It is a header for a newly generated Java files. There can be added some template string which will be preprocessed on writing to source code.

| Template Strings |  |
| --- | --- |
| $FILE_NAME | File name, without a path |
| $DATE | System date |
| $TIME | System time |
| $AUTHOR | User name on the system |

432105794

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Java CE Options' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='']

There can be added some template string which will be preprocessed on writing to source code.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 40.853657%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 51.870323%;" /><col style="width: 48.129677%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">Template Strings</th></tr><tr><td>$FILE_NAME</td><td>File name, without a path</td></tr><tr><td>$DATE</td><td>System date</td></tr><tr><td>$TIME</td><td>System time</td></tr><tr><td>$AUTHOR</td><td>User name on the system</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0ce1fb1-c5d6-488a-b5cb-1c5a28e7a211"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">432105794</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3244708c-6c33-45fd-a706-f04e0949900a"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Java CE Options" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8fe94dc1-a369-4372-8cd7-b985c8111ac4"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
